scheme_name: Financial Assistance To Disabled Students Pursuing (10th, 11th, 12th Equivalent Exams)
implementing_agency: State Literacy Mission, Kerala
tags: APL, BPL, Disabled, Financial Assistance, PwD, Student
brief_description: The scheme Financial Assistance to Disabled Students Pursuing (10th, 11th, 12th Equivalent Exams) was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala, for differently-abled students pursuing equivalent secondary and higher secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Kerala with 40% or more disability. Applicable to both APL and BPL category students.
documents_required: 1. Identity proof 2. Passport-size photo 3. Educational Certificates 4. Below Poverty Line (BPL) Card (If applicable) 5. Disability Certificate 6. Income Certificate / Copy of Ration card (If applicable) 7. Bank account details
application_process: Collect application form from Social Justice Office or official website, fill the form and submit with required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance for differently-abled students pursuing 10th, 11th and 12th equivalent courses.

scheme_name: Food Bill Assistance to SC Students
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Gujarat
tags: Financial Assistance, Food Bill, Hostel, SC, Student
brief_description: The food bill Assistance at the rate of Rs.1,500 per month (Up to 10 Months) will be granted to the Scheduled Caste (SC) students admitted in college-affiliated hostel.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a Scheduled Caste student of Gujarat residing in a college-affiliated hostel. Annual family income should not exceed Rs.6 lakh.
documents_required: 1. Passport size photo 2. Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card 3. Caste Certificate  4. Income Certificate 5. Educational Marksheet 6. Break Affidavit (If Break Gap is more than One Year) 7. School Leaving Certificate (If Any) 8. Bank Account Details 9. Hostel Certificate.
application_process: Apply online through the Digital Gujarat Portal by registering an account, completing the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: - The food bill Assistance of Rs.1,500 per month for up to 10 months towards hostel food expenses.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to SC Students Studying in Medical, Engineering and Diploma Courses for Purchasing Educational Instruments
implementing_agency: Director, Scheduled Caste Welfare, Government of Gujarat
tags: SC, Student, Medical, Engineering, Diploma, Educational Instruments, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste students in Gujarat pursuing Medical, Engineering, or Diploma courses for purchasing educational instruments and study equipment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a Scheduled Caste student residing in Gujarat and enrolled in a Medical, Engineering, or Diploma course. Annual family income should not exceed Rs.6 lakh.
documents_required: 1. Passport-size photograph, 2. Aadhaar Card, 3. Caste Certificate, 4. Income Certificate, 5. 10th and 12th Mark Sheets, 6. Institution Identity Card, 7. Bank Account Details, 8. School Leaving Certificate (if applicable), and other supporting documents as required.
application_process: Apply online through the Digital Gujarat Portal (https://www.digitalgujarat.gov.in/) by registering an account, completing the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.15,000 for Medical students, Rs.10,000 for Engineering students, and Rs.5,000 for Diploma students to purchase educational instruments and study equipment.

scheme_name: Scheme of Giving Awards to Eminent Artists - Kalai Valarmani Award
implementing_agency: Art and Culture Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Artists, Award, Culture, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The Kalai Valarmani Award scheme aims to promote and preserve the cultural heritage of Tamil Nadu by recognizing and supporting talented young artists.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an artist aged between 19 and 35 years.
documents_required: Photographs, Bio-data, Degree Certificate, Family Card or Voter ID Card, Age Proof, Proof of Artistic Ability, and other supporting documents as required.
application_process: Register as an artist on the Art and Culture Department portal, complete the application form, upload the required documents, and submit the application to the concerned Regional Cultural Centre.
benefits: Selected artists receive financial assistance of Rs.6,000, a certificate of appreciation, and a shawl worth Rs.500.

implementing_agency: Department of Women Empowerment and Child Development, Government of Uttarakhand
tags: Health, Nutrition, Pregnant Women, Lactating Women
brief_description: Mukhyamantri Mahila Poshan Yojana aims to reduce malnutrition among pregnant and lactating women in Uttarakhand by providing nutritious food supplements through Anganwadi Centres.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand and be a pregnant or lactating woman.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Proof of Pregnancy (Medical Report), and other supporting documents as required.
application_process: Visit the nearest Anganwadi Centre and submit the required documents to avail the benefits under the scheme.
benefits: Nutritious food supplements, including eggs twice a week. Dates are provided to registered beneficiaries who do not consume eggs.

implementing_agency: Department of Women Empowerment and Child Development, Government of Uttarakhand
tags: Women, Mother, Girl Child, Health, Nutrition
brief_description: Mukhyamantri Mahalaxmi Kit Yojana provides nutritional and essential care kits to postpartum mothers and their newborn daughters to support their health and well-being.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand, be a postpartum woman registered at an Anganwadi Centre, and not be an income tax payer. The benefit is available for the first two girl children of the beneficiary.
documents_required: Medical proof of postpartum status, Identity Proof, Birth Certificate of the child, and other supporting documents as required.
application_process: Visit the nearest Anganwadi Centre and submit the required documents to receive the benefits under the scheme.
benefits: Mothers receive a kit containing nutritional and personal care items, while eligible girl children receive a kit with essential baby care items. Benefits are available for the first two girl children, including twins.

scheme_name: Scheme of Extending Financial Assistance to New Drama Productions
implementing_agency: Art and Culture Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Artist, Drama, Financial Assistance, Production, Troupes
brief_description: The scheme encourages the production and staging of new Tamil dance dramas based on Tamil art, literature, culture, and history by providing financial assistance to eligible drama groups.
eligibility_criteria: The drama should promote Tamil art, culture, history, or literature, should not have been staged previously, and should not contain content promoting caste, religious, or political animosity. The script must be approved by the concerned authority.
documents_required: Three copies of the drama script and a No-Objection Certificate from the District Collector or Police Department for the dialogues and scenes included in the drama.
application_process: Interested applicants should obtain and submit the prescribed application form along with the required documents to the Tamil Nadu Eyal Isai Nataka Mandram.
benefits: Financial assistance of up to Rs.20,000 for the production and staging of eligible Tamil dramas. Additional support may be provided for auditorium, sound, lighting, and promotional expenses.

implementing_agency: Department of Women Empowerment and Child Development, Government of Uttarakhand
tags: Girls, Women, Health, Sanitary Napkins, Menstrual Hygiene
brief_description: The Sanitary Napkin Scheme aims to promote menstrual hygiene by providing affordable sanitary napkins to adolescent girls and women through Anganwadi Centres in Uttarakhand.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand and be an adolescent girl or woman.
documents_required: No documents are required.
application_process: Visit the nearest Anganwadi Centre to purchase sanitary napkins at the subsidized rate.
benefits: Sanitary napkins are provided to adolescent girls and women at a subsidized cost of Rs.6 per pack.

implementing_agency: Agriculture Department, Government of Rajasthan
tags: Agriculture, Farmer, Onion Cultivation, Storage Infrastructure, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to onion farmers in Rajasthan for constructing low-cost onion storage structures to reduce post-harvest losses and improve storage capacity.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident farmer of Rajasthan, cultivate onions, and possess at least 0.5 hectares of agricultural land for constructing the storage structure.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or Jan Aadhaar Card and a copy of Jamabandi issued within the last six months.
application_process: Apply online through the Raj Kisan Portal by registering, selecting the scheme, completing the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Subsidy of up to 50% of the unit cost for constructing a 25 metric ton onion storage structure, subject to a maximum grant as prescribed by the government.

implementing_agency: Agriculture Department, Government of Rajasthan
tags: Agriculture, Drip Irrigation, Farmer, Water Conservation
brief_description: The Drip Sanyantr scheme provides subsidies to farmers for installing drip irrigation systems, promoting efficient water use, improved crop productivity, and water conservation.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident farmer of Rajasthan and possess at least 0.2 hectares of irrigated cultivable land.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or Jan Aadhaar Card, Jamabandi copy issued within the last six months, Irrigation Source Certificate, Soil and Water Testing Report, and Supplier Quotation.
application_process: Apply online through the Raj Kisan Portal by registering, selecting the scheme, completing the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: General category farmers receive a subsidy of up to 70%, while small, marginal, Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, and women farmers receive a subsidy of up to 75% for drip irrigation systems on a maximum area of 5 hectares.

scheme_name: Special Scholarship to SC Students in Selected Schools
implementing_agency: Director, Scheduled Caste Welfare, Government of Gujarat
tags: Scheduled Caste, Scholarship, Students, Financial Assistance, Education
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste students studying in selected reputed schools to support their educational expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category of Gujarat and be studying in a designated school under the scheme. Annual family income should not exceed Rs.6 lakh. Assistance is available for eligible students admitted to specified classes in selected schools.
documents_required: Passport-size photograph, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Educational Certificates or Mark Sheets, School Identity Card, Bank Account Details, and other supporting documents as required.
application_process: Eligible students should apply through the concerned District Deputy Director's Office or the Director, Scheduled Caste Welfare Office, Gandhinagar, along with the required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance of up to Rs.50,000 per year or the actual educational expenses incurred, whichever is lower. The assistance covers admission fees, tuition fees, hostel fees, food expenses, books, uniforms, and other related educational costs.

scheme_name: Dr. P. G. Solanki Scheme for Loan/Assistance to Medical Graduate Doctors of Scheduled Caste
implementing_agency: Director, Scheduled Caste Welfare, Government of Gujarat
tags: Scheduled Caste, Doctors, Medical Graduates, Loan, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance and loans to Scheduled Caste medical graduates in Gujarat to establish independent medical practice or clinics.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a Scheduled Caste medical graduate residing in Gujarat with a recognized qualification such as MBBS, BAMS, BHMS, BDS, or equivalent. Annual family income should not exceed Rs.6 lakh. The applicant should not be employed in a government hospital or private organization.
documents_required: Passport-size photograph, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Residence Proof, Medical Degree Certificate, Registration Certificate, Bank Account Details, Age Proof, Guarantor Documents, and other supporting documents as required.
application_process: Apply online through the e-Samaj Kalyan Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.25,000 along with a loan of up to Rs.2.5 lakh to help eligible Scheduled Caste medical graduates establish their independent practice.

scheme_name: Loan Assistance to Scheduled Caste Students for Commercial Pilot Training
implementing_agency: Director, Scheduled Caste Welfare, Government of Gujarat
tags: Scheduled Caste, Students, Commercial Pilot Training, Loan Assistance, Aviation
brief_description: The scheme provides loan assistance to Scheduled Caste students in Gujarat for pursuing Commercial Pilot License (CPL) training and related aviation education.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Gujarat and belong to the Scheduled Caste category. The applicant must meet the educational and technical requirements for admission to a recognized Commercial Pilot License training institute.
documents_required: Passport-size photograph, Aadhaar Card, Residence Proof, Caste Certificate, Educational Certificates, Income Certificate, Bank Account Details, School or College Leaving Certificate, Surety Documents, Passport and Visa (for overseas training), and other supporting documents as required.
application_process: Apply online through the e-Samaj Kalyan Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Loan assistance of up to Rs.25 lakh at a concessional interest rate of 4% for Commercial Pilot License training.

scheme_name: Marriage Grant for One Daughter (Sainik Welfare)
implementing_agency: Rajya Sainik Board, Puducherry
tags: Ex-Servicemen, Widow, Daughter, Marriage Assistance, Financial Grant
brief_description: The scheme provides a one-time financial grant to Ex-Servicemen and widows of Ex-Servicemen in Puducherry for the marriage of one daughter.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Ex-Serviceman or widow of an Ex-Serviceman registered with the Department of Sainik Welfare, Puducherry. The daughter should be at least 18 years old. The benefit can be availed only once.
documents_required: ESM/Widow Identity Card, Marriage Certificate, Marriage Invitation Card, Purchase Bills for marriage-related expenses, Affidavit (if applicable), and Declaration.
application_process: Download or collect the application form from the Department of Sainik Welfare, fill it with the required details, attach supporting documents, and submit it to the Rajya Sainik Board within 3 months of the marriage.
benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs.25,000 for the marriage of one daughter.

implementing_agency: Rajya Sainik Board, Puducherry
target_beneficiaries: Non-Pensioner Ex-Servicemen and Widows of Ex-Servicemen
tags: Ex-Servicemen, Widow, Festival Assistance, Gift Coupons
brief_description: The scheme provides annual festival gift coupons to eligible non-pensioner Ex-Servicemen and widows of Ex-Servicemen in Puducherry.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered Ex-Serviceman or widow of an Ex-Serviceman in Puducherry. Ex-Servicemen should be non-pensioners and at least 60 years old. No age limit applies to widows.
documents_required: ESM/Widow Identity Card, Registration Proof with Department of Sainik Welfare, Age Proof, and other documents as required.
application_process: Download or collect the application form from the Department of Sainik Welfare, complete the form, attach required documents, and submit it to the Rajya Sainik Board.
benefits: Festival gift coupons worth Rs.4,000 per year.

implementing_agency: Rajya Sainik Board, Puducherry
tags: Ex-Servicemen, Widow, Spectacles, Eye Care, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Ex-Servicemen and widows of Ex-Servicemen in Puducherry for the purchase of spectacles.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered Ex-Serviceman or widow of an Ex-Serviceman in Puducherry and should not be a member of the Ex-Servicemen Contributory Health Scheme (ECHS).
documents_required: ESM/Widow Identity Card, Discharge Certificate, Doctor's Prescription, Original Purchase Bill, and Declaration.
application_process: Download or collect the application form from the Department of Sainik Welfare, attach the required documents, and submit it to the Rajya Sainik Board within 3 months of purchasing the spectacles.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.2,000 for the purchase of spectacles.

scheme_name: Cash Grant in lieu of Sewing Machine
implementing_agency: Rajya Sainik Board, Puducherry
tags: Ex-Serviceman, Widow, Sewing Machine, Grant, Financial Assistance
brief_description: Provides a one-time cash grant to widows of ex-servicemen for purchasing a sewing machine.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the widow of a registered Ex-Serviceman and registered with the Department of Sainik Welfare, Puducherry.
documents_required: ESM/Widow Identity Card.
application_process: Download or collect the application form from the Department of Sainik Welfare, complete the form, attach required documents, and submit it to the Rajya Sainik Board.
benefits: Cash grant of Rs.10,000 for purchase of a sewing machine.

implementing_agency: Department of Women Empowerment and Child Development, Uttarakhand
tags: Anganwadi Worker, Welfare Fund, Retirement Benefit, Women
category: Social Welfare & Empowerment, Banking, Financial Services and Insurance
brief_description: Provides retirement financial assistance to Anganwadi workers and helpers upon attaining 60 years of age.
eligibility_criteria: Permanent resident of Uttarakhand, Anganwadi Worker or Helper, and aged 60 years or above.
documents_required: Proof of retirement, Aadhaar Card, Bank account details, Other documents as required.
application_process: Download the application form from the official website and submit the completed form along with required documents to the CDPO office.
benefits: Financial assistance ranging from Rs.30,000 to Rs.1,30,000 based on years of service.

scheme_name: SAHAYTA (Sympathetic Assistance and Handholding for Youth Transformation and Amelioration)
implementing_agency: Mission Youth, Jammu and Kashmir
tags: Youth, Self Employment, Financial Assistance, Rehabilitation
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to youth affected by insurgency-related incidents for establishing self-employment ventures.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be 18-40 years old and have lost a family member or suffered permanent incapacitation due to an insurgency-related incident, cross-border shelling, or mine blast in Jammu and Kashmir. Applicant and family members must not be involved in anti-national activities.
documents_required: Certificate of non-involvement in militancy or anti-national activities, Death and Post-Mortem Certificate (if applicable), Medical Certificate (for permanent incapacitation), Relationship proof with victim, Date of Birth Certificate, Unemployment Certificate, Project Report, NOC/Undertaking from family members.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the concerned Deputy Commissioner within two years of the incident.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.2,00,000 or actual project cost, whichever is lower, for eligible self-employment ventures.

implementing_agency: National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC)
tags: Women Entrepreneur, SHG, Microfinance, Loan, Financial Inclusion
category: Business & Entrepreneurship, Banking, Financial Services and Insurance, Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women and Child
brief_description: Microfinance scheme aimed at empowering women from socially and economically disadvantaged backgrounds through concessional loans.
eligibility_criteria: Women aged 18-55 years belonging to economically and socially backward classes as notified by the Central or State Government.
documents_required: Age Proof, Identity Proof, SHG Membership ID, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Income Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Bank Account Details, Passport Size Photographs.
application_process: Submit the application through NSFDC channel partners, State Channelizing Agencies, Regional Rural Banks, Public Sector Banks, or other authorized institutions along with required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.1,40,000 with repayment period up to 3.5 years.

scheme_name: Fodder Minikits under Integrated Fodder Development Scheme
implementing_agency: Directorate of Animal Husbandry, Government of Gujarat
tags: Farmer, Fodder Seed, Agriculture, Livestock
brief_description: Provides improved fodder seed minikits to farmers for demonstration and adoption of improved fodder cultivation practices.
eligibility_criteria: Permanent resident of Gujarat, belonging to the General Category, and possessing at least 10 guntha of land in their name.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Residence Proof, Proof of ownership of 10 guntha of land, Other documents as required.
application_process: Contact the nearest Veterinary Dispensary or First Aid Veterinary Centre and submit the required documents to avail the benefits.
benefits: Fodder seed minikits of 3 kg, 7 kg, or 10 kg provided to eligible beneficiaries.

scheme_name: Dr. Savitaben Ambedkar Inter Caste Marriage Assistance Scheme
implementing_agency: District Deputy Director, Scheduled Caste Welfare Office (Urban Areas) / District Social Welfare Officer Office (Rural Areas)
target_beneficiaries: Inter-caste Married Couples (One Spouse Belonging to Scheduled Caste)
tags: Inter-Caste Marriage, Scheduled Caste, Marriage Assistance, Financial Assistance, Social Empowerment
brief_description: The scheme promotes social empowerment and reduction of caste-based discrimination by providing financial assistance to inter-caste married couples where one spouse belongs to the Scheduled Caste category.
eligibility_criteria: One spouse in the inter-caste marriage should belong to the Scheduled Caste category. One spouse should be a native of Gujarat. Marriage must be legally registered and the application submitted within two years of marriage. Parents of the couple should have resided in Gujarat for at least five years. Widows or widowers remarrying without dependent children are eligible. No income limit applies.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Marriage Registration Certificate, Residence Proof, School Leaving Certificate, Bank Passbook/Cancelled Cheque, Divorce or Death Certificate (if applicable), Declaration Form, and other documents as required.
application_process: Apply online through the e-Samaj Kalyan Portal (https://esamajkalyan.gujarat.gov.in). Register as a new user, update profile details, select the scheme, complete the application form, upload required documents, submit the application, and save the application number for tracking future status.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.2,50,000 to eligible inter-caste married couples. Rs.1,00,000 is provided for household expenses and Rs.1,50,000 is provided through National Savings Certificates in the joint name of the married couple.

scheme_name: Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam Shramik Pravidhik Shiksha Sahyata Yojana
implementing_agency: Uttar Pradesh Labour Welfare Board
tags: Education, Technical Education, Financial Assistance, Scholarship, Student Finance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to children of registered workers in Uttar Pradesh for pursuing technical education and skill-based professional courses.
eligibility_criteria: The worker should be employed in an establishment registered under the Shops and Commercial Establishments Act, 1962 or the Factories Act, 1948 in Uttar Pradesh. The worker's monthly salary (basic pay plus dearness allowance) should not exceed Rs.15,000. Eligible courses include Degree Courses (B.Tech, M.Tech, BCA, MCA, BBA, MBA, MS and other technical degree courses), Diploma Courses (Polytechnic, PG Diploma and other diploma courses), and Certificate Courses.
documents_required: Verified copy of the online application form, Bank Passbook copy of the worker or student showing IFSC code, Admission Proof or Bonafide Certificate from the technical education institution, and other documents as required.
application_process: Apply online through the Uttar Pradesh Labour Welfare Board portal (https://skpuplabour.in). Register as a new user, log in, select the scheme, fill out the application form, upload required documents, print the application, get it verified by the educational institution and employer, upload the verified application and supporting documents, and submit. Applicants can track the application status online through the portal.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.25,000 for Degree Courses, Rs.15,000 for Diploma Courses, and financial assistance for eligible Certificate Courses as prescribed by the scheme.

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Vridhavastha Pension Yojana
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Rajasthan
tags: Old Age Pension, Senior Citizens, Financial Assistance, BPL, Social Security
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly pension assistance to senior citizens belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families to support their financial needs and ensure a dignified life in old age.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Rajasthan. Applicant should be 60 years of age or above. Applicant should belong to a family living below the poverty line (BPL) as per Government of India criteria.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Jan Aadhaar/Bhamashah Card, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Passbook copy showing Account Number and IFSC Code.
application_process: Apply online through the Rajasthan Single Sign-On (SSO) portal. Register using Jan Aadhaar or Google account, log in to the SSO portal, access IFMS-RAJSSP, select 'Apply for Scheme', choose Indira Gandhi National Vridhavastha Pension Yojana, fill in the required details, and submit the application.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.1,000.

scheme_name: Chetan Chauhan Labor Sports Promotion Scheme
implementing_agency: Uttar Pradesh Labour Welfare Board
tags: Sports, Financial Assistance, Construction Workers, Athletes, Labour Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to children of registered workers and eligible workers who achieve recognition in sports competitions at district, state, national, or international levels.
eligibility_criteria: Worker should be employed in an establishment registered under the Factories Act, 1948. Monthly salary (basic salary plus dearness allowance) should not exceed Rs.15,000. Worker should have completed at least 6 months of continuous service and be in service at the time of application. Benefit is available to the worker or up to two children. Only players selected on or after 23 March 2021 are eligible. Female workers who are players themselves are also eligible.
documents_required: Attested copy of online application form, self-certified bank passbook copy with IFSC code, proof of relationship of dependent (ration card/family register or similar government record), Aadhaar cards of beneficiary and parents, and certificate from a recognized sports association confirming selection at district, state, national, or international level.
application_process: Visit the Uttar Pradesh Labour Welfare Board portal, register as a new user, log in, select the scheme, complete and submit the application form, obtain a printed copy, get it verified by the concerned establishment, upload the verified application and supporting documents, and track the application status online. Upon successful verification, the benefit amount is transferred to the beneficiary's bank account.
benefits: One-time financial assistance based on sports achievement level: Rs.25,000 for district level, Rs.50,000 for state level, Rs.75,000 for national level, and Rs.1,00,000 for international level.

scheme_name: Indira Mahila Shakti Prashikshan Evam Koshal Sanvardhan Yojana (RS-CSEP)
implementing_agency: Women and Child Development Department, Government of Rajasthan in collaboration with Rajasthan Knowledge Corporation Limited (RKCL)
tags: Women Empowerment, Skill Development, Spoken English, Personality Development, Training
brief_description: The scheme provides free spoken English and personality development training to women and girls in Rajasthan to improve employability, confidence, and life skills.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Rajasthan, aged between 16 and 45 years, and must have passed Class 12. Preference is given to widowed, divorced, abandoned, and violence-affected women.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Identity Proof, Income Certificate, Age Proof, Address Proof, Class 10 Marksheet, Class 12 Marksheet, FIR or Domestic Violence Report (if applicable), Caste Certificate (for SC/ST applicants), and supporting documents for widow/divorce/desertion status where applicable.
application_process: Visit the RKCL Women and Child Development portal, register using a mobile number and OTP, complete the online application form, upload required documents, and submit the application. Selected candidates receive an application ID and training center details through SMS or email. Training is conducted at RKCL-authorized IT Knowledge Centers with biometric attendance. Candidates with at least 65% attendance can appear for the examination and receive a certificate upon successful completion.
benefits: Free spoken English and personality development training conducted by Rajasthan Knowledge Corporation Limited (RKCL). The course duration is 130 hours, delivered over approximately 2 hours per day, 5 days per week.

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Vidhva Pension Yojana
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Rajasthan
target_beneficiaries: Widows from Below Poverty Line (BPL) Families
tags: Widow Pension, Women, Social Security, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly pension support to widowed women from Below Poverty Line (BPL) families in Rajasthan to ensure financial security and social welfare.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a domicile of Rajasthan, belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family, be a widow, and be 40 years of age or older.
documents_required: Passport-size photograph, Husband's Death Certificate, BPL Card, Age Proof Certificate, and other documents as required.
application_process: Register on the Rajasthan SSO portal using Jan Aadhaar or another eligible login method. After registration, log in to the portal, access the IFMS-RAJP platform, select the pension scheme application option, fill in the required details, upload necessary documents, and submit the application online.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.1,000 for widows aged 40 years and above but below 75 years. Higher pension benefits may be available for beneficiaries aged 75 years and above as per prevailing government norms.

scheme_name: Assistance for Tools, Equipment, Sorting/Grading Equipment, PHM Equipment for Scheduled Castes (HRT-4)
implementing_agency: Directorate of Horticulture, Government of Gujarat
target_beneficiaries: Scheduled Caste Farmers, Farmer Groups, Cooperative Societies, Self-Help Groups (SHGs)
tags: Scheduled Caste, Horticulture, Tools, Equipment, Post-Harvest Management, Farmer Groups, Cooperative Society, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste farmers, farmer groups, cooperative societies, and SHGs for purchasing horticulture tools, equipment, sorting/grading equipment, and post-harvest management (PHM) equipment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Gujarat and belong to the Scheduled Caste category. Individual farmers, farmer groups, cooperative societies, and eligible SHGs can apply. Equipment must be purchased from authorized dealers or empanelled manufacturers as per government guidelines.
documents_required: Scheduled Caste Certificate, Disability Certificate (if applicable), Land Records (7/12 and 8-A extracts), Aadhaar Card, Bank Passbook or Cancelled Cheque, Forest Rights Letter (if applicable), and any other documents as required.
application_process: Apply online through the iKhedut Portal. Select Horticulture Schemes, choose the HRT-4 scheme, fill out and submit the application, confirm the application, and upload or submit the signed application along with supporting documents. Applicants can also track application status through the Applicant Corner section of the iKhedut Portal.
benefits: Scheduled Caste farmers receive assistance up to 75% of the equipment cost or Rs.3,750, whichever is lower. Farmer groups, cooperative societies, and eligible organizations receive assistance up to 90% of the cost of Post-Harvest Management (PHM) equipment, subject to a maximum of Rs.7,50,000.

scheme_name: Harvesting Tools Assistance for Scheduled Tribes (HRT-3)
implementing_agency: Office of Director of Horticulture, Government of Gujarat
target_beneficiaries: Individual Farmers, Joint Liability Groups (JLGs), Self Help Groups (SHGs), Registered Societies, FPOs, FPCs, FIGs
tags: Farmer, Harvesting Tools, Scheduled Tribe, SHG, FPO, FPC, FIG, Cooperative Society, Financial Assistance, HRT-3
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Tribe farmers and eligible farmer organizations in Gujarat for purchasing harvesting tools.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Gujarat and belong to the Scheduled Tribe category. Individual farmers, FPOs, FPCs, FIGs, SHGs, and Cooperative Societies are eligible. Assistance can be availed once every five years. Equipment must be certified by ISO/BIS/ISI or a government-recognized organization and purchased through an authorized dealer.
documents_required: Scheduled Tribe Certificate, Disability Certificate (if applicable), Land Records (7/12 and 8-A), Aadhaar Card, Bank Passbook or Cancelled Cheque, Forest Rights Letter (if applicable), Consent Form for Joint Land Holders (if applicable), Registration and Audit Documents for FPO/FPC/Cooperative Society (if applicable), and other documents as required.
application_process: Apply online through the iKhedut Portal (https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in). Select Horticulture Schemes, choose HRT-3, complete and submit the application, upload required documents, confirm the application, and upload the signed application copy. Applicants can track their application status through the Applicant Corner on the portal.
benefits: Scheduled Tribe farmers receive assistance up to 50% of the cost of harvesting tools or Rs.1,00,000, whichever is lower. FPOs, FPCs, FIGs, SHGs, and Cooperative Societies receive assistance up to 75% of the cost or Rs.1,50,000, whichever is lower.

scheme_name: Harvesting Tools Assistance for Scheduled Castes (HRT-4)
implementing_agency: Directorate of Horticulture, Government of Gujarat
target_beneficiaries: Individual Farmers, Registered Societies, Self Help Groups (SHGs), FPOs, FPCs, FIGs, Cooperative Societies
tags: Farmer, Harvesting Tools, Scheduled Caste, SHG, FPO, FPC, FIG, Cooperative Society, Financial Assistance, HRT-4
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste farmers and eligible farmer organizations in Gujarat for purchasing harvesting tools and related equipment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Gujarat and belong to the Scheduled Caste category. Individual farmers, FPOs, FPCs, FIGs, SHGs, and Cooperative Societies are eligible. Assistance can be availed once every five years. Equipment must be certified by ISO/BIS/ISI or a government-recognized organization and purchased through an authorized dealer.
documents_required: Scheduled Caste Certificate, Disability Certificate (if applicable), Land Records (7/12 and 8-A), Aadhaar Card, Bank Passbook or Cancelled Cheque, Forest Rights Letter (if applicable), Consent Form for Joint Land Holders (if applicable), Registration and Audit Documents for FPO/FPC/Cooperative Society (if applicable), and other documents as required.
application_process: Apply online through the iKhedut Portal (https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in). Select Horticulture Schemes, choose HRT-4, complete and submit the application, upload required documents, confirm the application, and upload the signed application copy. Applicants can track their application status through the Applicant Corner on the portal.
benefits: Scheduled Caste farmers receive assistance up to 50% of the cost of harvesting tools or Rs.1,00,000, whichever is lower. FPOs, FPCs, FIGs, SHGs, and Cooperative Societies receive assistance up to 75% of the cost or Rs.1,50,000, whichever is lower.

scheme_name: Grant of Financial Assistance for Purchase of Textbooks/Notebooks to School-Going Children of Industrial Workers/Employees (GLWB)
implementing_agency: Goa Labour Welfare Board
tags: Education, School, Student, Textbooks, Notebooks, Worker
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to children of eligible industrial workers/employees in Goa studying in Classes 1 to 4 for the purchase of textbooks and notebooks.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a regular industrial worker/employee covered under the Goa Labour Welfare Board with at least 6 months of continuous service. The applicant should not be employed in supervisory or managerial positions earning Rs.1,600 or more per month and should have wages/salary not exceeding Rs.20,000 per month. The applicant's child should be studying in Classes 1 to 4 in a recognized or approved school. Preference is given to families with lower income, students studying in higher classes, and workers having not more than two children.
documents_required: 1. Proof of Regular Employment and Service Duration. 2. Salary Statement of the Worker. 3. School Enrollment Documents of the Student. 4. Caste Certificate and Income Certificate. 5. Certificate from the Institution.
application_process: Download the prescribed application form from the Goa Labour Welfare Board website, fill in the required details, attach supporting documents, and submit the completed application through the employer or the head of the educational institution where the child is studying to the Secretary, Goa Labour Welfare Board, Shram Shakti Bhavan, Panaji, Goa. Applications must reach the Board on or before 30th September each year.
benefits: Financial assistance for the purchase of textbooks and notebooks: Class 1 - Rs.1,200, Class 2 - Rs.1,200, Class 3 - Rs.1,800, and Class 4 - Rs.1,800.

implementing_agency: Goa Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Funeral Assistance, Death Benefit, Construction Worker, Financial Assistance, Labour Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to the nominee or dependent of a deceased construction worker to help meet funeral expenses.
eligibility_criteria: The deceased should have been a construction worker aged between 18 and 60 years, registered with the Goa Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, and should have completed at least 90 days of service as a registered worker. The applicant must be the nominee or dependent of the deceased worker. In the case of unregistered workers, eligibility may be considered upon submission of a certificate from the builder or area inspector confirming that the deceased was a construction worker.
documents_required: Worker Registration Card/Number, Receipt of First and Last Subscription Payment to the Board, Death Certificate of the Worker, Proof of Relationship between the Applicant and the Deceased Worker, and Certificate from Builder or Area Inspector for unregistered workers.
application_process: Obtain the prescribed application form from the Goa Labour Department website or the Office of the Commissioner, Labour & Employment, Panaji. Fill in the application form, attach the required documents, and submit it to the concerned authority at the Labour & Employment Office. A receipt will be issued upon successful submission.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.5,000 towards funeral expenses.

implementing_agency: Goa Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Financial Assistance, Pension, Spouse, Widow, Widower
brief_description: Provides family pension to the surviving spouse of a deceased pensioner registered with the Goa Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
eligibility_criteria: Spouse of a deceased pensioner who was registered with the Goa Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
documents_required: Worker Registration Card/Number, Subscription payment receipts, Death Certificate, Relationship Certificate from Village Officer, Certificates confirming no pension or salary from Government/Semi-Government/Private institutions.
application_process: Obtain the application form from the official website or Labour & Employment Office, fill it with required documents, submit it to the concerned office, and collect the acknowledgement receipt. Application must be submitted within 3 months of the pensioner's death.
benefits: 50% of the deceased pensioner's pension or Rs.200 per month, whichever is higher.

implementing_agency: Goa Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Accident, Death, Financial Assistance, Labour, Worker
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to the nominee/dependent of a deceased construction worker in cases of natural death or death due to an accident during employment.
eligibility_criteria: Deceased worker should be 18-60 years old, registered with the Goa Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, and have completed at least 90 days of service. Applicant must be the nominee/dependent of the deceased worker.
documents_required: Worker Registration Card/Number, Subscription payment receipts, Death Certificate, Cause of Death Certificate from a Government Doctor, Proof of relationship with the deceased, Certificate from builder/inspector for unregistered workers (if applicable).
application_process: Obtain the application form from the official website or Labour & Employment Office, fill it with required documents, submit it to the concerned authority, and collect the acknowledgement receipt.
benefits: Rs.30,000 for natural death and Rs.1,00,000 for death due to an accident during employment.

tags: Education, Financial Assistance, Scholarship
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to General category students of Classes 9 and 10 in Uttar Pradesh to support secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Uttar Pradesh, belong to the General category, be studying in Class 9 or 10, have a family income not exceeding Rs.2,50,000 per year, and be between 12 and 20 years of age.
documents_required: Previous examination marksheet, Income Certificate, Bank Passbook copy, Fee receipt (if applicable), Enrollment Number, Aadhaar Card, and Passport-size photograph.
application_process: Register on the scholarship portal, complete Fresh Login, fill the application form, upload required documents, complete verification, download the form, and submit it to the educational institution.
benefits: Rs.3,000 per year for the complete course duration.

scheme_name: Maintenance Allowance for Persons Affected with Muscular Dystrophy
tags: Disability, Financial Assistance, Monthly Allowance, Muscular Dystrophy, Social Welfare
brief_description: Provides monthly financial assistance to persons affected with muscular dystrophy to support their daily living and well-being.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be affected by muscular dystrophy and have a disability percentage of 40% or above.
documents_required: National Identity Card for Differently Abled Person, Family Card, Bank Account Details, and any other documents as required.
application_process: Obtain the application form from the District Differently Abled Welfare Office, fill it with required documents, submit it to the District Differently Abled Welfare Officer, and collect the acknowledgement receipt.
benefits: Monthly financial assistance of Rs.2,000, credited directly to the beneficiary's bank account through ECS.

scheme_name: Scheme for Providing Financial Assistance for the Purchase of Laptop or Desktop Computer to the Media Persons of the Union Territory of Puducherry
tags: Computer, Desktop, Financial Assistance, Laptop, Media, Newspaper
category: Science, IT & Communications, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to eligible media persons in Puducherry for purchasing a laptop or desktop computer.
eligibility_criteria: Indian citizen; native of Puducherry by birth or at least 10 years of residence; minimum 5 years of service as a full-time editor, reporter, sub-editor, photographer, correspondent, news agency reporter/photographer, or electronic media reporter/videographer.
documents_required: Nationality/Nativity/Residence Certificate, Service Certificate, Declaration with original purchase bill, Press ID Card issued by Directorate of Information & Publicity, Verification Certificate from Director of Information & Publicity.
application_process: Submit a formal application letter with required documents to the Director, Department of Information & Publicity, Puducherry.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.15,000 for purchase of one laptop or desktop computer.

implementing_agency: Urban Areas: District Deputy Director, Scheduled Caste Welfare Office; Rural Areas: District Social Welfare Officer Office
tags: Awas, BPL, Financial Assistance, House, Scheduled Caste
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste families in Gujarat for construction of a house.
eligibility_criteria: Resident of Gujarat; belongs to Scheduled Caste category; economically weaker section/BPL family; does not own a proper house or owns only a dilapidated house/plot; family has not availed benefits under another housing scheme; annual income up to Rs.6 lakh; age 21 years or above.
documents_required: Passport-size photo, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Ration Card, Income Certificate, Residence Proof, Land Ownership Proof, Bank Passbook/Cancelled Cheque, Election ID, House Construction Certificate, Self-Declaration, Photograph of plot/dilapidated house, and other required documents.
application_process: Register and apply through the e-Samaj Kalyan Portal, complete profile details, fill the application form, upload required documents, submit the application, and track status online.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.1,20,000 for house construction, released in three installments: Rs.40,000, Rs.60,000, and Rs.20,000.

scheme_name: Post Graduate Scholarship for GATE/GPAT Qualified Students for Pursuing M.Tech/M.E./M.Pharm-UGC
implementing_agency: University Grants Commission, Government of India
tags: GATE, GPAT, Post Graduate, Scholarship, Student, UGC
brief_description: Scholarship for GATE/GPAT qualified students pursuing full-time M.Tech, M.E., or M.Pharm courses in recognized institutions.
eligibility_criteria: GATE/GPAT qualified students admitted to full-time M.E., M.Tech., M.Pharm., or integrated dual degree programs in approved universities/institutions.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Age Proof, Mark Sheets/Degree Certificates, GATE/GPAT Score Card, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Disability Certificate (if applicable), Income Certificate (if applicable), Bank Account Details, and other required documents.
application_process: After admission, the institution submits the scholarship claim through the UGC portal. Students must register on the Canara Bank UGC Scholarship Portal using their student ID, complete profile verification, and track scholarship disbursement online.
benefits: Rs.12,400 per month scholarship and Rs.5,000 annual contingency grant for eligible students.

scheme_name: Grant to Meritorious Wards of Ex-Servicemen in Academics (Lump Sum Grant)
implementing_agency: Rajya Sainik Board, Puducherry
tags: College, Education, Ex-Serviceman, Merit, School, Student, Widow
brief_description: Provides lump sum grants to meritorious wards of Ex-Servicemen and widows of Ex-Servicemen for outstanding academic performance.
eligibility_criteria: Ward of an Ex-Serviceman or widow of an Ex-Serviceman; secured 1st, 2nd, or 3rd rank in Class 10 or 12 from a recognized institution, or obtained a gold medal in graduation/post-graduation from a recognized university.
documents_required: ESM/Widow Identity Card, Discharge Certificate, Mark Statement/Certificate, Birth Certificate, Study Certificate, Bank Passbook (first page), and Declaration by Ex-Serviceman/Widow.
application_process: Download the application form, fill it, attach required documents, and submit it to the Rajya Sainik Board, Department of Sainik Welfare, Puducherry.
benefits: Class 10: Rs.15,000 (1st), Rs.13,000 (2nd), Rs.11,000 (3rd); Class 12: Rs.18,000 (1st), Rs.16,000 (2nd), Rs.14,000 (3rd); grants also available for eligible undergraduate/postgraduate gold medalists.

scheme_name: Readers Allowance to Visually Impaired Persons
implementing_agency: Department for the Welfare of Differently Abled Persons, Government of Tamil Nadu.
tags: Differently-abled Person, Financial Assistance, Readers Allowance, Student, Visually Impaired
brief_description: Readers Allowance to Visually Impaired Persons provides annual financial assistance to visually impaired students in Tamil Nadu studying from Class 9 onwards.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants should be a visually impaired student and must be enrolled in a recognized institution.
documents_required: 1. Reader Certificate (issued from the head of the institution). 2. National Identity Card of the Differently Abled. 3. Statement of Marks (previous qualifying examination).
application_process: The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the District Differently Abled Welfare Oicer and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications. Then,fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of a the mandatory documents and submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the District Differently Abled Welfare Officer.
benefits: Reader's allowance is being paid at the following rate: 9th-10th Standard - Rs.3,000/- (per annum), Degree Courses - Rs.5,000/- (per annum), Post Graduate and Professional Courses - Rs.6,000/- (per annum).

scheme_name: Purchase/Construction of House for the Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Construction, Financial Assistance, Labour, Worker, Housing
brief_description: The Purchase/Construction of House for the Construction Workers scheme provides financial assistance to registered construction workers in Delhi for purchasing or constructing a house. Eligible workers can receive an advance of Rs.3,00,000 to Rs.5,00,000, which is recoverable in installments fixed by the Board.
eligibility_criteria: For registration, the applicant must be a resident of Delhi, a building/construction worker aged between 18 and 60 years, and must have worked for at least 90 days in a year. For availing the scheme, the applicant must have been registered with the Board for at least 3 years and should have a minimum of 10 to 15 years remaining before attaining 60 years of age.
documents_required: For house purchase: Encumbrance Certificate (14 years), Land Tax Receipt, Ownership Documents, Title Clearance Certificate, Sale Agreement, and affidavits from the applicant and sureties for repayment. For house construction: Approved building plan and estimate, along with an affidavit undertaking repayment of the advance.
application_process: Applicants must first register on the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board portal using Aadhaar, personal details, employment proof, bank details, and supporting documents. After successful registration and payment of the registration fee, eligible workers can log in to the portal and apply for the scheme. Application status can be tracked online through the Board's website.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.3,00,000 for workers with 10 years remaining before reaching 60 years of age and Rs.5,00,000 for workers with 15 years remaining before reaching 60 years of age, for the purchase or construction of a house.

scheme_name: Disability Pension for the Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Construction Workers, Disability, Financial Assistance, Labour, Pension
brief_description: The Disability Pension for the Construction Workers scheme provides a monthly pension of Rs.3,000 to registered construction workers in Delhi who are permanently disabled due to paralysis, leprosy, tuberculosis, accidents, or similar conditions.
eligibility_criteria: For registration, the applicant must be a resident of Delhi, a building or construction worker aged between 18 and 60 years, and must have worked for at least 90 days in a year. To avail the scheme, the applicant must be permanently disabled due to paralysis, leprosy, tuberculosis, an accident, or a similar condition.
documents_required: Permanent Disability Certificate issued by a Government Doctor or competent medical authority.
application_process: Applicants must first register on the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board portal by providing Aadhaar details, employment proof, personal information, bank account details, and required documents. After successful registration and payment of the registration fee, eligible workers can log in to the portal and apply for the Disability Pension scheme.
benefits: Monthly disability pension of Rs.3,000 for permanently disabled registered construction workers, payable from the date of registration subject to scheme conditions.

scheme_name: Reimbursement of Tuition Fees to the Wards of Ex-Servicemen/Widows (Sainik Welfare)
implementing_agency: Rajya Sainik Board, Puducherry
tags: Ex-Serviceman, Reimbursement, Student, Tuition Fees, Widow
brief_description: Provides tuition fee reimbursement to up to two wards of unemployed Ex-Servicemen or widows of Ex-Servicemen studying in government-recognized institutions in Puducherry.
eligibility_criteria: Ward of an unemployed Ex-Serviceman or widow of an Ex-Serviceman registered with the Department of Sainik Welfare, Puducherry. The ward must be studying in a government-recognized institution.
documents_required: Identity card of Ex-Serviceman/Widow, discharge certificate, proof of course completion, fee receipts, fee breakup certificate (if applicable), declaration, birth certificate, study certificate, and bank passbook copy.
application_process: Download and fill the application form, attach required documents, and submit it to the Rajya Sainik Board, Jawan Bhawan, Lawspet, Puducherry.
benefits: Tuition fee reimbursement up to the actual fee paid or prescribed limit: School Rs.1,000, Military/Sainik School Rs.2,000, Special School Rs.2,000, Polytechnic Diploma Rs.800, NDA/IMA Rs.2,000, Bachelor's Degree Rs.1,000, Master's Degree Rs.1,500, Professional Courses Rs.2,000.

scheme_name: Ex-gratia Payment for the Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Construction Workers, Ex-gratia Payment, Financial Assistance, Labour, Permanent Disability
brief_description: Provides a one-time ex-gratia assistance of Rs.1,00,000 to registered construction workers in Delhi who suffer permanent disability.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered construction worker of the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, aged 18-60 years, with at least 90 days of work in a year, and must have permanent disability.
documents_required: Permanent disability certificate issued by a government doctor or competent medical board.
application_process: Register on the DBOCW Board portal, log in using Aadhaar/Registration Card/OTP, apply for the scheme online, upload required documents, and track the application status through the portal.
benefits: One-time ex-gratia payment of Rs.1,00,000 in case of permanent disability.

scheme_name: Pension Benefit for the Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Construction Workers, Financial Assistance, Labour, Pension, Senior Citizens
brief_description: Provides a monthly pension of Rs.3,000 to registered construction workers in Delhi after attaining 60 years of age, with additional benefits for longer membership.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered construction worker in Delhi, aged 60 years or above, with at least one year of work as a building worker and 90 days of work in a year as required by the Board.
documents_required: Proof of date of birth.
application_process: Register on the DBOCW Board portal, log in using Aadhaar/Registration Card/OTP, apply for the pension scheme online, upload required documents, and track the application status through the portal.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.3,000 after completion of 60 years of age. An additional Rs.300 per month is provided for every completed year of membership beyond five years.

scheme_name: Accidental Death Assistance for the Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Accidental Death, Construction Workers, Financial Assistance, Labour
brief_description: Provides financial assistance of Rs.2,00,000 to the nominees or dependents of registered construction workers who die due to an accident during the course of employment.
eligibility_criteria: The deceased must have been a registered construction worker of the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, aged 18–60 years, with at least 90 days of work in a year. The applicant must be the nominee or dependent of the deceased worker.
documents_required: Death certificate, Aadhaar card of nominee, bank passbook/cancelled cheque of nominee, and FIR or MLC report related to the accident.
application_process: Register on the DBOCW Board portal, log in using Aadhaar/Registration Card/OTP, apply for the scheme online, upload required documents, and track the application status through the portal.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.2,00,000 to the nominee or dependent in case of accidental death of a registered worker during employment.

implementing_agency: State and Union Territory Governments
tags: Cash Incentive, Financial Assistance, Maternal Health, Pregnant Women, Lactating Mothers, Girl Child
category: Health & Wellness, Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women and Child Development
brief_description: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY) provides maternity benefits to pregnant and lactating women from socially and economically disadvantaged backgrounds. Eligible women receive Rs.5,000 for their first child in two installments, while Rs.6,000 is provided for a second child if the child is a girl.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be at least 19 years old and pregnant. Applicant should be employed and experiencing wage loss due to pregnancy. Benefits are available for the first living child. For the second child, benefits are available only if the child is a girl. Application must be submitted within 270 days of childbirth. Eligible categories include SC/ST women, women with disabilities, BPL families, PMJAY beneficiaries, e-Shram card holders, PM-KISAN beneficiaries, MGNREGA job card holders, NFSA ration card holders, ASHA/AWW workers, and families with annual income below Rs.8 lakh.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Aadhaar-linked Bank/Post Office Account, Bank Account Details, Eligibility Proof, MCP/RCHI Card, Last Menstrual Period (LMP) Details, Antenatal Checkup (ANC) Details, Child Birth Certificate, Child Immunization Records, and supporting documents such as BPL Card, e-Shram Card, PMJAY Card, PM-KISAN Proof, MGNREGA Job Card, Disability Certificate, Caste Certificate, or NFSA Ration Card, as applicable.
application_process: Visit https://pmmvy.wcd.gov.in/ and click on Citizen Login. Register using a mobile number and create an account. Log in with the generated credentials. Select 'Beneficiary Registration' under the Data Entry section. Choose whether applying for the first child or second girl child benefit. Fill in all required details, upload supporting documents, and submit the application online.
benefits: For the first child: Rs.3,000 is provided upon pregnancy registration and at least one antenatal checkup within six months of LMP, and Rs.2,000 is provided after birth registration and completion of required immunization up to 14 weeks, totaling Rs.5,000 under PMMVY (plus eligible JSY benefits). For a second child who is a girl: a one-time assistance of Rs.6,000 is provided after fulfilling pregnancy registration, ANC, birth registration, and immunization requirements.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance For Education Of Children Of Registered Worker (HBOCB)
implementing_agency: Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Children, Construction Workers, Education, Financial Assistance, Scholarship, Students
brief_description: Financial Assistance For Education Of Children Of Registered Worker (HBOCB) provides annual educational assistance ranging from Rs.8,000 to Rs.20,000 for children of registered construction workers in Haryana studying from Class 1 to postgraduate level. Additional incentives up to Rs.51,000 are provided for outstanding Class 10 performance.
eligibility_criteria: The worker must be registered under the Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HBOCB) with at least one year of regular membership. Children studying from Class 1 to postgraduate level in recognized schools, colleges, or institutions within Haryana are eligible. Students who are employed or self-employed are not eligible. Financial assistance is available for up to three children (three girls or two boys). Assistance is not provided again for the same class if a student fails.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Educational Certificate, Birth Certificate, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and a certificate from the head of the institution confirming regular study.
application_process: The worker must first register with HBOCB through the Haryana Labour Department portal and complete the required membership formalities. After obtaining eligibility, the applicant should register/login on the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal (https://saralharyana.gov.in), select the scheme, fill out the online application form, upload the required documents, and submit the application.
benefits: Annual assistance of Rs.8,000 for Classes 1-8, Rs.10,000 for Classes 9-12 and ITI courses, Rs.15,000 for undergraduate courses, and Rs.20,000 for postgraduate courses. Students scoring 90% or above in Class 10 receive a one-time incentive of Rs.51,000; 80-89% receive Rs.41,000; 70-79% receive Rs.31,000; and 60-69% receive Rs.21,000. Educational expenses for engineering and other professional courses may be covered by the Board. Hostel expenses up to Rs.1,20,000 may also be reimbursed.

scheme_name: Banglashree for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises: State Capital Investment Subsidy (CIS)
implementing_agency: Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises and Textiles, Government of West Bengal
tags: Entrepreneurship, Financial Assistance, Incentives, MSME, Subsidy
brief_description: State Capital Investment Subsidy (CIS) under the Banglashree for MSMEs scheme provides capital investment subsidy to eligible micro and small manufacturing enterprises in West Bengal for approved projects.
eligibility_criteria: The business must be a Micro, Small, or Medium Enterprise in the manufacturing sector. Commercial production must have commenced between 1 April 2020 and 31 March 2025. The enterprise may be in the private sector, cooperative sector, joint sector, state-owned undertaking, or industrial self-help group.
documents_required: Memorandum and Articles of Association/Partnership documents (if applicable), details of directors/partners/owners, approved project report, loan sanction and disbursement letters, audited balance sheets (where applicable), machinery invoices and receipts, production details (if applicable), land/building ownership or lease documents, mutation and conversion certificates, consent to operate certificate from WBPCB, SGST documents, trade licence, first electricity bill, Udyam Registration acknowledgement, SC/ST certificate (if applicable), statutory licences/certificates, self-declaration and self-certification forms.
application_process: Register on the Shilpasathi Portal, create an account, log in, select MSME Incentive services, complete the eligibility form and Common Application Form (CAF), upload required documents, verify details, submit the application, and obtain the application reference number for tracking.
benefits: Micro Enterprises: 25% subsidy of Fixed Capital Assets (FCA) in Zone C, 40% in Zone D, and 60% in Zone E. Small Enterprises: 15% in Zone C, 30% in Zone D, and 40% in Zone E. An additional 20% subsidy is available for enterprises fully owned by women, SC/ST, or minority entrepreneurs. Special subsidies are also available for such enterprises in Zones A and B. Subsidy ceilings apply based on enterprise category and zone.

scheme_name: Braille Watches by Welfare of Differently Abled Persons Department
implementing_agency: Department for the Welfare of Differently Abled Persons, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Braille Watches, Differently Abled Person, Employment, Social Welfare, Visually Challenged Person
brief_description: The scheme provides free Braille watches to visually impaired working persons in Tamil Nadu to help them manage time effectively and reach their workplace on time.
eligibility_criteria: The applicant must be a visually impaired working person employed in the unorganized sector. Eligible applicants may be employed or self-employed.
documents_required: National Identity Card of the Differently Abled Person and any other documents required by the authorities.
application_process: Visit the District Differently Abled Welfare Office during office hours and obtain the application form. Fill in the required details, attach the necessary documents, and submit the completed application to the District Differently Abled Welfare Officer. Obtain an acknowledgment receipt after submission.
benefits: Free distribution of Braille watches to eligible visually impaired working persons.

implementing_agency: Department for the Welfare of Differently Abled Persons, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Children, Differently Abled, Orthopedic Disability, Assistive Device, Social Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides rotators/walkers free of cost to orthopedically differently abled children who face difficulties in walking.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an orthopedically differently abled child with difficulty in walking.
documents_required: National Identity Card for Differently Abled Persons.
application_process: Apply through the District Differently Abled Welfare Office by submitting the prescribed application form along with required documents.
benefits: Free distribution of walking devices (rotators/walkers).

implementing_agency: Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HBOCB)
tags: Construction Worker, Paternity Benefit, Newborn, Financial Assistance, Labour Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered male construction workers for the care of a newborn child and nutritional support for the mother.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered male construction worker under HBOCB with at least one year of regular membership. The worker's wife must have delivered a child and should not have claimed maternity benefits from another employer.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or Voter ID, Passport-size Photograph, Residence Proof, Ration Card, Bank Account Details, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Birth Certificate of Child, Undertaking regarding non-availment of maternity benefits by spouse, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal after registration as a beneficiary under HBOCB and upload the required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.21,000, including Rs.15,000 for newborn care and Rs.6,000 for nutritional support to the mother.

scheme_name: Banglashree for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises: Subsidy for Work Force Welfare Assistance (AS)
implementing_agency: Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises and Textiles, Government of West Bengal
tags: MSME, Subsidy, EPF, ESI, Workforce Welfare, Entrepreneurship
brief_description: The scheme provides reimbursement of employer contributions towards EPF and ESI for eligible MSMEs employing workers registered with the State Employment Bank.
eligibility_criteria: Manufacturing MSMEs that commenced commercial production between 1 April 2020 and 31 March 2025 and employ at least 50% of workers from the State Employment Bank are eligible.
documents_required: Project Report, Udyam/Udyog Aadhaar Registration, EPF and ESI Contribution Records, SGST Documents, Trade Licence, Consent to Operate Certificate, Financial Statements, Land and Building Documents, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Shilpasathi Portal by registering, creating a CAF, submitting Form-A, uploading required documents, and completing verification.
benefits: Reimbursement of 100% of eligible EPF and ESI employer contributions in the first year and 75% in subsequent years for the approved assistance period.

scheme_name: Outstanding Entrepreneur under Motivation of Entrepreneurs to Start Industries and Fiscal Assistance to Industries - State Awards to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
implementing_agency: Industries and Commerce Department, Government of Puducherry
tags: Award, Entrepreneur, MSME, Industry, Recognition
brief_description: The scheme recognizes and rewards outstanding entrepreneurs and enterprises operating in Puducherry under the New Industrial Policy 2016.
eligibility_criteria: The industrial unit should have been operating profitably for at least 3 years and should provide at least 60% employment to residents of Puducherry.
documents_required: Entrepreneurs Memorandum Part-II (UAM/PMT) Registration or Commencement of Production Certificate.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Industries and Commerce when applications are invited.
benefits: Cash award of Rs.2,00,000 along with a citation.

scheme_name: Banglashree for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises: Subsidy for State Goods and Services Tax (SGST)
implementing_agency: Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises and Textiles, Government of West Bengal
tags: MSME, SGST Refund, Subsidy, Entrepreneurship, Manufacturing
brief_description: The scheme provides reimbursement of net SGST paid by eligible manufacturing MSMEs in West Bengal.
eligibility_criteria: Manufacturing MSMEs that commenced commercial production between 1 April 2020 and 31 March 2025 are eligible.
documents_required: Project Report, SGST Records, Udyam/Udyog Aadhaar Registration, Trade Licence, Consent to Operate Certificate, Land and Building Documents, Financial Statements, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Shilpasathi Portal by registering, creating a CAF, submitting Form-A, and uploading supporting documents.
benefits: Refund of net SGST paid for up to 8 years. Eligible enterprises may receive 30% refund in Zones B and C and 50% refund in Zones D and E, subject to scheme limits.

implementing_agency: Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HBOCB)
tags: Education, Coaching, Construction Worker, Student, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to children of registered construction workers for coaching classes related to professional and technical courses and competitive examinations.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered construction worker under HBOCB with at least one year of regular membership. Assistance is available for children pursuing coaching for professional or technical courses. The benefit can be availed only once per child and for up to three children.
documents_required: Worker Identity Card, Student Identity Proof, Proof of Examination Qualification, Certificate from Coaching Institute, Verification Certificate (for private institutes), Undertaking by Applicant, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the HBOCB beneficiary portal after logging in with registered credentials and submitting the required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.20,000 for coaching classes for professional or technical courses. Assistance of Rs.1,00,000 is provided for preparation of UPSC/HPSC Main Examination after qualifying the Preliminary Examination.

scheme_name: Subsidy to Pollution Control Equipment under Motivation of Entrepreneurs to Start Industries and Fiscal Assistance to Capital Intensive Industries
implementing_agency: Industries and Commerce Department, Government of Puducherry
tags: Industry, Pollution Control, Environment, MSME, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to industrial units for installation of pollution control equipment.
eligibility_criteria: Industrial units that have installed pollution control equipment and provide at least 60% employment to residents of Puducherry are eligible. The unit must comply with the conditions prescribed under the scheme.
documents_required: Entrepreneurs Memorandum/UAM Registration, Commencement of Production Certificate, Land Documents, Loan Sanction Letter, Pollution Control Board Approvals, Purchase Invoices, Chartered Accountant Certificate, Consent Orders, Affidavit, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Industries and Commerce, Puducherry.
benefits: Subsidy of 25% of eligible expenditure on pollution control equipment, subject to a maximum of Rs.5,00,000.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Medical Treatment of Journalists
implementing_agency: Tamil Development, Religious Endowments and Information Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Journalist, Medical Assistance, Health, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible journalists for medical treatment in recognized hospitals.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a full-time journalist with at least 5 years of work experience. Application must be submitted within three months of treatment. Retired journalists receiving pension are also eligible but may receive reduced assistance.
documents_required: Passport-size Photographs, Birth Certificate, Service Certificate or Pension Order (if applicable), Income Certificate, Experience Certificate, Residence Proof, Medical Records, Treatment Details, and Estimated Cost Certificate from the Hospital.
application_process: Download the application form, fill it with required details, attach supporting documents, and submit it to the District Public Relations Officer.
benefits: Financial assistance of up to Rs.4,00,000 for medical treatment.

scheme_name: Early Intervention Centre for the Visually Impaired
implementing_agency: Department for the Welfare of Differently Abled Persons, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Visually Impaired, Children, Education, Early Intervention, Social Welfare
brief_description: The scheme identifies and supports visually impaired children below six years of age and prepares them for mainstream education.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a visually impaired child aged six years or below.
documents_required: Disability Certificate, Age Proof, Identity Proof of Parent/Guardian, and other documents as prescribed by the department.
application_process: Apply through the District Differently Abled Welfare Office by submitting the prescribed application form along with supporting documents.
benefits: Early intervention, developmental support, and educational preparation for integration into mainstream schooling.

implementing_agency: Art and Culture Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Artist, Funeral Assistance, Death Benefit, Financial Assistance, Culture
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to the families of registered folk artists in the event of natural death and supports funeral expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the nominee or legal heir of a registered folk artist who has died a natural death.
documents_required: Death Certificate, Legal Heir Certificate, No Objection Certificate from Other Legal Heirs (if applicable), Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, Bank Account Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed claim form along with required documents to the Assistant Director or Deputy Director of the concerned regional office.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.55,000, including Rs.50,000 for natural death assistance and Rs.5,000 towards funeral expenses.

implementing_agency: Department of Justice, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India
tags: Access to Justice, Internship, Law Students, Legal Research, Justice Delivery
category: Education & Learning, Law & Justice, Skills & Employment
brief_description: The LLB Internship Programme provides law students with exposure to the functioning of the Department of Justice and offers practical experience in legal research, policy analysis, and justice delivery initiatives.
eligibility_criteria: Indian students who have completed the 2nd year of a three-year law degree course and are entering the 3rd year, or students in the 3rd year of a three-year law degree course, or students who have completed the 4th year and are entering the 5th year of a five-year integrated law degree course, or students in the 5th year of a five-year integrated law degree course.
