documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Marksheets of Previous Examinations, Recommendation Letter from Head of Department/Principal, No Objection Certificate (NOC) from Institution, Identity Proof, Proof of Age, Student Identity Card/Enrollment Proof, Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the Department of Justice Internship Portal by selecting the internship slot, filling the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Honorarium of Rs.5,000 upon successful completion of the internship and an Internship Completion Certificate.

scheme_name: Incentive Scheme for MSMEs in Powerloom Sector: Subsidy for Energy Efficiency
implementing_agency: Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises and Textiles, Government of West Bengal
tags: Energy Audit, Energy Efficiency, Energy Conservation, Powerloom, MSME, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible MSMEs in the powerloom sector for conducting energy audits and implementing energy conservation measures.
eligibility_criteria: MSMEs in the powerloom sector that commenced production between 1 January 2022 and 31 December 2024, have executed the required four-party agreement, and have an approved project sanctioned by a financial institution are eligible.
documents_required: Project Report, Loan Sanction Letter, Agreement Copy, Audited Financial Statements, Udyam Registration Certificate, Trade Licence, SGST Documents, Consent to Operate Certificate, Energy Audit Report, Bills and Receipts for Energy Audit and Energy Conservation Measures, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application in Form-A2 along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Textiles within 12 months from the date of commencement of commercial production.
benefits: 50% subsidy on the cost of energy audit and 25% subsidy on the cost of energy conservation measures, subject to a maximum of Rs.2,00,000.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Education - Haryana Silicosis Board
implementing_agency: Haryana Labour Welfare Board, Government of Haryana
target_beneficiaries: Children of Workers Affected by Silicosis
tags: Education, Silicosis, Student, Worker Welfare, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides educational assistance to children of workers diagnosed with silicosis by the Haryana Silicosis Diagnosis Board.
eligibility_criteria: The worker should be registered with the Haryana Silicosis Board and diagnosed with silicosis by the Silicosis Diagnosis Board. Assistance is available for children studying from Class I to Post-Graduation. Benefits are limited to up to two sons and three daughters.
documents_required: Worker Identity Card, Aadhaar Card, Voter ID Card, Admission Certificate, Previous Class Marksheet, Birth Certificate, Bank Passbook Copy, Death Certificate of Worker (if applicable), and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply through the Haryana Labour Department by submitting the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Assistant Director, Industrial Health.
benefits: Annual educational assistance of Rs.5,000 for Classes I-V, Rs.6,000 for Classes VI-VIII, Rs.8,000 for Classes IX-X, Rs.10,000 for Classes XI-XII, and Rs.12,000 for ITI, Diploma, Graduation, and Post-Graduation courses.

scheme_name: West Bengal Textile Incentive Scheme: Subsidy for Water Conservation and Environment Compliance
implementing_agency: Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises and Textiles, Government of West Bengal
tags: Textile, Water Conservation, Environment Compliance, Effluent Treatment, Subsidy, Entrepreneurship
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to textile enterprises for installation of captive effluent treatment plants and pollution control systems.
eligibility_criteria: Textile manufacturing and processing units that commenced production on or after 1 April 2022 and possess the required registration, commencement, and eligibility certificates are eligible.
documents_required: Bills and Receipts for Effluent Treatment Plant and Pollution Control Equipment, Details of Installed Systems, Registration Certificates, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Register on the West Bengal Textiles Portal, complete the Common Application Form (CAF), upload supporting documents, and submit the application online.
benefits: Reimbursement of 25% of eligible expenditure subject to a maximum of Rs.1.5 crore for effluent treatment plants and pollution control systems.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Kashmiri Migrant Families Scheme
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Haryana
tags: Kashmiri Migrants, Family Welfare, Financial Assistance, Rehabilitation
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Kashmiri migrant families residing in Haryana who were displaced from Jammu and Kashmir.
eligibility_criteria: The family should have migrated from Jammu and Kashmir to Haryana due to displacement and should be residing in Haryana. Annual family income from all sources should not exceed Rs.2,00,000.
documents_required: Migration Certificate issued by the competent authority, Ration Card, Voter ID Card, Aadhaar Card, Family ID Proof, Income Certificate, Passport-size Photographs, Bank Account Details, and Passbook Copy.
application_process: Obtain the prescribed application form from the District Social Welfare Officer or download it, fill in the required details, attach supporting documents, and submit it to the concerned District Social Welfare Office.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.1,250 per month per family member for five years, subject to a maximum of Rs.6,250 per family per month.

implementing_agency: Haryana Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation
tags: Women, Scheduled Caste, Agriculture, Self Employment, Loan, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste women for self-employment activities in agriculture and allied sectors through loans, margin money assistance, and subsidy.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a female resident of Haryana belonging to a Scheduled Caste community. The applicant's family should be listed under BPL and satisfy the income criteria prescribed by the corporation.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Residence Proof, BPL Certificate or BPL Survey Record, Income Certificate, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal by registering, selecting the scheme, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Loan assistance for self-employment projects up to Rs.50,000, margin money assistance of 10% of the project cost, and subsidy up to 50% of the project cost as per scheme norms.

implementing_agency: Haryana Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation
tags: Scheduled Caste, Self Employment, Entrepreneurship, Loan, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste beneficiaries for undertaking self-employment and income-generating activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana, belong to a Scheduled Caste community, be between 18 and 45 years of age, and satisfy the income criteria prescribed under the scheme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, Caste Certificate, Residence Proof, Income Certificate, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal by registering, selecting the scheme, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Loan assistance for self-employment projects up to Rs.2,00,000, margin money assistance of 10% of the project cost, and subsidy up to 50% of the project cost as per scheme guidelines.

scheme_name: Monetary Relief to the Victim of Atrocities - Haryana
implementing_agency: Directorate of Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes, Government of Haryana
tags: Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, Atrocities, Victim Relief, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides monetary relief to Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe victims of atrocities committed under the provisions of the SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana belonging to the Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe community and must be a victim, dependent, or family member of a victim of an atrocity for which an FIR has been registered under the SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Proof, SC/ST Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook, Copy of FIR or Charge Sheet, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal by registering, selecting the scheme, completing the application form, uploading supporting documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial assistance ranging from Rs.85,000 to Rs.8,25,000 depending upon the nature of the offence and relief norms prescribed under the SC/ST (Prevention of Atrocities) Rules.

scheme_name: Pragati Scholarship Scheme for Girl Students (Technical Diploma)
implementing_agency: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
tags: Scholarship, Girls Education, Technical Diploma, Higher Education, Women Empowerment
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to girl students pursuing diploma-level technical education in AICTE-approved institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a girl student admitted to the first year of a diploma course or the second year through lateral entry in an AICTE-approved institution. Annual family income from all sources should not exceed Rs.8,00,000. A maximum of two girl children per family are eligible.
documents_required: 10th Certificate, 12th Certificate (if applicable), ITI Certificate (for lateral entry), Aadhaar Card, Bank Passbook, Category Certificate (if applicable), Passport-size Photograph, Study Certificate, Family Income Certificate, Parent Declaration, and Bank Mandate Form.
application_process: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Scholarship of Rs.50,000 per annum for each year of study for up to 3 years for first-year diploma students and up to 2 years for lateral-entry students.

implementing_agency: Department of School Education and Literacy, Ministry of Education, Government of India
target_beneficiaries: Meritorious Students from Economically Weaker Sections
tags: Scholarship, Merit, Education, Economically Weaker Sections, School Students
brief_description: The scheme provides scholarships to meritorious students from economically weaker sections to reduce dropout rates at the secondary stage and encourage continuation of education.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a regular student studying in Class IX in a Government, Government-aided, or Local Body school. Annual parental income should not exceed Rs.3,50,000. Students must qualify the selection test conducted by the respective State or UT and satisfy the prescribed academic requirements.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Previous Class Marksheet, School Verification Certificate, Bank Account Details, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Passport-size Photograph, and other documents as prescribed by the National Scholarship Portal.
application_process: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading supporting documents, and submitting the application before the prescribed deadline.
benefits: Scholarship of Rs.12,000 per annum (Rs.1,000 per month) from Class IX to Class XII, subject to fulfillment of renewal conditions.

scheme_name: The Lokpal of India Internship Scheme
implementing_agency: Lokpal of India
tags: Internship, Law, Public Policy, Governance, Stipend, Students
brief_description: The internship scheme provides students with practical exposure to anti-corruption laws, governance institutions, and the functioning of the Lokpal of India.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a regular undergraduate or postgraduate student in Law, Political Science, Public Administration, Public Policy, Management, Economics, or Human Rights. The applicant should have secured at least 75% marks in Class 12 and at least 55% aggregate marks in the qualifying degree. Undergraduate students should have completed at least two years of study. The applicant must possess a personal laptop.
documents_required: Educational Certificates, Recommendation Letter from Institution, and other documents as prescribed by the Lokpal of India.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form through the concerned institution and send it along with supporting documents to the Deputy Secretary (Administration), Lokpal of India, by email or post.
benefits: Stipend of Rs.10,000 per month, Internship Completion Certificate, and access to workspace and internet facilities.

scheme_name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) for MSME Sector: Assistance for Quality Certification
implementing_agency: Department of Industries, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
tags: MSME, Quality Certification, Entrepreneurship, Subsidy, Reimbursement
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to MSMEs for obtaining national and international quality certifications and related testing equipment.
eligibility_criteria: New or existing MSME manufacturing or service units undertaking expansion or diversification in the Union Territory and commencing commercial production between 20 May 2022 and 19 May 2027 are eligible. Applications must be submitted within one year from the date of issue of the quality certificate.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, PAN, GST Registration, CIN or Business Registration Documents, Land or Lease Documents, Factory Licence, Consent to Operate Certificate, Electricity Connection Documents, Undertaking, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Single Window Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Reimbursement of 50% of certification fees for national or international quality certifications and 50% of the cost of testing equipment and machinery required for certification, subject to a maximum assistance of Rs.10,00,000.

scheme_name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) for MSME Sector: Assistance for ZED Certification
implementing_agency: Department of Industries, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
tags: MSME, ZED Certification, Quality Improvement, Entrepreneurship, Subsidy
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to MSMEs for obtaining Zero Defect Zero Effect (ZED) Certification to improve product quality and environmental sustainability.
eligibility_criteria: New or existing MSME manufacturing or service units undertaking expansion or diversification in the Union Territory and commencing commercial production between 20 May 2022 and 19 May 2027 are eligible. Applications must be submitted within one year from the date of issue of the ZED certificate.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, PAN, GST Registration, CIN or Business Registration Documents, Land or Lease Documents, Factory Licence, Consent to Operate Certificate, Electricity Connection Documents, Undertaking, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Single Window Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial assistance of 50% of eligible expenditure, subject to a maximum of Rs.1,00,000, after deducting assistance received from the Government of India for ZED Certification.

scheme_name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) for MSME Sector: Assistance for Saving in Consumption of Energy and Water
implementing_agency: Department of Industries, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
tags: Energy Conservation, Water Conservation, MSME, Entrepreneurship, Subsidy
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to MSMEs for implementing energy and water conservation measures based on audit recommendations.
eligibility_criteria: New or existing MSME manufacturing or service units undertaking expansion or diversification in the Union Territory and commencing commercial production between 20 May 2022 and 19 May 2027 are eligible. The enterprise must submit an energy or water audit certificate and proof of implementation of recommended measures.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, PAN, GST Registration, CIN or Business Registration Documents, Land or Lease Documents, Factory Licence, Consent to Operate Certificate, Energy or Water Audit Report, Undertaking, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Single Window Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: 75% reimbursement of energy or water audit costs subject to a maximum of Rs.1,00,000 and 25% reimbursement of the cost of recommended equipment subject to a maximum of Rs.20,00,000.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Social Welfare Department, Government of Bihar
tags: Disability, Mobility Assistance, Tricycle, Students, Empowerment
brief_description: The scheme provides battery-operated tricycles to eligible persons with locomotor disabilities to improve mobility, accessibility, and independence.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Bihar with at least 60% locomotor disability. The applicant should be a student or employed person aged 18 years or above, with annual family income not exceeding Rs.2,00,000. The distance between residence and place of study or work should be at least 3 kilometres.
documents_required: Photograph, Income Certificate, Residence Certificate, Disability Certificate, Student ID Card or Employment Certificate, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Bihar Social Welfare Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Free distribution of battery-operated tricycles.

implementing_agency: Department of Education, Government of Bihar
tags: Education Loan, Higher Education, Student Empowerment, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides education loans to eligible students of Bihar to pursue higher education in recognized institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Bihar, have passed Class 12 or equivalent examination, and secured admission to a recognized higher education course. The applicant should generally be below 25 years of age at the time of application.
documents_required: Application Form, Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Class 10 and 12 Mark Sheets and Certificates, Admission Proof, Course Fee Structure, Photographs, Income Certificate, Income Tax Returns (if applicable), Bank Statements, and Residence Proof.
application_process: Apply online through the 7 Nischay Portal, complete registration, fill the Bihar Student Credit Card application form, upload required documents, attend document verification at the District Registration and Counselling Centre (DRCC), and complete bank formalities after loan approval.
benefits: Education loan of up to Rs.4,00,000 for approved higher education courses. The loan can be used for tuition fees, books, laptops, and other educational expenses. Interest rate is 1% for girls, transgender persons, and differently-abled students.

scheme_name: Education Loan Scheme - Credit Line 1 for Minority Community
implementing_agency: Haryana Backward Classes and Economically Weaker Sections Kalyan Nigam
tags: Education Loan, Higher Education, Minority Community, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides education loans to students from minority communities for pursuing higher education in India and abroad.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana, belong to a notified minority community (Muslim, Sikh, Christian, Buddhist, Parsi, or Jain), be between 16 and 32 years of age, satisfy the prescribed income criteria, have secured at least 50% marks in the qualifying examination, and have obtained admission to a recognized professional or technical course.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Haryana Domicile Certificate, Minority Community Certificate, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), PAN Card, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, Fee Structure, Admission Proof, Educational Certificates, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal by registering, selecting the scheme, filling the application form, uploading supporting documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Education loan up to Rs.20,00,000 for studies in India and up to Rs.30,00,000 for studies abroad at an interest rate of 3%.

scheme_name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) for MSME Sector: Assistance for Participation in Industrial Exhibitions Abroad
implementing_agency: Department of Industries, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
tags: MSME, Industrial Exhibition, Export Promotion, Entrepreneurship, Subsidy
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to MSMEs for participating in international industrial exhibitions to promote products and expand business opportunities abroad.
eligibility_criteria: New or existing MSME manufacturing or service units undertaking expansion or diversification in the Union Territory and commencing commercial production between 20 May 2022 and 19 May 2027 are eligible. Applications should be submitted within three months of completion of the exhibition.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, PAN, GST Registration, Business Registration Documents, Land or Lease Documents, Factory Licence, Consent to Operate Certificate, Exhibition Participation Documents, Chartered Accountant Certified Bills, Bank Transaction Proof, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Single Window Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading supporting documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: 50% reimbursement of exhibition space rental charges, subject to a maximum of Rs.2,00,000.

scheme_name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) for MSME Sector: Incentive for Local Employment
implementing_agency: Department of Industries, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
tags: Employment Generation, MSME, Local Employment, Entrepreneurship, Incentive
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides incentives to MSMEs for generating employment opportunities for local residents of the Union Territory.
eligibility_criteria: New or existing MSME manufacturing or service units undertaking expansion or diversification in the Union Territory and commencing commercial production between 20 May 2022 and 19 May 2027 are eligible. Recruited employees should be local residents possessing domicile certificates and should be above 18 years of age.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, PAN, GST Registration, Business Registration Documents, Land or Lease Documents, Factory Licence, Consent to Operate Certificate, Salary Payment Records, Domicile Certificates of Employees, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Single Window Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading supporting documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: One-time recruitment incentive of Rs.3,00,000 for every 20 local employees, subject to a maximum assistance of Rs.15,00,000 per eligible unit.

implementing_agency: Haryana Backward Classes and Economically Weaker Sections Kalyan Nigam
tags: Backward Classes, Entrepreneurship, Professional Loan, Self Employment
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to young professionals from Backward Classes for establishing professional practices and self-employment ventures.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana, belong to a Backward Class category, be between 18 and 35 years of age, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.3,00,000, and possess the required professional qualifications or training.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Haryana Domicile Certificate, Backward Class Certificate, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, Income Certificate, Professional Qualification Certificates, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal by registering, selecting the scheme, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Loan assistance of up to Rs.10,00,000 for establishing professional clinics, laboratories, offices, engineering units, chemist shops, and similar self-employment ventures.

implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Uttarakhand
tags: Disability, Divyang, Pension, Financial Assistance, Social Security
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly pension assistance to eligible persons with disabilities residing in Uttarakhand.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand, aged 18 years or above, have at least 40% disability, and either belong to a BPL family or have a monthly income not exceeding Rs.4,000.
documents_required: Income Certificate or BPL Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Disability Certificate or UDID Card, Family Register Copy, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Uttarakhand Social Welfare Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.1,500.

implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Uttarakhand
tags: Farmer, Pension, Social Security, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly pension assistance to elderly farmers in Uttarakhand who cultivate their own land and do not receive any other pension.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand, be at least 60 years of age, own and cultivate not more than 2 hectares of land, and should not be receiving pension benefits from any other source.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Age Proof, Land Ownership Records, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Uttarakhand Social Welfare Portal by completing the registration, filling the application form, uploading supporting documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.1,200.

implementing_agency: Department of Art and Culture, Government of Puducherry
tags: Author, Writer, Literature, Children, Award, Culture
brief_description: The scheme recognizes authors for outstanding contributions to Tamil children's literature through a cash award and certificate.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a writer, author, or joint author who is a native of Puducherry by birth or has been residing in Puducherry continuously for at least five years. The submitted work should be an original Tamil children's literature publication and satisfy the conditions prescribed under the scheme.
documents_required: Completed Application Form, Passport-size Photograph, Copies of Published Book, Residence Proof, Author Declaration, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with the required documents and copies of the published work to the Department of Art and Culture, Puducherry.
benefits: Cash award of Rs.10,000 and a certificate.

implementing_agency: Haryana Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (HSFDC) in collaboration with National Safai Karamcharis Finance and Development Corporation (NSKFDC)
tags: Education Loan, Safai Karamchari, Higher Education, Professional Courses, Student
brief_description: The scheme provides education loans to Safai Karamcharis and their dependents for pursuing recognized professional and technical courses in India and abroad.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana, aged between 18 and 55 years, and be a Safai Karamchari or dependent of a Safai Karamchari. The applicant should have secured admission to a recognized full-time professional or technical course in India or abroad.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Admission Letter, Fee Structure, Educational Qualification Certificates, Safai Karamchari Certificate, Passport and Visa Details (for overseas studies), Bank Account Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the HSFDC Portal or Antyodaya SARAL Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Education loan up to Rs.10,00,000 for studies in India and up to Rs.20,00,000 for studies abroad.

scheme_name: Term Loan Scheme - Credit Line 2 for Minority Community
implementing_agency: Haryana Backward Classes and Economically Weaker Sections Kalyan Nigam
tags: Minority Community, Term Loan, Self Employment, Entrepreneurship
brief_description: The scheme provides concessional term loans to eligible persons from minority communities for self-employment and income-generating activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana, belong to a notified minority community, be between 18 and 55 years of age, have annual family income between Rs.98,001 and Rs.8,00,000 in rural areas or between Rs.1,20,001 and Rs.8,00,000 in urban areas, and possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Haryana Domicile Certificate, Minority Community Certificate, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), PAN Card, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal by registering, selecting the scheme, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Term loan up to Rs.30,00,000 at concessional interest rates for eligible self-employment and income-generating activities.

implementing_agency: Haryana Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (HSFDC) in collaboration with National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC)
tags: Education Loan, Scheduled Caste, Student, Higher Education, Professional Courses
brief_description: The scheme provides education loans to Scheduled Caste students pursuing recognized professional and technical courses in India and abroad.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana, belong to the Scheduled Caste community, be between 18 and 45 years of age, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.3,00,000, and be admitted to a recognized full-time professional or technical course in India or abroad.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Scheduled Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), Residence Proof, Admission Letter, Fee Structure, Educational Certificates, Passport and Visa Details (for overseas studies), Bank Account Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the HSFDC Portal or Antyodaya SARAL Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Education loan up to Rs.10,00,000 for studies in India and up to Rs.30,00,000 for studies abroad.

scheme_name: Sikkim Grant of Scholarship to Students with Disabilities Scheme
implementing_agency: Social Justice, Empowerment and Welfare Department, Government of Sikkim
tags: Disability, Scholarship, Education, Student, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides scholarship assistance to students with disabilities for pursuing academic, technical, and professional education.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be at least 5 years of age, have a disability of 40% or more certified by a competent medical authority, be enrolled as a regular student in a recognized educational institution, and should not be receiving a similar scholarship from any other source.
documents_required: Passport-size Photographs, Sikkim Subject Certificate or Identification Certificate, Income Certificate or BPL Certificate, Birth Certificate, Educational Certificates and Marksheets, Life Certificate, and Disability Certificate.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application along with supporting documents to the Social Welfare Division for scrutiny and approval.
benefits: Financial assistance for academic, technical, and professional education, including support for books, stationery, uniforms, and other educational expenses.

scheme_name: Micro Finance Scheme - Credit Line 2 for Minority Community
implementing_agency: Haryana Backward Classes and Economically Weaker Sections Kalyan Nigam
target_beneficiaries: Minority Community Individuals and Self Help Groups
tags: Micro Finance, Minority Community, SHG, Self Employment, Loan
brief_description: The scheme provides microfinance assistance to eligible minority community members and Self Help Groups for self-employment and income-generating activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana, belong to a notified minority community, be between 18 and 55 years of age, have annual family income between Rs.98,001 and Rs.8,00,000 in rural areas or between Rs.1,20,001 and Rs.8,00,000 in urban areas, and possess a valid Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Haryana Domicile Certificate, Minority Community Certificate, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), PAN Card, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Microfinance loan assistance up to Rs.1,50,000 per beneficiary through Self Help Groups or direct financing.

scheme_name: Saraswati Vidya Yojana: Free Distribution of Bicycles
implementing_agency: Directorate of Education, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
tags: Bicycle, Girls Education, Student, School Education, Women Empowerment
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides free bicycles to eligible girl students studying in Government and Government-aided schools to encourage continuation of education and reduce dropout rates.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a girl student studying in Class VIII in a Government or Government-aided school in Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu.
documents_required: Student Identity Proof and any other documents as prescribed by the school or education department.
application_process: Schools submit the enrollment details of eligible girl students to the District Education Office. Students are not required to submit separate applications.
benefits: Free distribution of bicycles to eligible girl students.

scheme_name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) for Thrust Sectors: Scheme for Vaccine Manufacturing Units
implementing_agency: Department of Industries, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
tags: Vaccine Manufacturing, Industry, MSME, Subsidy, Entrepreneurship
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides interest subsidy and capital investment subsidy to eligible vaccine manufacturing units established in the Union Territory.
eligibility_criteria: New or existing manufacturing units undertaking expansion or diversification and commencing commercial production between 20 May 2022 and 19 May 2027 in Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu are eligible.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, PAN, GST Registration, CIN Documents, Land or Lease Documents, Electricity Connection Documents, Factory Licence, Consent to Operate Certificate, Chartered Accountant Certified Bills, Undertaking, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Single Window Portal by registering, completing the Common Application Form, uploading supporting documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Credit-linked interest subsidy of 70% per annum subject to a maximum of Rs.60,00,000 per year for up to 5 years and capital investment subsidy of 15% of eligible investment subject to scheme limits.

implementing_agency: Haryana Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (HSFDC)
tags: Poultry Farming, Scheduled Caste, Self Employment, Loan, Subsidy, BPL
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste beneficiaries from BPL families for establishing poultry farming units and other income-generating self-employment activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana, belong to the Scheduled Caste category, belong to a BPL family, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.1,80,000, and be listed in the BPL survey records.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Ration Card, Identity Proof, Scheduled Caste Certificate, BPL Certificate or BPL Survey Proof, Income Certificate, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the HSFDC Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Project cost up to Rs.1,50,000. Subsidy of 50% of the project cost subject to a maximum of Rs.10,000. Remaining project cost is financed through bank loan and margin money assistance.

implementing_agency: Haryana Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (HSFDC)
tags: Professional Services, Self Employment, Scheduled Caste, Loan, Subsidy, BPL
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste beneficiaries from BPL families for establishing professional practices and self-employment ventures.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana, belong to the Scheduled Caste category, belong to a BPL family, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.1,80,000, and be listed in the BPL survey records.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Ration Card, Identity Proof, Scheduled Caste Certificate, BPL Certificate or BPL Survey Proof, Income Certificate, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the HSFDC Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Project cost up to Rs.1,50,000. Subsidy of 50% of the project cost subject to a maximum of Rs.10,000. Margin money assistance of 10% of project cost and bank loan support for the remaining amount.

scheme_name: Additional Pension to Widow of Ex-Serviceman Having Children
implementing_agency: Sainik and Ardh Sainik Welfare Department, Government of Haryana
tags: Widow Pension, Ex-Serviceman, Children, Financial Assistance, Sainik Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides additional pension assistance to widows of ex-servicemen who died while in service but not in battle, for the support of their children.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the widow of an ex-serviceman who died while in service but not in battle and should be a resident of Haryana. Benefits are available for up to three children until they attain 20 years of age, get married, secure employment, or die, whichever is earlier.
documents_required: Ex-Serviceman or Widow Identity Card, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), Discharge Book, Haryana Domicile Certificate, Aadhaar Card, PPO, Bank Passbook, Affidavit, Identity Proof, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading the required affidavit and supporting documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Additional pension of Rs.100 per child per month for Other Ranks (maximum Rs.300), Rs.150 per child per month for JCOs (maximum Rs.450), and Rs.200 per child per month for Officers (maximum Rs.600).

scheme_name: Sanctioning of Touch Phone to Hearing and Speech Impaired Persons
implementing_agency: Andhra Pradesh Differently Abled and Senior Citizens Assistance Corporation
tags: Disability, Hearing Impaired, Speech Impaired, Assistive Device, Touch Phone
brief_description: The scheme provides touch phones to eligible hearing and speech impaired persons to improve communication and accessibility.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be 18 years or above, be hearing and speech impaired, know sign language, be capable of using video relay services, and be a student, self-employed person, or unemployed person.
documents_required: Self Declaration, Income Certificate, SADAREM Disability Certificate, Aadhaar Card, and Mobile Number.
application_process: Apply online through the Andhra Pradesh Differently Abled and Senior Citizens Assistance Corporation Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Free or subsidized touch phones based on family income criteria. Full cost assistance for beneficiaries with family income up to Rs.15,000 per month and partial assistance for higher income categories as per scheme norms.

implementing_agency: Haryana Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (HSFDC)
tags: Industrial Sector, Self Employment, Scheduled Caste, Loan, Subsidy, Entrepreneurship
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste beneficiaries from BPL families for establishing industrial and manufacturing-based self-employment ventures.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana, belong to the Scheduled Caste category, belong to a BPL family, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.1,80,000, and be listed in the BPL survey records.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Ration Card, Identity Proof, Scheduled Caste Certificate, BPL Certificate or BPL Survey Proof, Income Certificate, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the HSFDC Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Project cost up to Rs.1,50,000. Subsidy of 50% of the project cost subject to a maximum of Rs.10,000. Margin money assistance of 10% of project cost and bank loan support for the remaining amount.

scheme_name: The West Bengal Incentive Scheme: Reimbursement of Stamp Duty and Registration Fee
implementing_agency: Department of Tourism, Government of West Bengal
tags: Tourism, Entrepreneurship, Stamp Duty, Registration Fee, Reimbursement, Incentive
brief_description: The scheme provides reimbursement of stamp duty and registration fees paid for acquisition of land, buildings, or property for approved tourism projects in West Bengal.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible tourism projects, tourism units, and expansion projects established on or after 22 February 2021 are eligible. Hotel units that commenced commercial operations between 7 January 2015 and 31 December 2020 and could not avail benefits under the previous scheme due to the absence of a bar facility are also eligible. The unit must possess Registration, Date of Commencement, and Eligibility Certificates issued by the competent authority.
documents_required: Project Report, Memorandum and Articles of Association or Partnership Deed, Land Documents, Trade Licence, GST Registration Certificate, Fire Safety Certificate, Pollution Control Board Clearance, Registration Certificate, Eligibility Certificate, Proof of Payment of Stamp Duty and Registration Fee, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Shilpasathi Portal by registering, creating a Common Application Form (CAF), uploading required documents, obtaining the necessary certificates, and submitting the incentive application.
benefits: Reimbursement of stamp duty and registration fees: 50% for Group A and B areas, 60% for Group C and D areas, and 70% for Special Areas.

implementing_agency: Sikkim Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Government of Sikkim
tags: Construction Worker, Marriage Assistance, Women, Labour Welfare, Financial Assistance
category: Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women & Child Development
brief_description: The scheme provides marriage assistance to registered women construction workers and their immediate dependents.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered woman beneficiary of the Sikkim Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, be a resident of Sikkim, be between 18 and 60 years of age, and have worked for at least 90 days in a year.
documents_required: Photographs, Proof of Age, Proof of Address, Employer Certificate, Identity Card, Marriage Certificate, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Registered beneficiaries should submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the District Labour Office or authorized welfare board official.
benefits: Marriage assistance of Rs.10,000.

scheme_name: Assistance to Voluntary Organizations Working for Welfare of OBCs
implementing_agency: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
tags: OBC, NGO, Skill Development, Employment, Welfare, Capacity Building
category: Education & Learning, Social Welfare & Empowerment, Skills & Employment
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible voluntary organizations for skill development, educational, and socio-economic empowerment programs benefiting Other Backward Classes (OBCs).
eligibility_criteria: The organization should be registered under the Societies Registration Act, Trust Act, or other relevant laws, have been operational for at least three years, maintain a bank account in its name, possess appropriate administrative structures, and have experience in welfare or skill development activities. Beneficiaries should belong to OBC communities and satisfy the prescribed income criteria.
documents_required: NGO PAN Card, Utilization Certificate, Audited Accounts, Budget Estimates, Annual Performance Report, Registration Certificate, Bank Account Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Register on NGO DARPAN, obtain a Unique ID, register on the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment Grants Portal, submit the online proposal with required documents, and complete the application process through the portal.
benefits: Financial assistance of up to 90% of approved project costs for eligible welfare and skill development projects. Assistance is released in installments subject to utilization and reporting requirements.

scheme_name: H.P. Swaran Jayanti Merit Scholarship Scheme
implementing_agency: Department of Elementary Education, Government of Himachal Pradesh
tags: Scholarship, Merit, School Education, Students, Himachal Pradesh
brief_description: The scheme identifies and supports meritorious students studying in Class V of Government schools through a state-level scholarship examination.
eligibility_criteria: Students studying as regular Class V students in Government schools of Himachal Pradesh are eligible. Students must continue their education in Government schools, maintain at least 75% attendance, and satisfy other conditions prescribed by the department.
documents_required: School Identity Card, Educational Records, Passport-size Photograph, Disability Certificate (if applicable), Caste Certificate (if applicable), and other documents as required by the school.
application_process: Eligible students should obtain the application form through their school, submit the completed form with required documents, appear in the state-level examination conducted by SCERT, and await result declaration.
benefits: Scholarship of Rs.4,000 per year in Class VI, Rs.5,000 per year in Class VII, and Rs.6,000 per year in Class VIII for selected students.

scheme_name: Ex-Gratia Grant for Disability to Defence Forces Personnel
implementing_agency: Sainik and Ardh Sainik Welfare Department, Government of Haryana
tags: Defence Personnel, Disability, Ex-Gratia, Sainik Welfare, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides ex-gratia financial assistance to defence personnel from Haryana who are permanently disabled and discharged from service due to disability arising from war, operational duties, terrorist activities, or natural calamities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana, belong to the Army, Navy, or Air Force, have suffered permanent disability attributable to war, operational duties, terrorist activities, or natural calamities, and have been discharged from service due to such disability.
documents_required: Battle Casualty Report or Service Record, Disability Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Haryana Domicile Certificate, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), Bank Passbook, Identity Proof, Affidavit, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Ex-gratia grant of Rs.35,00,000 for disability of 75% and above, Rs.25,00,000 for disability between 50% and 74%, and Rs.15,00,000 for disability between 25% and 49%.

scheme_name: MSME Skill Training and Employment Scheme
implementing_agency: Directorate of Industries and Commerce, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: MSME, Skill Development, Training, Employment, Stipend
brief_description: The scheme supports MSMEs in providing industry-oriented skill training to youth and helps meet the skilled manpower requirements of MSME units.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an MSME unit in Tamil Nadu with adequate training infrastructure and expertise in the proposed trade. Trainees should be between 18 and 25 years of age and possess qualifications such as Engineering Degree, Polytechnic Diploma, ITI Certificate, or other qualifications prescribed by the relevant Sector Skill Council.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate or UAM Acknowledgement, Details of Training Infrastructure and Equipment, Self-Declaration by Trainees, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the MSME Tamil Nadu Portal by registering the MSME unit, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Training subsidy of Rs.2,000 per trainee per month for a maximum period of 6 months. Successful trainees receive skill certification through the appropriate assessment and certification agency.

implementing_agency: Anusandhan National Research Foundation (formerly Science and Engineering Research Board), Government of India
target_beneficiaries: Indian Scientists and Engineers Working Abroad
tags: Research, Fellowship, Scientist, Engineering, Innovation, Higher Education
brief_description: The fellowship supports outstanding Indian scientists and engineers working abroad who wish to return to India and pursue research in Indian scientific institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian scientist or engineer working abroad, below 40 years of age, possess a Ph.D. or equivalent qualification, have an outstanding research record, and should not be holding any permanent, tenure-track, or contractual position in an Indian institution at the time of nomination.
documents_required: Photograph, Proof of Age, Educational Qualification Documents, Publication Details, Patent Details (if any), Nomination Letter, Curriculum Vitae, Plagiarism Undertaking, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: The candidate should obtain sponsorship from a host institution in India and submit the nomination through the SERB Online Portal. Applications are reviewed by a Search-cum-Selection Committee on a rolling basis.
benefits: Fellowship of Rs.1,35,000 per month, research grant of Rs.7,00,000 per year, and overhead support of Rs.60,000 per year for a period of five years.

scheme_name: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme
implementing_agency: Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Health Insurance, Cashless Treatment, Hospitalization, Healthcare, Financial Protection
brief_description: The scheme provides cashless medical treatment for eligible families through empanelled government and private hospitals across Tamil Nadu.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Tamil Nadu, have a family annual income not exceeding Rs.1,20,000, and have their name included in the family ration card. Eligible family members include spouse, children, and dependent parents.
documents_required: Family Ration Card, Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate issued by the competent authority, Self-Declaration by the Head of Family, and other documents as prescribed.
application_process: Eligible beneficiaries may enroll through designated enrollment centres or avail treatment directly at empanelled hospitals after verification of eligibility documents.
benefits: Cashless health insurance coverage of up to Rs.5,00,000 per family per year on a floater basis for approved medical treatments and procedures.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Marketing and Inspection, Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India
target_beneficiaries: Farmers, FPOs, Cooperatives, Agri-Entrepreneurs, and Other Eligible Promoters
tags: Agriculture, Marketing Infrastructure, Storage Infrastructure, Farmer, FPO, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme provides credit-linked capital subsidy for creation and modernization of agricultural marketing infrastructure, including storage, grading, processing, and rural market facilities.
eligibility_criteria: Individuals, Farmers, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), Cooperatives, Companies, NGOs, SHGs, Panchayats, State Agencies, and other eligible entities undertaking approved agricultural marketing infrastructure projects are eligible.
documents_required: Project Report, Loan Sanction Letter, Land Documents, Approved Building Plans, Financial Institution Certificates, Category Certificate (if applicable), Affidavit, Registration Documents, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: The promoter should obtain a term loan from a financial institution. The financing institution submits subsidy claims through the NABARD ENSURE Portal. Subsidy is released after project completion and inspection.
benefits: Credit-linked capital subsidy for eligible agricultural marketing infrastructure projects. Subsidy rates vary based on beneficiary category and project location as prescribed under scheme guidelines.

implementing_agency: Revenue Department, Government of Andhra Pradesh
tags: Housing, House Site Pattas, BPL, Shelter, Rural Housing, Urban Housing
brief_description: The scheme provides house site pattas to eligible homeless families in rural and urban areas of Andhra Pradesh to facilitate construction of permanent houses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should not own a house or house site anywhere in Andhra Pradesh, should not have benefited from any previous government housing scheme, and should satisfy the prescribed landholding and income criteria. Rural beneficiaries should belong to identified BPL households, while urban beneficiaries should have annual household income not exceeding Rs.3,00,000.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, Income Certificate, Residence Proof, Landholding Declaration, Family Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Eligible beneficiaries are identified and verified by local revenue authorities. House site pattas are issued through the Revenue Department after completion of verification and approval procedures.
benefits: Free allotment of house site pattas for construction of houses under the state housing program.

scheme_name: National Scholarship for Post Graduate Studies
implementing_agency: University Grants Commission (UGC), Ministry of Education, Government of India
tags: Scholarship, Higher Education, Postgraduate Studies, Financial Assistance, Students
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to meritorious students pursuing their first postgraduate degree in recognized institutions in India.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be pursuing the first postgraduate degree, enrolled in the first year of a full-time postgraduate program in a recognized institution, be below 30 years of age, and should not have already completed a postgraduate degree.
documents_required: Educational Certificates and Marksheets, Admission Letter or Proof of Admission to the Postgraduate Course, Identity Proof, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photographs, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Scholarship of Rs.15,000 per month for 10 months in a year.

implementing_agency: Department of Science and Technology, Government of India
target_beneficiaries: Students Pursuing Basic and Natural Sciences
tags: Scholarship, Science Education, Research, Higher Education, Students
brief_description: The scheme encourages meritorious students to pursue higher education and research careers in Basic and Natural Sciences through financial support.
eligibility_criteria: Students who are among the top 1% in Class XII examinations of recognized Boards and pursuing B.Sc., B.S., Integrated M.Sc., or Integrated M.S. courses in Basic and Natural Sciences are eligible. Students selected through KVPY, NTSE, JBNSTS, International Olympiads, or specified national examinations may also be eligible as per scheme guidelines.
documents_required: Class X Certificate or Marksheet, Class XII Marksheet, Endorsement Certificate from Institution, SBI Bank Account Details, Eligibility Certificate (if applicable), Performance Reports, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the INSPIRE Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application. Selected candidates must subsequently upload performance reports and other required documents for continuation of the scholarship.
benefits: Scholarship of Rs.80,000 per year, comprising Rs.60,000 annual scholarship and Rs.20,000 annual summer internship or mentorship grant.

implementing_agency: Uttarakhand Renewable Energy Development Agency (UREDA), Government of Uttarakhand
tags: Solar Energy, Self Employment, Renewable Energy, Entrepreneurship
brief_description: The scheme promotes self-employment through installation of solar power projects and supports sustainable livelihood opportunities in Uttarakhand.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Uttarakhand, between 18 and 65 years of age, possess the required land or site for the proposed project, and should not be a defaulter of any bank or financial institution.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Bank Account Details, Land Ownership Documents or Lease Documents, Passport-size Photograph, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Disability Certificate (if applicable), and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Mukhyamantri Swarojgar Yojana Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial assistance through bank-linked loans and applicable government subsidies for establishment of solar power projects under the scheme.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Raut, Charwaha Evam Doodh Duhane Wale Cycle Sahayata Yojana
implementing_agency: Labour Department, Government of Chhattisgarh
tags: Financial Assistance, Bicycle, Unorganized Workers, Labour Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered unorganized workers for the purchase of bicycles to support their livelihood activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be registered as an unorganized worker in Chhattisgarh, be between 18 and 60 years of age, and should not be receiving benefits under a similar state government scheme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Bank Account Details, Registration Card of the Unorganized Workers Welfare Board, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Registered beneficiaries should apply online through the Chhattisgarh Labour Department Portal by selecting the scheme, filling the application form, and submitting the required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.3,000 per beneficiary for the purchase of a bicycle or assistance as per the rate fixed by the competent authority.

implementing_agency: Leather Industries Development Corporation of Maharashtra (LIDCOM)
tags: Education Loan, Scheduled Caste, Higher Education, Student, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides education loans to eligible students from the Charmakar community for pursuing higher education in India and abroad.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian citizen, a permanent resident of Maharashtra, belong to the Charmakar community (including Dhor, Chambhar, Holar, Mochi and related communities), be between 18 and 50 years of age, and have annual family income not exceeding Rs.3,00,000.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Domicile Certificate, Income Certificate, Educational Qualification Certificates, Admission Letter, Fee Structure, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the concerned District Office of LIDCOM for verification and loan processing.
benefits: Education loan up to Rs.20,00,000 for postgraduate studies in India or abroad.

implementing_agency: Department of Animal Husbandry and Veterinary Services, Government of Goa
tags: Dairy Farming, Animal Husbandry, Award, Milk Production, Farmer
brief_description: The scheme recognizes and rewards dairy farmers in Goa who achieve outstanding milk production through scientific dairy farming practices.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a dairy farmer and a member of a registered Dairy Cooperative Society, Bachat Gat, or Self Help Group. The applicant should be a bonafide resident of Goa for at least five years, own a cattle shed with milch animals, have a registered dairy farm, and have supplied at least 1,00,000 litres of milk during the preceding year with an average daily supply of at least 250 litres.
documents_required: Membership Certificate from Dairy Cooperative Society, Bachat Gat, or Self Help Group, Dairy Farm Records, Residential Certificate, Proof of Dairy Farm Registration, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Department of Animal Husbandry and Veterinary Services through the concerned Area Officer.
benefits: Certificate of Appreciation, Memento, and cash award of Rs.1,00,000.

scheme_name: Scheme to Provide Special Financial Assistance for Organizing Cultural Events
implementing_agency: Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa
target_beneficiaries: Individuals, Cultural Groups, Institutions, and NGOs
tags: Cultural Events, Festival, Exhibition, Workshop, Grant, Arts
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for organizing cultural events, exhibitions, festivals, workshops, camps, and other cultural programs in Goa.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant may be an individual, cultural group, institution, or NGO with experience in organizing cultural activities. Applicants should not have any pending utilization certificates or financial irregularities relating to previous grants received from the Directorate of Art and Culture.
documents_required: Registration Certificate (if applicable), Estimated Expenditure Statement, Affidavit, Event Proposal, Previous Utilization Certificate and Audited Statements (if applicable), Bank Account Details, Aadhaar Card (for individuals), and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.5,00,000 or 80% of the approved expenditure, whichever is lower. In special cases identified by the Directorate, assistance may cover up to 100% of the approved expenditure subject to scheme limits.

scheme_name: Grant of Quality Certification Reimbursement of Charges
implementing_agency: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: MSME, Quality Certification, ISO Certification, Reimbursement, Entrepreneurship
brief_description: The scheme reimburses expenses incurred by MSMEs for obtaining national and international quality certifications and related consultancy services.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a Micro, Small, or Medium Enterprise operating in Tamil Nadu. Eligible expenses include payments made to consultancy agencies, certification agencies, and NABL-accredited testing laboratories for quality certification.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, Business Registration Documents, Electricity Service Connection Proof, Invoices and Receipts, Quality Certification Documents, NABCB Accreditation Documents, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the MSME Tamil Nadu Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application within one year from the date of certification.
benefits: Reimbursement of certification-related expenses up to Rs.2,00,000 for National Level Certifications and up to Rs.10,00,000 for International Level Certifications.

scheme_name: Disha - Early Intervention and School Readiness Scheme
implementing_agency: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
tags: Disability, Early Intervention, Child Development, Special Education, Rehabilitation
brief_description: The scheme establishes Disha Centres for early intervention, therapy, training, and school readiness support for children with disabilities covered under the National Trust Act.
eligibility_criteria: Children aged 0 to 10 years with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Intellectual Disability, or Multiple Disabilities covered under the National Trust Act, 1999 are eligible.
documents_required: Enrollment Form, Birth Certificate or Age Proof, Disability Certificate, Income Certificate or BPL Certificate (where applicable), Parent or Guardian Identity Proof, and Residence Proof.
application_process: Parents or guardians should visit a registered Disha Centre with the required documents. After assessment and verification, eligible children are enrolled and provided services through the centre.
benefits: Early intervention services, therapies, special training, school readiness support, counseling for parents, transportation support (where available), and periodic assessment and evaluation.

implementing_agency: Department of Tourism, Government of Arunachal Pradesh
tags: Tourism, Hospitality, Education, Skill Development, Sponsorship
brief_description: The scheme sponsors eligible students from Arunachal Pradesh for professional education and training in tourism, hospitality, and hotel management courses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a domicile of Arunachal Pradesh and belong to a Scheduled Tribe community. The applicant should have passed the required qualifying examination for the respective tourism or hospitality course and satisfy the merit criteria prescribed by the department.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photographs, Class X and Class XII Mark Sheets, Scheduled Tribe Certificate or Permanent Resident Certificate, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Eligible candidates should submit applications with supporting documents to the Directorate of Tourism or the concerned District Tourism Office after notification of admissions.
benefits: Sponsorship for B.Sc. Hospitality and Hotel Administration, Diploma Courses, and Post Graduate Diploma Courses in tourism and hospitality-related fields at approved institutions.

scheme_name: Scheme for Waiver of Loans to Handloom and Powerloom Sector
implementing_agency: Industries and Commerce Department, Government of Andhra Pradesh
target_beneficiaries: Weavers, Weaver Groups, and Self Help Groups
tags: Loan Waiver, Handloom, Powerloom, Weaver, SHG, Debt Relief
brief_description: The scheme provides loan waiver benefits to eligible handloom weavers, weaver groups, and powerloom units in Andhra Pradesh to reduce debt burden and improve livelihoods.
eligibility_criteria: Loans availed for weaving purposes and outstanding as on 31 March 2014 are eligible. Eligible beneficiaries include individual weavers, weaver groups, holders of Weaver Credit Cards, and powerloom units below 5 HP capacity availing the 50% power subsidy scheme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Weaver Identity Card or Weaver Credit Card, Bank Loan Documents, SHG or Group Membership Proof (if applicable), Powerloom Registration Documents (if applicable), and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Eligible beneficiaries should submit the required loan and identity documents through the concerned bank or designated government authority for verification and processing under the loan waiver scheme.
benefits: Waiver of eligible outstanding loans availed for weaving activities under the scheme guidelines.

implementing_agency: Uttar Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
target_beneficiaries: Children of Registered Construction Workers and Orphaned Children
tags: Education, Residential School, Construction Workers, Children, Free Education
brief_description: The scheme provides free residential education to children of registered construction workers and eligible orphaned children in Uttar Pradesh.
eligibility_criteria: Children aged 6 to 16 years who are orphaned, covered under the Chief Minister Child Service Scheme, or are children of registered construction workers having at least one year of membership with the Welfare Board are eligible. A maximum of two children per registered worker can avail the benefit.
documents_required: Labour Registration Card of Parent, Birth Certificate, Previous Class Marksheet, Passport-size Photograph, Medical Certificate (if required), and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Obtain the application form from the Labour Office, Tehsil Office, or Block Development Office, fill in the required details, attach supporting documents, and submit it to the concerned authority.
benefits: Free residential education, boarding, lodging, meals, healthcare facilities, sports activities, and overall development support.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Higher Education, Government of Goa
tags: Scholarship, NDA, Defence, Cadet, Higher Education
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible National Defence Academy cadets from Goa belonging to economically weaker families.
eligibility_criteria: Cadets should be born in Goa or domiciled in Goa for at least 15 years. Annual family income should not exceed Rs.1,80,000. For SC, ST, and OBC candidates, annual family income should not exceed Rs.4,50,000. The cadet should not be receiving similar financial assistance from any other source.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, NDA Identity Proof, Birth Certificate or Domicile Certificate, Income Certificate, Caste Certificate (if applicable), and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Higher Education, Government of Goa.
benefits: Scholarship of Rs.1,000 per month during the training period, subject to a maximum of 24 months or the course duration, whichever is lower, and a one-time outfit allowance of Rs.10,000.

scheme_name: National Awards for Outstanding Services in the Field of Prevention of Alcoholism and Substance (Drug) Abuse - Best Research or Innovation
implementing_agency: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
tags: Award, Drug Abuse Prevention, Alcoholism, Research, Innovation, Rehabilitation
brief_description: The award recognizes outstanding contributions in prevention of alcoholism, substance abuse, rehabilitation, research, innovation, awareness generation, and treatment services.
eligibility_criteria: Individuals and institutions working in the field of prevention, treatment, rehabilitation, research, innovation, or awareness relating to alcoholism and substance abuse are eligible. Self-nominations are not permitted. Nominations must be submitted through authorized agencies or recommending authorities.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Educational and Professional Credentials, Experience Certificates, Publications, Evidence of Contributions and Achievements, Awards and Recognitions, Registration Documents of Organizations, Audited Accounts, Annual Reports, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Authorized agencies or recommending authorities should submit nominations online through the National Awards Portal in the prescribed format along with supporting documents.
benefits: Citation, Memento, and cash award of Rs.5,00,000. Travel, boarding, and lodging expenses for awardees and one companion are borne by the Government of India.

scheme_name: National Handloom Development Programme (NHDP) - Scholarship Component
implementing_agency: Office of the Development Commissioner (Handlooms), Ministry of Textiles, Government of India
target_beneficiaries: Children of Handloom Weavers and Workers
tags: Handloom, Scholarship, Weaver, Education, Textile
brief_description: The scholarship supports children of handloom weavers and workers pursuing diploma, undergraduate, and postgraduate studies in recognized textile institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Children of handloom weavers or workers are eligible. Benefits are available for up to two children pursuing Diploma, Undergraduate, or Postgraduate courses in Central or State Government recognized and funded textile institutions.
documents_required: Weaver Identity Card, Aadhaar Card, Age Proof, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Admission Proof, Educational Certificates and Marksheets, Bank Account Details, Institution Recognition Documents, and Undertaking regarding non-receipt of similar assistance.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the concerned Weavers' Service Centre (WSC). Applications are verified by the WSC and processed through the Zonal Office for sanction and Direct Benefit Transfer.
benefits: Scholarship covering tuition fees, admission fees, examination fees, and other academic charges, along with a stipend of Rs.5,000 per month, subject to a maximum assistance of Rs.2,00,000 per child per year or actual expenses, whichever is lower.

implementing_agency: Department of Tourism, Government of Arunachal Pradesh
target_beneficiaries: Individuals, Homestay Operators, and Tourism Entrepreneurs
tags: Tourism, Homestay, Adventure Tourism, Entrepreneurship, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme promotes tourism development in Arunachal Pradesh by providing financial assistance for homestays, food courts, patisseries, and adventure tourism activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a domicile of Arunachal Pradesh. Homestay operators should be registered with the Department of Tourism, and trained homestay operators are given preference. Beneficiaries operating food courts, patisseries, cab services, or tourism-related businesses should be registered with the Department of Tourism.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Permanent Resident Certificate, Tourism Department Registration Certificate, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, Project Proposal, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Obtain the prescribed application form from the District Tourism Office, fill in the required details, attach supporting documents, and submit the application to the concerned office. Eligible applicants may be interviewed and verified before approval.
benefits: Financial assistance of up to Rs.5,00,000 for establishing patisseries, food courts, homestays, and procurement of adventure tourism equipment, subject to scheme guidelines.

scheme_name: Tea Development and Promotion Scheme (TDPS) - Plantation Development for Small Tea Growers (PDSTG): Mechanization for Individual Small Growers
implementing_agency: Tea Board India, Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India
tags: Tea, Small Tea Grower, Agriculture, Mechanization, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to small tea growers for purchasing mechanized equipment to improve tea production, productivity, and quality.
eligibility_criteria: Small tea growers, including members of Self Help Groups (SHGs), Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), and Farmer Producer Companies (FPCs), are eligible. Applicants should possess Tea Board registration or a valid Tea Board identity card.
documents_required: Tea Board Registration Certificate or Identity Card, Survey Map of Tea Area, Aadhaar Card, PAN/TAN/GST Registration (where applicable), Self-Declaration, Bank Account Details, Cancelled Cheque, and SHG/FPO/FPC Resolution Documents (if applicable).
application_process: Register on the Tea Board online portal, complete the application form, upload required documents, pay the prescribed application fee (if applicable), and submit the application online.
benefits: Financial assistance for approved mechanization equipment including pruning machines, mechanical harvesters, power sprayers, brush cutters, and plucking shears, subject to equipment-wise ceiling limits prescribed under the scheme.

scheme_name: Long Term Fellowship under the Human Resource Development Programme for Health Research
implementing_agency: Department of Health Research, Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India
tags: Health Research, Fellowship, Medical Research, Training, Stipend
brief_description: The fellowship supports advanced training in India and abroad for medical and health research professionals in priority areas of health research.
