eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian citizen working in an Indian institution, possess qualifications such as MD, MS, MDS, MVSc, M.Sc., M.Pharma, M.Tech, or equivalent with PhD where applicable, have at least three years of regular service experience, and be below 45 years of age.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Passport-size Photograph, Academic Qualification Certificates, Age Proof, Mentor Consent Letter, Institute Recommendation Certificate, Research Proposal, Mandate Form, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Department of Health Research e-PMS Portal by registering, completing the profile, selecting the appropriate fellowship scheme, uploading required documents, and submitting the proposal online.
benefits: For training in India, fellowship stipend up to Rs.40,000 per month and contingency grant up to Rs.30,000. For training abroad, fellowship stipend of up to USD 3,000 per month along with travel, contingency, conference, and insurance support as per scheme norms.

scheme_name: Scheme for Welfare of Children in Need of Care and Protection
implementing_agency: Directorate of Women and Child Development, Government of Goa
tags: Children, Orphan, Destitute, Shelter, Child Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered voluntary organizations caring for orphaned, destitute, and vulnerable children up to 18 years of age.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant organization should be a registered voluntary organization in Goa. Beneficiary children should be orphaned, destitute, from single-parent families lacking adequate care, or belong to families facing severe socio-economic hardship.
documents_required: Registration Certificate of the Organization, Details of Beneficiary Children, Bank Account Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the Directorate of Women and Child Development, Government of Goa.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.400 per child per month for children housed in the organization's own premises and Rs.500 per child per month for children housed in rented premises.

implementing_agency: Department of Social Welfare, Government of Uttar Pradesh
tags: Marriage Assistance, Mass Marriage, Financial Assistance, Social Welfare
brief_description: The scheme promotes social harmony and supports economically weaker families by organizing mass marriage ceremonies and providing financial assistance to eligible couples.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Uttar Pradesh. Annual family income should not exceed Rs.2,00,000. The bride should be at least 18 years of age and the groom should be at least 21 years of age. The scheme is open to all communities.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Income Certificate, Age Proof of Bride and Groom, Bank Account Details of the Bride, Passport-size Photographs, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply through the District Social Welfare Office or designated online portal by submitting the prescribed application form along with required documents before the scheduled mass marriage event.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.51,000 per eligible couple, including Rs.35,000 deposited into the bride's bank account, marriage-related materials worth Rs.10,000, and Rs.6,000 towards organization of the marriage ceremony.

scheme_name: Theka Shramik, Hamal Shramik Evam Gharelu Mahila Kamgar Gambhir Bimari Chikitsa Sahayata Yojana
implementing_agency: Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board, Labour Department, Government of Chhattisgarh
tags: Health Assistance, Medical Aid, Unorganized Workers, Labour Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for treatment of serious illnesses to registered contract workers, hamal workers, and domestic women workers in Chhattisgarh.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be registered with the Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board for at least 90 days, be between 18 and 60 years of age, and should not be receiving similar benefits under any other state government scheme.
documents_required: Application Form, Aadhaar Card, Labour Registration Card, Voter ID Card, PAN Card, Medical Documents, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Registered workers may apply online through the Chhattisgarh Labour Department Portal by selecting the scheme, filling the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application. Unregistered workers must first register with the Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board.
benefits: Financial assistance of up to Rs.50,000 for treatment of serious illnesses such as cancer, kidney disease, heart disease, sickle cell anemia, AIDS, and paralysis.

implementing_agency: Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board, Labour Department, Government of Chhattisgarh
target_beneficiaries: Sanitation Workers and Their Family Members
tags: Skill Development, Training, Sanitation Workers, Employment
brief_description: The scheme provides skill development training to sanitation workers and their family members to improve employability and livelihood opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered sanitation worker under the Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board, be above 18 years of age, and should not be availing similar benefits under another state government scheme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Labour Registration Card, Residence Certificate, Ration Card, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Eligible applicants may apply online through the Chhattisgarh Labour Department Portal after registration with the Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board.
benefits: Free skill development training in trades identified by the State Technical Education Department from time to time.

scheme_name: Agriculture and Processed Foods Export Promotion Scheme of APEDA (2021-26)
implementing_agency: Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA)
target_beneficiaries: APEDA Registered Exporters, FPOs, SHGs, Cooperatives, Government Agencies, and Institutions
tags: APEDA, Export Promotion, Agriculture Export, Infrastructure, Quality Development, Market Development
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for export infrastructure development, quality certification, market development, and value chain strengthening of agricultural and processed food products.
eligibility_criteria: APEDA registered exporters, FPOs, SHGs, cooperatives, government agencies, state agencies, institutions, and other eligible organizations engaged in APEDA scheduled products are eligible as per scheme guidelines.
documents_required: Detailed Project Report (DPR), Registration Certificate, Audited Financial Statements, Land Documents, Bank Appraisal Report (if applicable), Quotations for Equipment, Statutory Approvals, APEDA Registration Documents, and other documents prescribed in the scheme guidelines.
application_process: Submit an online application through the APEDA Financial Assistance Scheme Portal along with the prescribed documents. Applications are examined by APEDA and assistance is released after approval and completion of eligible activities.
benefits: Financial assistance for export infrastructure, quality development, certification, traceability systems, market development activities, feasibility studies, branding, and export promotion initiatives. Assistance rates generally range from 40% to 90% of eligible project cost depending on the component and beneficiary category.

implementing_agency: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India
tags: Natural Farming, Organic Practices, Sustainable Agriculture, Chemical-Free Farming
brief_description: The mission promotes natural farming practices across India to reduce dependence on external inputs, lower cultivation costs, improve soil health, and enhance farmers' incomes.
eligibility_criteria: All farmers in India are eligible to participate in activities supported under the National Mission on Natural Farming.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Land Records, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, Caste Certificate (if applicable), and other documents prescribed by the concerned State Agriculture Department.
application_process: Interested farmers may apply through the concerned State Agriculture Department, District Agriculture Office, or designated implementing agency as per state-specific implementation procedures.
benefits: Support for capacity building, farmer training, demonstrations, certification, awareness programs, and promotion of natural farming practices through assistance provided under the mission guidelines.

implementing_agency: Kerala Biotechnology Commission, Kerala State Council for Science, Technology and Environment (KSCSTE)
tags: Biotechnology, Post-Doctoral Fellowship, Research, PhD, Stipend
brief_description: The fellowship supports young researchers with PhD qualifications in biotechnology-related disciplines to undertake independent research under expert mentorship in reputed institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a native of Kerala, possess a first-class Master's degree in a relevant discipline and a PhD in biotechnology-related fields, be below 35 years of age (relaxable for eligible categories), and should not be receiving any other fellowship or financial assistance.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Age Proof, PhD Certificate, Educational Certificates and Marksheets, Nativity Certificate, Research Proposal, Publications, Mentor Consent Letter, Host Institution Endorsement, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Applications are invited online twice a year. Eligible candidates should submit the application along with the research proposal and supporting documents through the Kerala Biotechnology Commission as per the notification.
benefits: Fellowship of Rs.32,000 per month plus HRA and annual contingency grant of Rs.50,000. Candidates who have submitted their PhD thesis and are awaiting award of the degree receive Rs.22,000 per month until award of the PhD.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa
tags: Award, Culture, Literature, Education, Sports, Social Service
brief_description: The award honors distinguished personalities of Goan origin who have made exceptional contributions in fields such as art, culture, literature, education, sports, science, public service, and other areas of national or international significance.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be of Goan origin and have demonstrated distinguished and exceptional achievements or services in fields such as art, culture, literature, education, sports, medicine, social work, civil services, public affairs, trade, science, engineering, industry, economics, philosophy, politics, or other recognized fields. The award is generally not conferred posthumously.
documents_required: Identity Proof of Recommender, Biodata of Nominee, Passport-size Photograph of Nominee, and supporting documents relating to achievements and contributions.
application_process: Submit the prescribed nomination form along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa, within the notified application period.
benefits: Memento, Citation, and cash award of Rs.5,00,000.

implementing_agency: Department of Information Technology, Electronics and Communications, Government of Goa
tags: IT Sector, Capital Investment, Subsidy, Startup, Business Growth
brief_description: The scheme provides capital investment subsidy to eligible Information Technology units operating in Goa to encourage investment, expansion, and employment generation.
eligibility_criteria: New and existing IT units operating in Goa are eligible. Applicants may be proprietorships, partnership firms, LLPs, private limited companies, or public limited companies. The unit must satisfy the conditions prescribed under the Goa Information Technology Policy.
documents_required: Incorporation Certificate, Registration Certificate, PAN, Aadhaar, GST Registration, Memorandum and Articles of Association or Partnership Documents, Occupancy Certificate, Business Plan or DPR, Employee Details, Investment Proofs, Chartered Accountant Certificate, and Udyam Registration (if applicable).
application_process: Apply online through the Goa Online Portal by registering, selecting the relevant IT scheme, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Capital investment subsidy of up to 20% of eligible capital investment. Additional subsidy may be available for Mega Units and units operating in identified focus sectors, subject to scheme limits.

scheme_name: Subsidy for Asset Creation for Intellectual Property: Trade Mark and Geographical Indications Registration
implementing_agency: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: MSME, Intellectual Property, Trademark, Geographical Indication, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme encourages MSMEs to create and protect intellectual property by reimbursing expenses incurred for trademark registration and geographical indication registration in India or abroad.
eligibility_criteria: New and existing Micro, Small, and Medium Manufacturing Enterprises, including clusters located in Tamil Nadu, are eligible.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, Trademark Registration Documents, Geographical Indication Registration Documents, Expenditure Statements, Bills and Receipts, Bank Transaction Proofs, Loan Sanction Letter (if applicable), Invoices, and Advance Stamped Receipt.
application_process: Apply online through the MSME Tamil Nadu Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading supporting documents, and submitting the application within the prescribed time limit.
benefits: 50% subsidy on the cost of obtaining Trade Mark Registration or Geographical Indication Registration, subject to a maximum of Rs.25,000 per registration.

scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
implementing_agency: Backward Classes and Minorities Welfare Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
tags: Scholarship, OBC, Higher Education, Student Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Other Backward Class (OBC) students pursuing post-matric education to help them continue higher studies.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh, belong to the OBC category, have passed Class 10, and have annual family income not exceeding Rs.3,00,000.
documents_required: Samagra ID, College Code, Income Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Class 10 Marksheet, Digital Caste Certificate, Residence Proof, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Madhya Pradesh Scholarship Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Scholarship assistance covering tuition fees and other admissible educational expenses as per government norms. Eligible students may receive up to 100% scholarship support depending on course and income criteria.

implementing_agency: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy, Government of India
tags: Internship, Renewable Energy, Research, Students, Skill Development
brief_description: The scheme provides internship opportunities to students and research scholars to gain exposure to renewable energy policies, programs, research, and implementation activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be pursuing undergraduate, graduate, or postgraduate studies in Engineering, Science, Management, Law, Renewable Energy, or related disciplines, or be working in renewable energy related research institutions or organizations.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Educational Certificates and Marksheets, Recommendation Letter from Institution, No Objection Certificate, Identity Proof, Age Proof, Student Identity Card or Enrollment Proof, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the MNRE Internship Portal by filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application before the prescribed deadline.
benefits: Stipend of Rs.15,000 per month for physical internships. Internship completion certificate is issued upon successful completion. Virtual interns are not eligible for stipend.

scheme_name: Moovalur Ramamirtham Ammaiyar Higher Education Assurance Scheme (Pudhumai Penn Scheme)
implementing_agency: Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Education, Financial Assistance, Girls, Higher Education, Scholarship
brief_description: The scheme encourages girl students from Government schools to pursue higher education by providing monthly financial assistance for undergraduate, diploma, ITI, and other recognized courses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Tamil Nadu, be a female student, and have studied from Classes 6 to 12 in Government schools of Tamil Nadu. The student should be pursuing her first higher education course in a recognized institution, college, university, diploma, or ITI program.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Passport-size Photograph, Residence Certificate, Educational Certificates, Bank Account Details, and Income Certificate (if applicable).
application_process: Apply online through the Penkalvi Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.1,000 per month until completion of undergraduate degree, diploma, ITI, or other recognized higher education courses. The assistance is provided in addition to other eligible scholarships.

implementing_agency: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: MSME, Electricity, Power Tariff, Subsidy, Manufacturing
brief_description: The scheme provides power tariff subsidy to eligible MSME manufacturing enterprises to reduce electricity costs and improve competitiveness.
eligibility_criteria: New Micro manufacturing enterprises established anywhere in Tamil Nadu, new Small and Medium manufacturing enterprises established in notified industrially backward blocks, eligible agro-based industries, and existing enterprises undertaking substantial expansion or diversification are eligible. Only enterprises using Low Tension Power Supply (Tariff-B) are eligible.
documents_required: Electricity Board Meter Card, Udyam Registration Certificate, Electricity Bills and Payment Receipts, Eligibility Certificate, Advance Stamped Receipt, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the MSME Tamil Nadu Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application within the prescribed timeline.
benefits: Power tariff subsidy of 20% for 36 months from the date of commencement of commercial production or the date of obtaining power connection, whichever is later.

scheme_name: Mahila Kamgar Kaushal Unnayan Evam Theka Shramik, Hamal Tatha Gharelu Mahila Kamgar Parivar Sashaktikaran Yojana
implementing_agency: Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board, Labour Department, Government of Chhattisgarh
target_beneficiaries: Women Workers, Contract Workers, Hamals and Domestic Workers
tags: Skill Development, Women Empowerment, Training, Labour Welfare, Entrepreneurship
brief_description: The scheme provides skill development training and family empowerment support to women workers, contract workers, hamals, and domestic workers registered with the welfare board.
eligibility_criteria: Female domestic workers, contract workers, and hamals registered with the Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board are eligible. Applicant should be above 18 years of age and should not be receiving similar benefits under another state government scheme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Labour Registration Card, Educational Qualification Documents, Occupation Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Registered beneficiaries may apply online through the Chhattisgarh Labour Department Portal. Unregistered applicants must first register with the Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board before applying for the scheme.
benefits: Free skill development training in trades such as beautician, nutrition, tailoring, hospitality, nursing, cooking, household product preparation, and other notified trades, along with family empowerment and entrepreneurship support.

implementing_agency: Ministry of Minority Affairs, Government of India
tags: Education Loan, Overseas Education, Minority Students, Interest Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme provides interest subsidy on educational loans for overseas higher education to students belonging to notified minority communities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to a notified minority community, be admitted to a recognized Master's, M.Phil., or Ph.D. program abroad, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.6,00,000, and have obtained an education loan from a scheduled bank under the Indian Banks' Association Education Loan Scheme.
documents_required: Application Form, Admission Letter, Passport, Income Certificate, Education Loan Documents, Aadhaar Card, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the designated Padho Pardesh Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Interest subsidy on education loans during the moratorium period for eligible students pursuing higher education abroad.

scheme_name: Immediate Death Relief Assistance under Welfare and Relief for Fishermen During Lean Seasons and Natural Calamities Scheme
implementing_agency: Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare, Government of Puducherry
tags: Fishermen Welfare, Death Relief, Financial Assistance, Family Support
category: Agriculture, Rural & Environment, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: The scheme provides immediate financial assistance to the family of a deceased fisherman to compensate for the loss of livelihood and support the family during distress.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the legal heir of a deceased fisherman who was a resident of Puducherry, aged between 18 and 60 years, enrolled in a Fishermen or Fisherwomen Cooperative Society, and who died while engaged in fishing activities.
documents_required: Photograph of Legal Heir, Residence Certificate of Deceased Fisherman, Age Proof, Declaration regarding non-receipt of Old Age Pension, Cooperative Society Membership Certificate, No Claim Certificate, Electoral Identity Card, Ration Card, Legal Heir Certificate, Affidavit, and Death Certificate.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare within 30 days of the death of the fisherman.
benefits: Immediate death relief assistance of Rs.1,00,000 to the legal heir or family of the deceased fisherman.

scheme_name: Subsidy for the Purchase of Wooden/Fibre Reinforced Plastic/Steel Mechanised Boats
implementing_agency: Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare, Government of Puducherry
tags: Fisheries, Mechanised Boat, Subsidy, Fishermen, Marine Fishing
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for the purchase, modernization, and conversion of mechanised fishing boats to promote marine fisheries development in Puducherry.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the fishing community or be engaged in fishing or fish trade on a full-time basis. The applicant must be a resident of Puducherry for at least 5 years, be a member of a Fishermen Cooperative Society, be between 23 and 60 years of age, and should not be employed in Government service or receiving old age pension.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, EPIC/Voter ID, Ration Card, Birth Certificate, Residence Certificate, Fishermen Cooperative Society Membership Certificate, Fishing Experience Certificate, Declaration regarding non-receipt of Old Age Pension, Declaration regarding non-employment in Government service, Loan Sanction Letter, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Obtain the prescribed application form from the Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare, fill in the required details, attach supporting documents, and submit the application to the concerned office.
benefits: 50% subsidy up to Rs.7,50,000 for mechanised boats above 47 feet, 50% subsidy up to Rs.11,00,000 for steel mechanised boats of 45–54 feet, 50% subsidy up to Rs.2,50,000 for boat modernization, and 50% subsidy up to Rs.5,00,000 for tuna long-lining modernization projects.

scheme_name: Grant of Marriage Allowance to the Widow's Daughter
implementing_agency: Department of Women and Child Development, Government of Puducherry
tags: Marriage Assistance, Widow, Daughter, Financial Assistance, Social Welfare
category: Women & Child Development, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to widows for the marriage of their daughters.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a widow with annual income not exceeding Rs.75,000, be a resident of Puducherry, and the benefit is available only for the first marriage of one daughter. The bride must be at least 18 years old and the groom at least 21 years old.
documents_required: Residence Certificate, Income Certificate, Marriage Invitation, Birth Certificate or Transfer Certificate of Bride and Groom, Death Certificate of Bride's Father, Marriage Registration Certificate, Aadhaar Cards, Caste Certificate, Ration Card, Passport-size Photograph, and Voter ID Card.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form with required documents to the Department of Women and Child Development at least 30 days before the proposed marriage date.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.25,000 for marriage expenses.

scheme_name: Free Coaching Scheme for SC and OBC Students
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
tags: Coaching, Competitive Exams, SC, OBC, Stipend, Education
brief_description: The scheme provides quality coaching and financial support to economically disadvantaged SC and OBC students preparing for competitive examinations and higher education entrance tests.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to SC or OBC category, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.8,00,000, and should be pursuing or have completed the qualifying educational level required for the examination. Benefits can be availed a maximum of two times.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Caste Certificate, Residence Proof, Age Proof, Educational Certificates, Aadhaar-linked Bank Account Details, and self-declaration regarding non-availment of similar coaching benefits.
application_process: Apply online through the Free Coaching Scheme Portal during the notified application period and upload all required documents.
benefits: Monthly stipend of Rs.4,000 for the course duration (up to 12 months), coaching fee support, and incentive of Rs.15,000 for candidates qualifying the mains stage of Civil Services examinations and similar competitive examinations.

scheme_name: Varnam Scheme for Transgender Students Pursuing Distance Education Courses
implementing_agency: Social Justice Department, Government of Kerala
tags: Transgender, Distance Education, Scholarship, Higher Education, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to transgender students pursuing degree, diploma, and postgraduate courses through distance education.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Kerala, identify as a transgender person, and be pursuing a degree, diploma, or postgraduate course through distance education, open university, or private registration mode.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Transgender Identity Card or Self-Declaration, Residence Proof, Previous Semester Hall Ticket, Fee Receipts and Bills, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents through the concerned District Social Justice Office.
benefits: Financial assistance of up to Rs.24,000 per year. Assistance is available for up to 3 years for undergraduate courses and up to 2 years for postgraduate courses.

scheme_name: Sub-Mission on Seeds and Planting Material (SMSP)
implementing_agency: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India
tags: Agriculture, Seeds, Planting Material, Subsidy, Seed Production
brief_description: The Sub-Mission on Seeds and Planting Material aims to improve the availability, quality, production, storage, certification, and distribution of quality seeds and planting materials across India.
eligibility_criteria: Small and marginal farmers and other eligible stakeholders involved in seed production, distribution, certification, and related agricultural activities are eligible as per component-specific guidelines.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Land Records, Farmer Registration Details, Bank Account Details, and other documents prescribed under the relevant component.
application_process: Eligible beneficiaries may apply through the concerned State Agriculture Department or implementing agency under the applicable component of the scheme.
benefits: Financial assistance and subsidies for seed production, seed villages, seed processing facilities, storage facilities, seed certification, quality control, transportation of seeds, and other approved activities under the scheme.

implementing_agency: Backward Classes Welfare Department, Government of Uttar Pradesh
tags: Marriage Assistance, OBC, Financial Assistance, Women Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for the marriage of daughters belonging to economically weaker families from Other Backward Classes (OBCs) in Uttar Pradesh.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the OBC category (excluding minority OBCs). Annual family income should not exceed Rs.56,460 in urban areas and Rs.46,080 in rural areas. The bride must be at least 18 years old and the groom at least 21 years old. Assistance is available for a maximum of two daughters per family.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Caste Certificate, Marriage Invitation Card, Widow or Disability Certificate (if applicable), and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the Marriage Grant Portal or through Jan Suvidha Kendras. Applications must be submitted within 90 days before or after the date of marriage along with the required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.20,000 per marriage.

scheme_name: Marriage Assistance to Daughters of Differently Abled Parents (Parinayam-I)
implementing_agency: Social Justice Department, Government of Kerala
tags: Marriage Assistance, Disability, Daughter, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to differently abled parents in Kerala for the marriage of their daughters.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Kerala and a differently abled parent. The bride must be at least 18 years old. Annual family income should not exceed Rs.1,00,000. Assistance is available for up to two daughters subject to scheme conditions.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Birth Certificate of Bride, Disability Identity Card, Ration Card, Marriage Certificate or Confirmation Details, Family Income Certificate, Affidavit from Groom, Bank Passbook Copy, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the SUNEETHI Portal by registering, completing the profile, selecting the scheme, uploading documents, and submitting the application at least one month before the marriage date.
benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs.30,000 for the marriage of a daughter.

scheme_name: Educational Assistance to Children of Prisoners (Pursuing Professional Courses)
implementing_agency: Social Justice Department, Government of Kerala
tags: Education Assistance, Professional Course, Prisoners, Student, Scholarship
brief_description: The scheme provides educational assistance to children of prisoners pursuing professional degree courses in Government and aided colleges in Kerala.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Kerala, belong to a BPL family, be the child of a prisoner sentenced to life imprisonment or death sentence, have secured at least 70% marks in Higher Secondary examinations, and have obtained admission through merit.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Ration Card, College Certificate showing fees and admission details, Previous Educational Certificates, Jail Superintendent Certificate, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply through the SUNEETHI Portal by registering, selecting the scheme, uploading required documents, and submitting the application online.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.1,00,000 per student, including tuition fees and hostel fees for professional degree courses.

implementing_agency: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), Department of Biotechnology, Government of India
tags: Biotechnology, Innovation, Entrepreneurship, Incubation, Research Infrastructure
brief_description: The scheme supports establishment of E-YUVA Centers in universities and institutions to promote innovation, entrepreneurship, translational research, and biotechnology-based startups among students and researchers.
eligibility_criteria: Universities and institutions established as legal entities in India with at least three years of existence, life science departments, innovation support mechanisms, dedicated incubation space, and linkages with bio-incubators are eligible.
documents_required: Registration or Recognition Certificate of the Institution, Infrastructure Details, Bio-incubator Collaboration Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Eligible institutions should submit online proposals through the BIRAC portal. Applications are evaluated by an expert committee followed by presentation and site assessment before approval.
benefits: Financial support for creation of pre-incubation infrastructure, equipment, manpower, workshops, outreach activities, and operational expenses of E-YUVA Centers for an initial period of three years.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Construction/Purchase of Wooden/FRP Fishing Vessel (Canoe) for Fishermen
implementing_agency: Department of Fisheries, Government of Goa
tags: Fisheries, Fishing Vessel, Canoe, Subsidy, Fishermen
brief_description: The scheme provides subsidy for construction or purchase of wooden or FRP fishing canoes to support traditional fishermen in Goa.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a traditional fisherman by birth or profession, be a resident of Goa, and should not be a defaulter with the Directorate of Fisheries. The fishing vessel must be registered with the Directorate of Fisheries and used exclusively for fishing purposes.
documents_required: Vessel Registration Certificate, Fishing Vessel License, Fishing Net License, Fishing Net Book, GST Bill, Aadhaar Card, Election Card, Bank Passbook, Bank Letter (if financed), Inspection Report, and Caste Certificate (if applicable).
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the Department of Fisheries or its designated offices in Goa.
benefits: 50% subsidy up to Rs.60,000 for General Category beneficiaries and 50% subsidy up to Rs.80,000 for SC/ST beneficiaries. Assistance is available once every four years for one craft per family.

implementing_agency: Department of Information Technology, Electronics and Communications, Government of Goa
tags: IT Sector, Lease Rental, Office Space, Subsidy, Employment
brief_description: The scheme provides lease rental subsidy to eligible Information Technology units operating in Goa to encourage investment, expansion, and employment generation.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a new or existing IT unit operating in Goa and may be a proprietorship, partnership firm, LLP, private limited company, or public limited company. Bank accounts of the unit and its promoters should be Aadhaar-linked.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, Incorporation Certificate, PAN, Aadhaar Card, GST Registration, Memorandum and Articles of Association or Partnership Documents, Occupancy Certificate, Business Plan or DPR, Employee Details, Proof of Employment, Lease Agreement, Rent Payment Proof, and related supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Goa Online Portal by registering, selecting the scheme under IT Services, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application. Claims may be submitted quarterly, half-yearly, or annually after incurring eligible expenditure.
benefits: Lease rental reimbursement of up to 50% of actual lease rentals for two years. Units with at least 30% women employees may receive up to 60% reimbursement. Maximum benefit for smaller business units is Rs.10,00,000.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Nirman Shramik Awas Sahayata Yojana
implementing_agency: Chhattisgarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Housing Assistance, Construction Workers, Labour Welfare, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered construction workers for construction or purchase of a house.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered construction worker with at least three years of continuous active registration. The worker or family members should not own a residential house in Chhattisgarh. Only one beneficiary per household is eligible.
documents_required: Employer Certificate showing at least 90 days of construction work, Housing Loan Sanction Letter, Interest Calculation Statement, Land Ownership Documents or Lease Documents, Aadhaar Card, and Self-Declaration Form.
application_process: Apply online through the Chhattisgarh Labour Department Portal under the Chhattisgarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board services by selecting the scheme and submitting the required documents.
benefits: Subsidy of up to Rs.50,000 towards interest paid on housing loans taken for construction or purchase of a house.

scheme_name: Women in Science and Engineering - Societal Challenges with Opportunities (WISE-SCOPE) Fellowship
implementing_agency: Department of Science and Technology, Government of India
tags: WISE, Fellowship, Research, Women Scientists, Innovation
brief_description: The fellowship supports women scientists with doctoral qualifications to develop science and technology solutions for societal challenges and sustainable development.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a woman scientist aged between 27 and 60 years, possess a PhD in Basic or Applied Sciences or equivalent qualifications such as MD/MS/MDS, and should not be in regular employment.
documents_required: Project Proposal, Mentor CV, Mentor Consent Letter, Endorsement from Host Institution, Highest Degree Certificate, Date of Birth Proof, HRA Declaration, Conflict of Interest Document, and Undertaking.
application_process: Apply online through the DST e-PMS Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading supporting documents, and submitting the proposal electronically.
benefits: Fellowship of Rs.55,000 per month plus HRA, research grant of Rs.2,00,000 per year, field demonstration support up to Rs.1,00,000, and equipment grant up to Rs.2,00,000.

implementing_agency: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
target_beneficiaries: Faculty Members of AICTE Approved Institutions
tags: PhD, Scholarship, Faculty Development, Higher Education, Foreign University
brief_description: The scheme provides scholarship support to eligible faculty members pursuing PhD programmes in top-ranked foreign universities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a full-time regular faculty member of an AICTE-approved degree-level institution, possess a Master's degree in the relevant discipline, have at least three years of teaching experience, and hold an admission offer from an eligible foreign university.
documents_required: Application Form, Admission Offer Letter from Foreign University, Passport and Visa Copies, Undertaking, Institute Recommendation Letter, Relieving Order, and Bank Details.
application_process: After obtaining admission to an eligible foreign university, applicants should submit the prescribed application along with required documents to AICTE for scholarship consideration.
benefits: Scholarship of Rs.25,000 per month for eligible faculty pursuing doctoral studies in approved foreign universities.

implementing_agency: Kerala State Council for Science, Technology and Environment (KSCSTE)
tags: Emeritus Scientist, Fellowship, Research, Science, Academia
brief_description: The scheme provides an opportunity for outstanding retired scientists and academicians to continue research activities and contribute to the scientific development of Kerala.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Kerala, be a retired scientist or academician with notable research achievements, apply within five years of retirement, and have significant recent research publications or patents.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Photograph, Residence Proof, PhD Certificate, Research Publications, Biodata, Retirement Certificate, Consent Letter from Host Institution, Declaration Form, and Endorsement from the Institution.
application_process: Apply online through the KSCSTE submission portal. Applications undergo eligibility screening, peer review, and expert committee evaluation before approval.
benefits: Monthly fellowship of Rs.31,000 for up to three years, annual contingency grant of Rs.1,00,000, and provision for technical assistance through a project fellow.

implementing_agency: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
tags: Disability, Day Care, Vocational Training, Counseling, Special Care
brief_description: The scheme provides day care, vocational training, life skills development, and caregiver support for persons with disabilities covered under the National Trust Act.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be 10 years of age or above and have Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Intellectual Disability, or Multiple Disabilities covered under the National Trust Act, 1999. Beneficiaries enrolled under Samarth or Gharaunda schemes are not eligible.
documents_required: Enrollment Form, Birth Certificate or Age Proof, Disability Certificate, Income Certificate or BPL Certificate (if applicable), Parent or Guardian Identity Proof, Residence Proof, Guardianship Declaration or Legal Guardianship Certificate (if applicable).
application_process: Visit a registered Vikas Centre with the required documents. After assessment and verification, eligible beneficiaries are enrolled and provided services through the centre.
benefits: Day care services, vocational and interpersonal skill development, counseling and guidance for parents, support for daily living activities, assistance for education and employment opportunities, and optional transportation facilities.

implementing_agency: Department of Education, Government of Arunachal Pradesh
tags: Mid Day Meal, Nutrition, School Education, Children
brief_description: The scheme provides free nutritious cooked meals to school children to improve nutrition, increase enrollment, and reduce dropout rates.
eligibility_criteria: Students studying in Government and Government-aided schools, generally between 6 and 14 years of age, are eligible. Students should be regularly attending recognized schools.
documents_required: School Enrollment Records and other documents as prescribed by the school authorities.
application_process: Eligible students enrolled in Government or Government-aided schools are automatically covered under the scheme through their schools.
benefits: Free cooked nutritious meals during school days.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Aids and Appliances for Disabled Persons
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Special Assistance, Government of Maharashtra
tags: Disability, Aids and Appliances, Financial Assistance, Social Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to persons with disabilities for the purchase of aids and appliances based on the type and severity of disability.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Maharashtra, have at least 40% disability, and possess a valid disability certificate. Financial assistance varies based on income criteria prescribed by the Government.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Disability Certificate, Income Certificate, Residence Proof, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, Medical Recommendation, and Quotations or Bills for Aids and Appliances.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the District Social Welfare Office of the Department of Social Justice and Special Assistance.
benefits: Financial assistance of up to 100% of the cost of aids and appliances for eligible low-income beneficiaries and partial assistance for other eligible categories as per scheme norms.

implementing_agency: All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)
tags: Fellowship, Higher Education, Academic Excellence, Mentorship
brief_description: The fellowship utilizes the expertise of highly accomplished retired professionals and academicians to mentor students and faculty in AICTE-approved institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a highly qualified retired professional or academician with exceptional contributions in their field, recognized leadership, significant research and development achievements, and notable societal impact.
documents_required: Application Form, Curriculum Vitae, Proof of Academic and Professional Achievements, Identity Proof, Retirement Proof, Recommendation Documents, and other documents prescribed by AICTE.
application_process: Eligible candidates may apply to AICTE in the prescribed format along with supporting documents as notified under the fellowship guidelines.
benefits: Honorarium of Rs.10,000 per day for interaction with students and faculty, subject to AICTE fellowship norms and tenure conditions.

implementing_agency: Women and Child Development Department, Government of Haryana
tags: Girl Child, Financial Assistance, Education, Social Welfare, LIC Investment
brief_description: The scheme promotes the welfare, education, and social security of girl children through financial investment made in their name by the Government of Haryana.
eligibility_criteria: The first girl child born on or after 22 January 2015 in SC or BPL families, the second girl child in any family, and eligible twin or multiple girl children born in Haryana are covered. The family should be resident in Haryana, the birth should be registered, and the child should possess Aadhaar and immunization records.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card of Child and Parents, Residence Proof, Caste Certificate, Birth Certificate, Immunization Card, Passport-size Photograph, BPL Certificate (if applicable), and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Obtain the application form from the Anganwadi Centre or Department website, submit the completed form with supporting documents through the Anganwadi Worker or Health Staff, and complete verification by the Women and Child Development Department.
benefits: One-time investment of Rs.21,000 in the name of eligible girl children through LIC. The maturity amount is payable when the beneficiary attains 18 years of age and remains unmarried.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Child Victims of Violence under the National Foundation for Communal Harmony (NFCH)
implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Jammu and Kashmir
tags: Child Welfare, Education Assistance, Orphan, Destitute, Rehabilitation
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for the education, rehabilitation, and welfare of children affected by communal, caste, ethnic, or terrorist violence.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Jammu and Kashmir, be below 18 years of age (extendable up to 21 years in exceptional cases), belong to a BPL family, and be an orphan, destitute, or a child whose primary breadwinner was killed or permanently incapacitated due to communal, caste, ethnic, or terrorist violence. The applicant should not be receiving regular assistance from another source.
documents_required: Domicile Certificate, Passport-size Photographs, Age Proof, Aadhaar Card, BPL Certificate, Death Certificate of Parents (if applicable), Bank Account Details, and other documents required by the District Social Welfare Office.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the District Social Welfare Office (DSWO). Applications are verified by the District Committee and recommended for sanction under NFCH.
benefits: Monthly educational assistance of Rs.750 to Rs.800 until 18 years of age, additional vocational training assistance of Rs.150 per month, and group personal accident insurance coverage up to Rs.25,000.

implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Himachal Pradesh
tags: Widow, Re-Marriage, Financial Assistance, Women Welfare
category: Women & Child Development, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to widows who remarry to support their rehabilitation and social security.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a widow who has remarried, be a permanent resident of Himachal Pradesh, the bride should be at least 18 years of age, and the groom should be at least 21 years of age.
documents_required: Marriage Certificate, Widow Certificate, Residence Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, Age Proof of Bride and Groom, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the District Welfare Office of the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.25,000, including Rs.10,000 as immediate cash assistance and Rs.15,000 kept as a fixed deposit for five years.

implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Uttarakhand
tags: Scholarship, SC Students, Education, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides scholarship assistance to Scheduled Caste students studying in recognized schools in Uttarakhand.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Uttarakhand, belong to the Scheduled Caste category, study in a recognized institution, have passed the previous examination, and should not be enrolled in more than one institution during the same academic year.
documents_required: Bank Passbook Copy, Identity Proof, Income Certificate, Student Photograph, Caste Certificate, and Aadhaar Card.
application_process: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Rs.600 per annum for Classes 1-5, Rs.960 per annum for Classes 6-8, Rs.3,000 per annum for day scholars in Classes 9-10, and Rs.6,250 per annum for hostellers in Classes 9-10.

implementing_agency: Housing Department, Government of West Bengal
tags: Housing, Government Employees, Residential Flats, Housing Scheme
brief_description: The scheme provides affordable housing facilities to serving State Government employees of West Bengal on a no-profit, no-loss basis.
eligibility_criteria: Serving State Government employees as defined under the West Bengal Service Rules are eligible, except employees already covered under specific government housing schemes such as housing schemes for police personnel or other notified categories.
documents_required: Employee Identity Card, Service Certificate, Salary Certificate, Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Passport-size Photograph, and other documents prescribed by the Housing Department.
application_process: Eligible employees may apply through the Housing Department or West Bengal Housing Infrastructure Development Corporation as per the notification issued for each housing project.
benefits: Allotment of residential flats under government housing projects at subsidized no-profit, no-loss pricing.

implementing_agency: Development Commissioner for Handlooms, Ministry of Textiles, Government of India
tags: Handloom, Weaver, Technology Upgradation, Productivity Enhancement
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for technology upgradation of handlooms and accessories to improve productivity and fabric quality.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Odisha, be a handloom weaver, have undergone weaving training under recognized government programmes within the previous two years, and be willing to contribute 10% of the project cost.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Weaver Identity Card, Residence Certificate, Training Certificate, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application through the concerned District Handloom Office or implementing agency along with supporting documents.
benefits: Financial assistance covering 90% of the cost of approved technology upgradation items, with the beneficiary contributing the remaining 10%.

scheme_name: KARMASHREE - The Chief Minister's Award for Excellence in Public Administration
implementing_agency: Administrative Reforms and Training Department, Government of Assam
tags: Award, Public Administration, E-Governance, Innovation, Government Officers
brief_description: The award recognizes and rewards outstanding, innovative, and exemplary work performed by State Government officers in public administration and implementation of government schemes and programmes.
eligibility_criteria: Individual State Government officers or groups of officers are eligible. A group may consist of up to four officers including the applicant. Selection is based on innovation, sustainability, scalability, impact on service delivery, transparency, efficiency, governance improvement, and social or economic benefits generated by the initiative.
documents_required: Nomination Form, Project Report or Initiative Details, Supporting Documents Demonstrating Impact, Recommendation Letter from Competent Authority, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed nomination form along with supporting documents through the Administrative Reforms and Training Department. Applications are screened by a Screening Committee, evaluated by an Expert Committee, and finalized by a State Level Committee.
benefits: Citation and recognition under the Chief Minister's Award for Excellence in Public Administration.

scheme_name: Student READY (Rural Entrepreneurship Awareness Development Yojana)
implementing_agency: Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR)
tags: Agriculture, Student, Entrepreneurship, Internship, Skill Development
brief_description: The programme provides practical training, entrepreneurship exposure, rural work experience, internships, and skill development opportunities to undergraduate students in agriculture and allied sciences.
eligibility_criteria: Students pursuing undergraduate programmes in ICAR-accredited agricultural universities and institutions under the National Agricultural Research and Education System (NARES) are eligible. Students must maintain good academic performance and conduct throughout the programme.
documents_required: Student Identity Card, Academic Records, Institution Recommendation, Enrollment Certificate, Bank Account Details (for stipend components), and other documents prescribed by the institution.
application_process: Eligible students are nominated and enrolled through their respective agricultural universities as part of the prescribed undergraduate curriculum.
benefits: Hands-on training, entrepreneurship development, rural agricultural work experience, industrial attachment opportunities, student projects, and stipend support of up to Rs.3,000 per month for eligible rural training components.

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)
implementing_agency: Women and Child Development Department, Government of Sikkim
tags: Widow Pension, BPL, Social Security, Direct Benefit Transfer
category: Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women & Child Development
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly pension assistance to widows belonging to Below Poverty Line families in Sikkim.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a domicile of Sikkim, belong to a BPL family, be 21 years of age or above, should not be receiving salary, pension, or family pension from any government source, and should have a bank account under Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana. Pension ceases upon remarriage.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, BPL Certificate, Residence Certificate, Widow Certificate, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form with supporting documents to the Women and Child Development Department or the designated local authority.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.2,000 through Direct Benefit Transfer.

implementing_agency: Education Department, Government of Gujarat
tags: Scholarship, Higher Education, Tuition Fee Assistance, Hostel Grant
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to economically weaker students of Gujarat pursuing diploma, undergraduate, professional, and technical courses.
eligibility_criteria: Students should have secured at least 80 percentile in Class 10 or Class 12 examinations for fresh admissions, or 65% marks in diploma programmes for degree admissions. Annual family income should not exceed Rs.6,00,000.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Admission Letter, Previous Academic Mark Sheets, Bank Account Details, Hostel Certificate (if applicable), and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Apply online through the MYSY Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Tuition fee assistance, hostel grant of Rs.1,200 per month for 10 months where hostel facilities are unavailable, book and equipment assistance, and additional support for eligible professional courses as per scheme norms.

scheme_name: CM's Lairik Heikhanashi Programme (CMLHP) - Pre-Matric Remedial Coaching
implementing_agency: Directorate for Welfare of OBCs and SCs, Government of Manipur
tags: Remedial Coaching, Education, SC Students, OBC Students, Minority Students
brief_description: The programme provides free remedial coaching in English, Mathematics, and Science to academically weak students belonging to SC, OBC, and Minority communities in Manipur.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Manipur, studying in Classes 8 to 10 in a Government or Government-aided institution, and belong to a Scheduled Caste, Other Backward Class, or Minority community. Selection is based on academic performance and economic criteria.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate or Minority Certificate, Income Certificate, School Enrollment Certificate, Previous Academic Records, Passport-size Photograph, and Residence Proof.
application_process: Eligible students may apply through their schools or designated coaching centres empanelled under the programme as per notifications issued by the Directorate.
benefits: Free remedial coaching in English, Mathematics, and Science for a period of up to six months through empanelled coaching centres.

scheme_name: Assistance Scheme for Construction Workers in Case of Death or Injury
implementing_agency: Rajasthan Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Construction Worker, Accident, Death Benefit, Injury Compensation, Labour Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered construction workers and their families in cases of accidental injury, permanent disability, or death.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Rajasthan, aged between 18 and 60 years, and registered as a construction worker with the Rajasthan Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Benefits are not admissible in cases of suicide, intoxication, or injuries resulting from unlawful activities.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Construction Worker Registration Card, Residence Proof, Medical Certificate or Disability Certificate (for injury cases), FIR or Accident Report (if applicable), Death Certificate (for death cases), Bank Account Details, and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Rajasthan Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board or the designated Labour Department office.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.3,00,000 in case of accidental death or permanent disability, along with reimbursement of eligible medical expenses arising from workplace accidents.

implementing_agency: Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation Limited (KMDC)
tags: Minority Welfare, Entrepreneurship, Loan, Skill Development, Self Employment
brief_description: The scheme provides skill development support and concessional loans to minority community entrepreneurs for self-employment activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Karnataka, belong to a notified religious minority community, be between 18 and 55 years of age, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.3.50 lakh, should not have availed KMDC benefits during the previous five years (except Arivu Scheme), and should not be a loan defaulter.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Minority Certificate, Income Certificate, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, Educational Qualification Certificate (if applicable), and Self-Employment Project Details.
application_process: Apply online through the KMDC portal, print the application form, and submit it along with supporting documents to the concerned District Minority Office or Selection Committee for verification and approval.
benefits: Loan assistance of Rs.50,000 at 4% interest repayable in 36 installments. Upon successful repayment of 50% of the loan within the prescribed period, the remaining 50% is treated as back-end subsidy.

implementing_agency: Department of Revenue, Government of NCT of Delhi
tags: Pilgrimage, Senior Citizens, Travel Assistance, Religious Tourism
brief_description: The scheme provides free pilgrimage trips to senior citizens of Delhi, covering travel, accommodation, food, and related expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Delhi, aged 60 years or above, should not be employed with the Central Government, State Government, or local bodies, and should not have previously availed benefits under the scheme. One attendant aged 21 years or above may accompany the beneficiary.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Proof, Age Proof, Passport-size Photograph, Voter ID Card, Medical Fitness Certificate (if required), and Attendant Details (if applicable).
application_process: Apply online through the Delhi e-District Portal. Applications may also be facilitated through SDM Offices, Tirth Yatra Facilitation Centres, and MLA Offices.
benefits: Free pilgrimage travel including transportation, accommodation, meals, and related expenses for eligible senior citizens and one attendant.

implementing_agency: Health, Medical and Family Welfare Department, Government of Telangana
tags: Eye Care, Vision Screening, Health Camp, Blindness Prevention
brief_description: Kanti Velugu is a universal eye screening programme aimed at achieving avoidable blindness-free Telangana through comprehensive eye examinations and treatment support.
eligibility_criteria: All residents of Telangana are eligible for free eye screening and related services under the programme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or any valid identity proof for registration at screening camps.
application_process: Beneficiaries can visit designated Kanti Velugu screening camps organized across Telangana and undergo free eye examinations by authorized medical teams.
benefits: Free eye screening, vision testing, distribution of spectacles, referral for advanced treatment, and identification and management of eye-related diseases.

scheme_name: Post-Matric Scholarship for Scheduled Tribe Students
implementing_agency: Higher and Technical Education Department, Government of Mizoram
tags: Scholarship, ST Students, Higher Education, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Tribe students pursuing post-matric education in recognized institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to a Scheduled Tribe community, be a permanent resident of Mizoram, be studying in Class XI or above including post-doctoral courses, and have annual parental income not exceeding Rs.2,50,000.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Scheduled Tribe Certificate, Income Certificate, Residence Certificate, Previous Examination Marksheet, Admission Proof, Bank Account Details, and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Apply through the designated scholarship portal or the concerned educational institution as notified by the Government of Mizoram.
benefits: Maintenance allowance, reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees, study tour charges, thesis typing and printing charges, and other admissible educational expenses as per scholarship norms.

implementing_agency: Dr. YSR Aarogyasri Health Care Trust, Government of Andhra Pradesh
tags: Health Insurance, Cashless Treatment, Government Employees, Pensioners, Hospitalization
brief_description: The Employee Health Scheme (EHS) provides cashless medical treatment through empanelled hospitals to Andhra Pradesh Government employees, pensioners, and their dependent family members.
eligibility_criteria: Regular State Government employees, provincialized employees of local bodies, service pensioners, family pensioners, and re-employed service pensioners of Andhra Pradesh are eligible under the scheme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Employee ID or Pension Payment Order (PPO), Aadhaar-linked Mobile Number, Dependent Family Member Details, Passport-size Photograph, and Service or Pension Records.
application_process: Register through the Employee Health Scheme Portal, complete beneficiary enrollment, generate a health card, and avail cashless treatment at empanelled network hospitals.
benefits: Cashless hospitalization, post-hospitalization care, treatment for notified diseases and medical procedures, and coverage for eligible dependent family members through empanelled hospitals.

scheme_name: Ministry of External Affairs Internship Programme
implementing_agency: Administration Division, Ministry of External Affairs, Government of India
tags: Internship, Foreign Policy, International Relations, Students, Skill Development
brief_description: The Ministry of External Affairs Internship Programme provides young Indians an opportunity to gain exposure to foreign policy, diplomacy, and the functioning of the Ministry of External Affairs.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian citizen, either a graduate from a recognized university or a student in the final year of graduation where internship is a mandatory part of the curriculum. The applicant should not be more than 25 years of age as on 31 December of the internship year.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Educational Certificates or Academic Records, Proof of Enrollment (for final-year students), Resume, and Category Certificate (if applicable).
application_process: Apply online through the Ministry of External Affairs Internship Portal during the notified application period and upload the required documents.
benefits: Internship exposure in various divisions of the Ministry of External Affairs, interaction with policymakers and diplomats, and a stipend as notified by the Ministry for the internship cycle.

implementing_agency: Department of Social Welfare, Government of Karnataka
tags: Startup, Entrepreneurship, SC, ST, Innovation, Seed Funding
brief_description: The Unnati Scheme supports innovative SC/ST entrepreneurs and startups by providing financial assistance, incubation support, seed funding, and market access opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a startup registered in Karnataka with an innovative technology-based idea. At least one founder should belong to the SC/ST community and hold a substantial stake in the enterprise. Total turnover should be less than Rs.50 crore and at least 50% of the qualified workforce should be employed in Karnataka.
documents_required: Startup Registration Certificate, Aadhaar Card, SC/ST Caste Certificate, PAN Card, Incorporation Documents, Business Plan or Pitch Deck, Bank Account Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Unnati Scheme Portal by submitting startup details, business proposal, founder information, and supporting documents for evaluation and selection.
benefits: Financial assistance of up to Rs.50 lakh, incubation support, mentoring, market linkage opportunities, and startup development assistance.

implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka
tags: Law Graduates, SC, ST, Stipend, Legal Profession
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance in the form of a stipend to SC/ST law graduates to support legal practice and professional training under senior advocates.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Karnataka, belong to the SC/ST community, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.2 lakh, be below 40 years of age, and possess a 3-year or 5-year law degree from a recognized university or institution.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Law Degree Certificate, Bank Passbook Copy, Mobile Number, and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Apply online through the Social Welfare Department stipend portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Monthly stipend of Rs.5,000 for a period of up to four years to support legal practice and professional training.

implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Haryana
tags: Dwarfism, Disability Assistance, Social Security, Monthly Allowance
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly financial assistance to persons with dwarfism to ensure social security and improve their quality of life.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a domicile of Haryana, have been residing in the state for at least one year, be 18 years of age or above, and possess a certificate from the Civil Surgeon certifying dwarfism. Male applicants should have a height of 3 feet 8 inches or less and female applicants should have a height of 3 feet 3 inches or less.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Dwarfism Certificate issued by the Civil Surgeon, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, Family ID, and Age Proof.
application_process: Apply through Antyodaya Saral Centres, Atal Seva Kendras, or the Saral Haryana Portal by submitting the prescribed application form and required documents.
benefits: Monthly allowance of Rs.2,500 per beneficiary.

implementing_agency: Department of Skill Development, Technical Education and Employment, Government of Chhattisgarh
tags: Unemployment Allowance, Financial Assistance, Youth, Employment
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly financial assistance to educated unemployed youth of Chhattisgarh until they secure employment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a native of Chhattisgarh, aged between 18 and 35 years, have passed at least Class 12 from a recognized board, be registered with a District Employment and Self-Employment Guidance Centre for at least two years, and have annual family income not exceeding Rs.2,50,000. Only one eligible member per family can avail the benefit.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Class 12 or Higher Educational Qualification Certificate, Employment Registration Certificate, Income Certificate, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and Mobile Number.
application_process: Apply online through the Chhattisgarh Berojgari Bhatta Portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Monthly unemployment allowance of Rs.2,500 to eligible unemployed youth until employment is secured or as per scheme guidelines.

implementing_agency: Department of Animal Husbandry and Veterinary, Government of Mizoram
target_beneficiaries: Entrepreneurs, SHGs, FPOs and Dairy Farmers
tags: Dairy Farming, Breed Multiplication Farm, Entrepreneurship, Livestock Development
brief_description: The scheme promotes establishment of Breed Multiplication Farms to produce disease-free, high-yielding dairy animals and strengthen the dairy sector through entrepreneurship.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant may be an entrepreneur, private individual, SHG, FPO, FCO, JLG, or Section 8 Company registered in India. The applicant should have experience in dairy farming or breeding, possess or lease at least 5 acres of land, and establish a Breed Multiplication Farm with a minimum capacity of 200 milch cows or buffaloes.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Land Ownership or Lease Documents, Project Report, Registration Certificate (for organizations), Bank Account Details, Experience Certificate in Dairy Farming (if applicable), and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application along with a detailed project proposal and supporting documents to the Department of Animal Husbandry and Veterinary, Government of Mizoram.
benefits: Capital subsidy of 50% of the project cost subject to a maximum limit of Rs.2 crore for establishing a Breed Multiplication Farm.

scheme_name: Rythu Bima - Farmers Group Life Insurance Scheme
implementing_agency: Agriculture Department, Government of Telangana
tags: Farmers, Insurance, Life Insurance, Financial Security
category: Agriculture, Rural & Environment, Banking, Financial Services and Insurance
brief_description: Rythu Bima provides life insurance coverage to farmers in Telangana, ensuring financial security to their families in the event of the farmer's death.
eligibility_criteria: All farmers of Telangana aged between 18 and 59 years who possess agricultural land records and are enrolled under the scheme are eligible.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Pattadar Passbook or Land Records, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and Nominee Details.
application_process: Eligible farmers are enrolled through the Agriculture Department using land ownership records. No separate premium payment or application is required from beneficiaries.
benefits: Life insurance cover of Rs.5,00,000 payable to the nominee in case of the death of the insured farmer, irrespective of the cause of death.

implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Jammu and Kashmir
tags: Blind, Disability, Education, Residential School, Shelter
brief_description: The Residential School for the Blind provides free education, accommodation, care, and rehabilitation services to visually impaired children in Jammu and Kashmir.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian citizen, a permanent resident of Jammu and Kashmir, visually impaired or blind, and between 6 and 21 years of age.
documents_required: Disability Certificate, Domicile Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Birth Certificate or Age Proof, Passport-size Photograph, Medical Certificate, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the application through the concerned District Social Welfare Officer (DSWO) along with the required documents for verification and admission.
benefits: Free residential accommodation, education, food, clothing, rehabilitation support, and other welfare services.

scheme_name: Meghalaya Tourism Development and Investment Promotion Scheme
implementing_agency: Tourism Department, Government of Meghalaya
tags: Tourism, Homestay, Resort, Infrastructure Development, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme promotes tourism infrastructure development in Meghalaya through financial assistance for homestays and resorts.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should own the land or property where the project is proposed. Homestay projects should have a project cost up to Rs.16 lakh, while resort projects should have a project cost up to Rs.100 lakh. Projects must be completed within the prescribed time limits and comply with government norms.
documents_required: Land Ownership Documents, Project Report, Identity Proof, Bank Account Details, Building Plan Approval, Photographs of Site, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with the project proposal and supporting documents to the Tourism Department, Government of Meghalaya.
benefits: Capital subsidy of 30% of the approved project cost for eligible homestay and resort projects, subject to scheme limits.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Jan Kalyan Shiksha Protsahan Yojana
implementing_agency: Higher Education Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
tags: Higher Education, Scholarship, Financial Assistance, Students, Unorganized Workers
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for higher education to children of registered unorganized workers in Madhya Pradesh by covering tuition and other eligible academic fees.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh. The student's mother or father must be registered as an unorganized worker under the Labour Department. The student should have secured admission in eligible degree, diploma, medical, engineering, law, polytechnic, ITI, or other approved higher education courses in recognized institutions.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Income Certificate, Age Proof, Passport-size Photograph, Mobile Number, Email ID, Bank Account Details, Admission Proof, and Parent's Labour Registration Certificate.
application_process: Apply online through the Madhya Pradesh Scholarship Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: The State Government bears tuition fees and other eligible academic fees for approved higher education courses. Admission fees and tuition fees are covered as per prescribed norms, excluding mess charges and caution deposits.
