implementing_agency: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Government of Uttar Pradesh
tags: Disability Pension, Social Security, Financial Assistance, Disability Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly financial assistance to persons with disabilities to improve their quality of life and support their livelihood.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Uttar Pradesh, aged 18 years or above, possess a disability certificate showing at least 40% disability, and belong to a family within the prescribed income limits. Beneficiaries receiving assistance under other similar pension schemes are not eligible.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Age Proof, Disability Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Income Certificate, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the Uttar Pradesh Pension Portal by filling the application form, entering personal, income, bank, and disability details, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.1,000 per beneficiary.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Cultural Affairs, Government of Assam
target_beneficiaries: Artists and Families of Deceased Artists
tags: Artist Pension, Culture, Financial Assistance, Family Pension, Art and Culture
brief_description: The scheme provides pension and financial assistance to artists and families of deceased artists who have made outstanding contributions to the cultural heritage of Assam.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an artist who has made significant contributions in the field of art and culture in Assam. Family pension is available to indigent family members of deceased artists who had made notable contributions to the cultural sphere of the state. Additional provisions are available for differently abled and bedridden artists.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Proof of Contribution in Art and Culture, Income Certificate, Residence Certificate, Bank Account Details, Death Certificate (for family pension cases), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Directorate of Cultural Affairs portal or submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the concerned Cultural Development Officer.
benefits: Monthly artist pension, family pension for eligible dependents, annual financial assistance under Barshik Silpi Sanman, and one-time special financial assistance to eligible families of deceased artists.

implementing_agency: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Self Employment, Entrepreneurship, MSME, Subsidy, Youth
brief_description: The scheme promotes self-employment among educated unemployed youth by providing subsidized loans for establishing manufacturing, service, and business enterprises.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Tamil Nadu, aged 18 to 35 years for General Category and up to 45 years for Special Categories (SC/ST/MBC/BC/Minorities/Ex-Servicemen/Differently Abled/Transgender). Applicant should have passed at least 8th Standard and have annual family income not exceeding Rs.5 lakh.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Passport-size Photograph, Income Certificate, Educational Qualification Certificate, Age Proof, Community Certificate, Ration Card or Nativity Certificate, Project Report, Quotations, Bank Account Details, and relevant category certificates.
application_process: Apply online through the UYEGP Portal of the MSME Department, complete registration, upload documents, attend verification and interview processes, complete Entrepreneurship Development Programme (EDP) training, and obtain bank loan sanction.
benefits: Subsidy of 25% of the project cost subject to a maximum of Rs.2.50 lakh. Loan assistance is available for manufacturing projects up to Rs.15 lakh and service or business projects up to Rs.5 lakh.

scheme_name: Jai Bhim Mukhyamantri Pratibha Vikas Yojana
implementing_agency: Department for the Welfare of SC/ST/OBC/Minorities, Government of NCT of Delhi
target_beneficiaries: SC Students Preparing for Competitive Examinations
tags: Coaching, Competitive Exams, SC Students, Stipend, Education
brief_description: The scheme provides quality coaching and financial assistance to economically disadvantaged Scheduled Caste students preparing for competitive examinations and professional entrance tests.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category, be a resident of Delhi, have studied in Delhi schools, and have annual family income not exceeding Rs.6 lakh. Students must meet the academic eligibility criteria prescribed for the relevant coaching programme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Class 10 and 12 Mark Sheets, Residence Proof, Caste Certificate, Ration Card, Passport-size Photograph, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply through empanelled coaching institutions by submitting the prescribed application form along with supporting documents. Selection is based on merit and eligibility criteria prescribed under the scheme.
benefits: Monthly stipend of Rs.2,500 for local students. Government covers 75% of admissible coaching fees, while students bear 25%. Students with annual family income up to Rs.2 lakh receive 100% coaching fee support.

scheme_name: AGR-4 Farm Mechanization Scheme for SC Farmers
implementing_agency: Directorate of Agriculture, Government of Gujarat
tags: Agriculture, Farm Mechanization, Agricultural Equipment, SC Farmers, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste farmers for purchasing agricultural machinery and equipment to improve farm productivity and promote agricultural mechanization.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Gujarat and belong to the Scheduled Caste category. The farmer should purchase approved agricultural equipment from authorized dealers as per scheme guidelines.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Land Ownership Documents (if applicable), Scheduled Caste Certificate, Disability Certificate (if applicable), Ration Card (if applicable), Bank Account Details, and Beneficiary Consent Form.
application_process: Apply online through the i-Khedut Portal by selecting the scheme, completing the application form, uploading required documents, confirming the application, and submitting it within the prescribed period.
benefits: Financial assistance for the purchase of approved agricultural machinery and equipment such as power tillers, rice transplanters, tractor-operated implements, self-propelled machinery, electric motor-operated equipment, and other farm mechanization components.

scheme_name: Vijayamritham Scheme - Cash Award for Meritorious Children with Disabilities
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
tags: Disability, Education, Cash Award, Meritorious Students, Scholarship
brief_description: The scheme provides one-time cash awards and recognition to meritorious students with disabilities who achieve high academic performance in degree, postgraduate, and professional courses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a student with at least 40% disability, have completed studies in Kerala, have passed the examination in the first attempt, and secured the prescribed minimum marks in eligible degree, postgraduate, or professional courses.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Mobile Number, Email ID, Passport-size Photograph, Disability Certificate or Disability Identity Card, Course Completion Certificate, Mark Sheets, Bank Passbook Copy, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Register and apply online through the Suneethi Portal of the Department of Social Justice by creating a profile, updating personal details, selecting the scheme, uploading documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Cash award of Rs.8,000 for eligible Degree/Equivalent course students and Rs.10,000 for eligible Postgraduate and Professional course students. Selected beneficiaries also receive certificates and mementos.

implementing_agency: Department of Tribal Welfare, Government of Goa
tags: Skill Development, Training, Scheduled Tribe, Self Employment, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Tribe youth for undertaking short-term professional training courses to improve employability and self-employment opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Scheduled Tribe community of Goa and be between 18 and 50 years of age.
documents_required: Scheduled Tribe Certificate, Age Proof, Educational Qualification Certificates (if applicable), Learning Licence (for driving courses), Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Passbook Copy, Fee Structure or Quotation from Training Institute, and Declaration Form.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Goa State Scheduled Tribes Finance and Development Corporation Limited.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.5,000 for one approved training course. Up to 50% of the approved course fee (maximum Rs.2,500) may be released in advance, with the balance released upon successful completion of the course.

implementing_agency: Agriculture Department, Government of Rajasthan
tags: Agriculture, Seeds, Farmers, Subsidy, Seed Production
brief_description: The scheme encourages farmers to produce quality seeds for their own use and promotes self-reliance in seed production through financial assistance and training support.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident farmer of Rajasthan and a member of a farmer group formed under the scheme for seed production activities.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card and Bank Passbook Copy.
application_process: Interested farmers should contact the Agriculture Department Office or Krishi Vigyan Kendra in their district, obtain the application form, submit the required documents, and complete the registration process.
benefits: Subsidy of up to 50% on seed production activities, free distribution of foundation or certified seeds to selected seed-producing farmers, training support, and assistance for roguing activities during seed production.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Purchase of Musical Instruments to Bhajani, Choir and Cultural Groups
implementing_agency: Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa
tags: Culture, Music, Grant-in-Aid, Musical Instruments, Cultural Groups
brief_description: The scheme provides grant-in-aid to Bhajani Mandals, Choir Groups, Cultural Troupes, and Institutions for purchasing musical instruments and promoting cultural activities.
eligibility_criteria: The group or institution should have at least 10 members, conduct regular cultural or musical performances, and should not have previously availed benefits under the scheme.
documents_required: Registration Certificate, Activity Report, Certificate of Existence and Functioning, List of Members, Identity Proof of Contact Person, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa.
benefits: Grant-in-aid up to Rs.15,000 per eligible group or institution for the purchase of musical instruments.

scheme_name: Internship Scheme by the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI)
implementing_agency: Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI)
tags: Internship, Technology, Cyber Law, Management, Research, Students
brief_description: The UIDAI Internship Scheme provides students and research scholars with practical exposure to technology, legal, management, and policy-related work carried out by UIDAI.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian citizen and a bonafide student of a recognized university or institution. Eligible applicants include pre-final or final year B.Tech/BE students with at least 60% marks, postgraduate students with at least 60% marks in graduation, research scholars, and PhD students. Graduates awaiting admission to higher studies may also apply subject to prescribed conditions.
documents_required: No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the institution, Academic Records, Identity Proof, Passport-size Photograph, Application Form, and additional documents as prescribed. PhD applicants must submit a recommendation letter from the supervisor and institution sponsorship.
application_process: Submit the duly filled application form along with required documents through email to UIDAI as per the internship notification and prescribed format.
benefits: Monthly stipend ranging from Rs.10,000 to Rs.50,000 depending on qualification, mode of internship, and discipline. Internship certificate is issued upon successful completion.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Women and Child Development, Government of Goa
target_beneficiaries: Individuals Promoting Safety and Dignity of Women
tags: Award, Women Safety, Courage, Social Service, Recognition
category: Social Welfare & Empowerment, Public Safety, Law & Justice
brief_description: The Rakshak Puraskars Award recognizes individuals who demonstrate exceptional courage and commitment in protecting, assisting, and supporting women and girls facing violence, harassment, or abuse.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should have shown exceptional courage or service in helping victims of violence, harassment, or abuse against women and girls. Applicant should be at least 25 years of age and either be a resident of Goa for the last five years or have worked in Goa for at least two years.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Identity Proof, Residential Certificate, Bank Account Details, Biodata, Supporting Certificates, Appreciation Letters, News Clippings, and Application Form.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Women and Child Development, Government of Goa.
benefits: Cash award of Rs.20,000 along with a citation and memento.

implementing_agency: Department of Tribal Welfare, Government of Goa
tags: Coaching, Scheduled Tribe, Education, Reimbursement, Competitive Preparation
brief_description: The scheme provides coaching support and fee reimbursement to Scheduled Tribe students studying in Classes X, XI (Science), and XII to help them prepare for higher education and professional courses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Scheduled Tribe community and be a regular full-time student studying in a recognized school or higher secondary institution in Goa. Fee reimbursement is available only for the class currently being studied.
documents_required: Scheduled Tribe Certificate, Birth Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Bank Account Details, Student Identity Card, Coaching Institute Fee Receipt, Coaching Institute Registration Certificate, and Fee Structure.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the Department of Tribal Welfare, Government of Goa.
benefits: Reimbursement of 75% of coaching fees, subject to a maximum of Rs.10,000 per annum per subject in rural areas and Rs.12,000 per annum per subject in urban areas, through Direct Benefit Transfer.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Cultural Organizations with National Presence
implementing_agency: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
tags: Culture, Grant-in-Aid, Cultural Organizations, Financial Assistance, National Presence
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to established cultural organizations with national presence for organizing cultural activities, festivals, research projects, and promotion of art and culture.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant organization should be registered in India, have a national presence, primarily undertake cultural activities, possess adequate infrastructure and experience, conduct at least 20 cultural events annually, and have spent at least Rs.1 crore on cultural activities during three of the last five years.
documents_required: Registration Documents, Constitution of the Organization, Governing Body Details, Annual Reports, Audited Financial Statements, Project Report, Bank Account Details, and Indemnity Bond.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Ministry of Culture through the recommended channel or as notified in scheme advertisements.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.2 crore per project, which may be enhanced up to Rs.5 crore in exceptional cases. Assistance is generally limited to 67% of the approved project cost.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa
tags: Artist Award, Youth, Culture, Recognition, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme recognizes and honours young artists of Goa for their outstanding achievements and contributions to the field of art and culture.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an individual artist aged between 25 and 40 years, with at least five years of contribution in the field of art and culture in Goa. Age relaxation of up to five years may be granted in exceptional cases.
documents_required: Recommendation Letter, Identity Proof, Age Proof, Biodata, Passport-size Photograph, Photographs of Contributions or Performances, Certificates of Achievement, Aadhaar Card, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa within the prescribed application period.
benefits: Cash award of Rs.25,000 along with a certificate of appreciation and memento. Up to six artists may be selected annually across different cultural fields.

scheme_name: State Post-Matric Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Special Assistance, Government of Maharashtra
tags: Scholarship, Disability, Higher Education, Financial Assistance, Post-Matric
brief_description: The scheme encourages students with disabilities to pursue higher education by providing post-matric scholarship assistance for approved courses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian citizen and permanent resident of Maharashtra, have at least 40% disability, have passed Class 10, be enrolled in a recognized institution in Maharashtra, and be pursuing Higher Secondary, Technical, Professional, Medical, Engineering, or other post-matric courses.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Disability Certificate, Domicile Certificate, Previous Examination Marksheet, Admission Receipt, Bonafide Certificate, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and Income Certificate (if applicable).
application_process: Apply online through the Maharashtra Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) Portal by completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application through the institution.
benefits: Scholarship assistance covering maintenance allowance, tuition fees, examination fees, and other admissible educational expenses as per Government norms.

implementing_agency: Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR)
tags: Research Fellowship, ICMR, JRF, Life Sciences, Social Sciences, PhD
brief_description: The ICMR Junior Research Fellowship examination provides opportunities for eligible candidates to pursue PhD and biomedical or social science research through ICMR-supported fellowships.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should possess a postgraduate degree in Life Sciences, Social Sciences, or related disciplines with at least 55% marks for General/OBC candidates and 50% marks for SC/ST/PwD candidates. The upper age limit is 28 years, with relaxation as per Government norms.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Passport-size Photograph, Educational Certificates and Mark Sheets, Age Proof, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Disability Certificate (if applicable), and other documents specified in the notification.
application_process: Apply online through the ICMR portal after publication of the official notification. Candidates are selected based on performance in the national-level ICMR-JRF examination.
benefits: JRF stipend of Rs.31,000 per month for the first two years, SRF stipend of Rs.35,000 per month after upgradation, annual contingency grant of Rs.20,000, and eligibility to pursue PhD research in approved institutions.

implementing_agency: Development Commissioner (Handicrafts), Ministry of Textiles, Government of India
tags: Handicrafts, Infrastructure, Technology Support, Marketing Hub, Common Facility Centre
brief_description: The scheme supports the development of infrastructure and technology facilities for the handicrafts sector to improve production, quality, marketing, and competitiveness.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible applicants include statutory bodies, apex cooperative societies, national-level societies, trusts, producer companies supported by the Office of Development Commissioner (Handicrafts), and other approved implementing agencies. Non-government implementing agencies should have a minimum audited annual turnover of Rs.1.5 crore during the previous three consecutive years.
documents_required: Registration Certificate, Memorandum and Articles of Association, Audited Financial Statements, Annual Reports, Detailed Project Report, Land Ownership or Lease Documents, Construction Plan, Cost Estimates, Feasibility Report, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed proposal along with supporting documents to the concerned Handicrafts Service Centre under the Office of the Development Commissioner (Handicrafts).
benefits: Financial assistance for establishment and strengthening of Urban Haats, Emporia, Marketing and Sourcing Hubs, Craft Villages, Common Facility Centres, Raw Material Depots, Handicraft Museums, and other approved infrastructure projects as per scheme norms.

implementing_agency: University Grants Commission (UGC)
tags: Post Doctoral Fellowship, Women, Research, UGC, Higher Education
brief_description: The scheme provides financial support to unemployed women holding a PhD degree to undertake advanced research in universities, colleges, and research institutions in India.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an unemployed woman with a PhD degree, not more than 55 years of age at the time of application, and meet the prescribed academic requirements. Age relaxation is available for reserved categories and persons with disabilities as per Government norms.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Age Proof, Academic Certificates and Degrees, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Disability Certificate (if applicable), Bank Account Details, and No Objection Certificate from the proposed research institution.
application_process: Apply online through the UGC Fellowship Portal following publication of the notification. Shortlisted candidates are selected through the process prescribed by UGC.
benefits: Fellowship of Rs.38,800 per month for the first two years and Rs.46,500 per month thereafter, annual contingency grant of Rs.50,000, HRA as per norms, and reader or escort assistance for eligible PwD candidates.

implementing_agency: Department of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India
tags: Agriculture, Food Security, Rice, Wheat, Pulses, Millets, Seed Distribution
brief_description: The National Food Security Mission aims to increase the production and productivity of rice, wheat, pulses, millets, and other notified crops through improved technologies and farm support interventions.
eligibility_criteria: All eligible farmers cultivating notified crops in approved districts may avail benefits under the mission. Priority is given to Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, women, small, and marginal farmers as per scheme guidelines.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Bank Account Details, Land Records or Cultivation Proof, Caste Certificate (if applicable), and other documents prescribed by the State Agriculture Department.
application_process: Eligible farmers may apply through the State Agriculture Department or designated implementing agencies. Beneficiary selection is carried out through a transparent process involving local authorities and agricultural institutions.
benefits: Support for quality seed distribution, demonstrations, cluster frontline demonstrations, farm machinery, nutrient management, pest management, seed production, water-carrying pipes, post-harvest technologies, and other agricultural interventions under approved NFSM components.

scheme_name: Nirman Shramik Ke Bachchon Hetu Utkrisht Khel Protsahan Yojana
implementing_agency: Chhattisgarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Sports, Financial Assistance, Construction Workers, Students, Incentive
brief_description: The scheme provides financial incentives to children of registered construction workers who achieve excellence in recognized sports competitions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the child of a registered construction worker in Chhattisgarh. The child must have achieved recognition or won medals in district, divisional, state, or national-level sports competitions recognized by the Sports Department.
documents_required: Construction Worker Registration Certificate, School Certificate of the Child, Sports Achievement or Medal Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the Chhattisgarh Labour Department portal by selecting the relevant welfare scheme, entering registration details, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial incentive of Rs.1,000 for district-level achievement, Rs.2,000 for divisional-level achievement, Rs.5,000 for state-level achievement, and higher assistance as prescribed for national-level achievements.

implementing_agency: Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), Government of India
tags: Research, Technology Translation, Innovation, SERB, Award, Patents
brief_description: SERB-TETRA supports researchers in translating laboratory research and patented innovations into commercially viable technologies through industry collaboration.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a Principal Investigator or Co-Investigator of an ongoing or recently completed SERB-funded project with translational potential and associated intellectual property. The applicant must hold a regular academic or research position in a recognized institution in India and possess a PhD, MD, MS, MDS, MVSc, or equivalent qualification.
documents_required: Biodata, Patent Details, Endorsement Letter from the Head of Institution, Technology Transfer Documents, Support Letter from Industry or Startup Partner (if applicable), and Plagiarism Undertaking.
application_process: Apply online through the SERB Online Portal by registering, completing the proposal form, uploading supporting documents, and submitting the proposal before the prescribed deadline.
benefits: Funding support of up to Rs.15 lakh per year for technology translation, prototype development, industry collaboration, and commercialization activities.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa
tags: Culture, Art, Award, Lifetime Achievement, Music, Dance, Drama
brief_description: The Goa State Cultural Award honours eminent artists for their lifetime achievements and outstanding contributions to the field of art and culture in Goa.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an artist aged 60 years or above, have made exceptional contributions to art and culture, and have contributed to the cultural field in Goa for at least 10 years.
documents_required: Age Proof, Aadhaar Card, Biodata, Passport-size Photographs, Documentary Evidence of Artistic Contributions, Certificates of Achievement, Bank Account Details, and Recommendation Letter.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa within the notified application period.
benefits: Award comprising a shawl, certificate of appreciation, memento, and cash prize of Rs.1,00,000. Awardees may also receive monthly assistance under applicable artist welfare schemes.

scheme_name: Self-Employment Scheme for Dependents of Prisoners
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala
tags: Self Employment, Financial Assistance, Prisoners, Rehabilitation, BPL Families
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for self-employment and rehabilitation of economically disadvantaged dependents of prisoners in Kerala.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Kerala belonging to a BPL family. The applicant should be a dependent of a prisoner who has undergone imprisonment of at least five years. Assistance is available only during the imprisonment period and only once for the same purpose.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Mobile Number, Passport-size Photograph, Ration Card, Ward Member or Councillor Certificate, Project Proposal, Bank Account Details, Affidavit from Jail Superintendent, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Suneethi Portal by completing one-time registration, updating profile details, selecting the scheme, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.30,000 for self-employment and rehabilitation activities. Assistance is provided only once to an eligible beneficiary.

scheme_name: Vasantrao Naik Merit Awards for VJNT Students
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Special Assistance, Government of Maharashtra
tags: Merit Award, VJNT, Education, Board Examination, Scholarship
brief_description: The scheme rewards meritorious VJNT students who secure first rank at the State or Board level in Class 10 and Class 12 examinations.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Vimukta Jati and Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) category and must have secured first rank at the State level or first rank in a Board examination in Class 10 or Class 12.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Maharashtra Domicile Certificate, VJNT Caste Certificate, Family Income Certificate, Class 10 or Class 12 Mark Sheet, Passport-size Photograph, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the MahaDBT Portal by registering, completing the profile, selecting the scheme, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Rs.1,00,000, trophy, and certificate for State-level first rank holders (one boy and one girl) in SSC and HSC examinations. Rs.51,000, trophy, and certificate for Board-level first rank holders (one boy and one girl) in SSC and HSC examinations.

scheme_name: Vocational Training of Destitute Women and Adolescent Girls
implementing_agency: Welfare Training Institute, Takyel, Social Welfare Department, Government of Manipur
tags: Women, Girls, Skill Development, Vocational Training, Social Welfare
category: Skills & Employment, Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women & Child Development
brief_description: The scheme provides six-month vocational training courses to destitute women and adolescent girls to enhance employability and self-reliance.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a domicile of Manipur, be a destitute woman or adolescent girl aged between 18 and 38 years. For computer courses, the minimum educational qualification is Class 12 pass.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Domicile Certificate, Age Proof, Educational Qualification Certificate (for computer course applicants), Passport-size Photograph, and other documents prescribed by the Welfare Training Institute.
application_process: Collect the application form from the Welfare Training Institute, Takyel or download it from the Social Welfare Department website. Submit the completed application form through the concerned District Social Welfare Officer.
benefits: Free six-month vocational training in Tailoring and Cutting, Embroidery, and Computer courses.

implementing_agency: Department of Agriculture, Government of West Bengal
tags: Agriculture, Farmer Welfare, Income Support, Death Benefit, Sharecropper
brief_description: Krishak Bandhu is a flagship farmer welfare scheme providing assured annual income support and financial assistance to the families of deceased farmers and sharecroppers.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of West Bengal and a farmer or recorded sharecropper cultivating agricultural land. For death benefit assistance, the deceased farmer or sharecropper should have been between 18 and 60 years of age, and the legal heir is eligible to claim the benefit.
documents_required: Land Records (RoR/Barga Record/Forest Patta), Voter ID Card, Aadhaar Card, Bank Passbook or Cancelled Cheque, Passport-size Photograph, Mobile Number, Death Certificate (for death benefit claims), and Legal Heir Certificate where applicable.
application_process: Apply through Gram Panchayat offices, Duare Sarkar camps, or the Agriculture Department. Applications are verified by the Assistant Director of Agriculture and benefits are transferred through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).
benefits: Annual income support of Rs.10,000 per acre for farmers with one acre or more cultivable land and Rs.4,000 per year for farmers with less than one acre. Legal heirs of deceased farmers or sharecroppers receive a one-time death benefit of Rs.2,00,000.

implementing_agency: Border Areas Development Department, Government of Meghalaya
tags: Border Area Development, Infrastructure, Rural Development, Social Welfare, Meghalaya
brief_description: The programme supports socio-economic and infrastructure development in villages located along the Meghalaya-Assam inter-state border.
eligibility_criteria: Residents of Meghalaya living in notified villages located within the designated border development area are eligible to benefit from projects implemented under the programme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Permanent Residence Certificate, Income Certificate, Certificate from Border Area Development Officer, Bank Account Details, and other documents as required.
application_process: Development proposals are prepared and implemented through the Border Areas Development Department, Deputy Commissioners, Village Councils, and concerned line departments based on local requirements.
benefits: Development of roads, water supply systems, public distribution infrastructure, education and healthcare facilities, livelihood opportunities, tourism infrastructure, environmental conservation projects, and other community assets.

scheme_name: Grant-in-Aid to Cultural Organizations (Maintenance / Regular Grants)
implementing_agency: Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa
tags: Culture, Grant-in-Aid, Cultural Organizations, Financial Assistance, Arts Promotion
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered cultural organizations for promoting cultural activities and developing artistic talent in Goa.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant organization should be registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860 or the Indian Trusts Act and actively engaged in cultural activities. Preference is given to institutions involved in cultural education and training.
documents_required: Registration Certificate, Constitution or Memorandum of Association, Identity Proof of Office Bearers, Activity Reports, List of Managing Committee Members, Audited Financial Statements, Utilization Certificate, Bank Account Details, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa.
benefits: Grant-in-aid up to 80% of eligible expenditure incurred during the previous financial year, subject to category-wise ceilings ranging from Rs.10,000 to Rs.3,00,000.

scheme_name: Chief Ministergi Artistesini Tengbang (CMAT) - Festival Assistance Scheme
implementing_agency: Department of Art and Culture, Government of Manipur
tags: Artists, Culture, Festival Assistance, Travel Support, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to artists and artist groups from Manipur for participation in recognized national and international cultural festivals.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Manipur and an artist or artist group. Applicants should have participated in recognized state and national cultural festivals and should not be fully sponsored by another organization for the same purpose.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Invitation Letter from Festival Organizers, Artist Profile, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, Performance Records, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Department of Art and Culture, Government of Manipur for consideration by the Screening and Selection Committee.
benefits: Financial assistance towards travel, journey expenses, and festival participation costs for eligible artists participating in national and international festivals.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Purchase of Barrier Net and its Accessories
implementing_agency: Department of Fisheries, Government of Goa
tags: Fishermen, Fishing Nets, Subsidy, Financial Assistance, Fisheries
brief_description: The scheme provides subsidy assistance to traditional fishermen for the purchase of barrier nets and related accessories used for fishing activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident fisherman of Goa, possess a valid Barrier Net Registration Certificate and Licence issued by the Department of Fisheries, and should not have availed assistance under the scheme within the prescribed period.
documents_required: Original Invoice from Authorized Dealer, Barrier Net Registration Certificate, Licence Certificate issued by the Department of Fisheries, Aadhaar Card, Undertaking in Prescribed Format, Bank Passbook Copy or Mandate Form, and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Obtain the application form from the Department of Fisheries, fill in the required details, attach the prescribed documents, and submit it to the Department of Fisheries or the nearest Fisheries Office.
benefits: 50% subsidy up to Rs.30,000 on a unit cost of Rs.60,000 for General Category beneficiaries. 60% subsidy up to Rs.36,000 on a unit cost of Rs.60,000 for SC/ST/Women beneficiaries.

implementing_agency: Finance, Planning and Investment Department, Government of Arunachal Pradesh
tags: Innovation, Infrastructure, Development, Community Participation, Technology
brief_description: The scheme promotes innovative district-level projects that address critical infrastructure gaps, improve public services, and encourage community participation in development.
eligibility_criteria: Projects should address critical district-level needs, demonstrate innovation, generate community impact, create employment opportunities, support sustainability, and utilize modern or innovative technologies.
documents_required: Detailed Project Report (DPR), Cost Estimates, Quotations (where applicable), Sustainability Plan, Community Participation Details, Employment Impact Assessment, and other documents prescribed by the Department.
application_process: Eligible project proposals are submitted by District Administrations to the Finance, Planning and Investment Department for evaluation and approval based on innovation, planning, budget, social impact, and sustainability criteria.
benefits: Financial support for innovative infrastructure and development projects that address critical district needs and promote sustainable community development.

implementing_agency: State Horticulture Mission, Government of Uttarakhand
tags: Beekeeping, Honey Production, Entrepreneurship, Training, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme promotes beekeeping and honey production by providing subsidies, training, and support for establishing honey production units in rural areas of Uttarakhand.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be between 18 and 55 years of age, be a resident of Uttarakhand, possess owned or leased agricultural land, and have completed beekeeping training from KVIC, NABARD, KVK, or another approved training institution.
documents_required: Application Form, Aadhaar Card, Residence Proof, Identity Proof, Land Ownership or Lease Documents, Project Report, Bank Passbook Copy, PAN Card, Small and Marginal Farmer Certificate (if applicable), Caste Certificate (if applicable), and Self Declaration.
application_process: Download and complete the prescribed application form, attach the required documents, and submit the application to the concerned District Horticulture Office.
benefits: Up to 80% subsidy for beekeeping activities, financial assistance for bee boxes and colonies, free or subsidized training programs, and support for honey production units under the Madhugram initiative.

scheme_name: Subsidy for Asset Creation for Intellectual Property - Patent Registration
implementing_agency: Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Patent, Intellectual Property, MSME, Innovation, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme encourages MSMEs to create intellectual property by providing reimbursement support for patent registration expenses incurred in India and abroad.
eligibility_criteria: New and existing Micro, Small, and Medium Manufacturing Enterprises located in Tamil Nadu are eligible under the scheme.
documents_required: UDYAM Registration Certificate, Patent Registration Certificate, Trademark or GI Registration Documents (if applicable), Expenditure Statements, Bills and Vouchers, Bank Payment Proof, Loan Sanction Letter (if applicable), Invoices, Receipts, and Advance Stamped Receipt.
application_process: Apply online through the MSME Tamil Nadu Portal by registering, selecting the scheme, filling the application form, uploading supporting documents, and submitting the application within six months of obtaining patent registration.
benefits: 75% reimbursement of patent registration expenses, including first-time maintenance fees, subject to a maximum assistance of Rs.3,00,000 per patent.

implementing_agency: National Institute of Electronics and Information Technology (NIELIT)
tags: Internship, Students, Electronics, Information Technology, Skill Development
brief_description: The NIELIT Internship Programme provides practical work experience and industry exposure to students under the guidance of experienced mentors and supervisors.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian citizen studying in a recognized institution and have secured at least 60% marks in the last qualifying examination. Eligible candidates include diploma, undergraduate, postgraduate, integrated degree, and professional course students.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Educational Certificates and Mark Sheets, Recommendation Letter from Institution, No Objection Certificate (NOC), Student Identity Card, Proof of Enrollment, Identity Proof, Age Proof, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Submit the application through the sponsoring institution or directly to NIELIT along with the required documents and recommendation letter. Shortlisted candidates may be called for a test or interview.
benefits: Monthly stipend of Rs.5,000 for Science and Technology interns and Rs.3,000 for Non-Science and Technology interns. Internship completion certificate is provided upon successful completion.

implementing_agency: Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare, Government of Puducherry
tags: Fishermen, Fishing Ban, Relief Assistance, Welfare, BPL Families
category: Agriculture, Rural & Environment, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: The scheme provides relief assistance to fishermen families affected by fishing ban periods and natural calamities, helping compensate for loss of livelihood during such periods.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident fisherman of Puducherry belonging to a BPL family. The beneficiary should not be residing abroad during the assistance period. No family member should be a regular government employee or pensioner. Ownership or non-ownership of fishing boats does not affect eligibility.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Fisherman Identity Card, BPL/Ration Card, Residence Proof, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and other documents prescribed by the Department.
application_process: Obtain the prescribed application form from the concerned Fisheries Office, fill in the required details, attach supporting documents, and submit it to the designated authority in the respective region.
benefits: 100 kg of rice and cash assistance of Rs.750 per fishermen family during the notified fishing ban period.

scheme_name: Safai Karmakar Gambhir Bimari Chikitsa Sahayata Yojana
implementing_agency: Chhattisgarh Unorganised Workers State Social Security Board
tags: Medical Assistance, Serious Illness, Health Support, Unorganised Workers, Social Security
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for treatment of serious illnesses suffered by registered unorganised workers in Chhattisgarh.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered unorganised worker between 18 and 60 years of age, registered with the Board for at least 90 days, and enrolled as a beneficiary under the relevant provisions of the Act. Assistance can be availed only once and is not available to beneficiaries receiving similar assistance under parallel schemes.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Registration Card, Medical Certificate from Competent Medical Authority, Disease Details Report, Estimated Treatment Expenditure Certificate, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the Chhattisgarh Labour Department portal after registration with the Chhattisgarh Unorganised Workers State Social Security Board. Eligible beneficiaries can submit the scheme application online along with supporting documents.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.50,000 for treatment of serious illnesses such as kidney disease, cancer, sickle cell anemia, heart disease, AIDS, and paralysis.

scheme_name: Phase-II Grid Connected Rooftop Solar Programme
implementing_agency: Jammu and Kashmir Energy Development Agency (JAKEDA)
target_beneficiaries: Residential Electricity Consumers and Housing Societies
tags: Solar Energy, Rooftop Solar, Renewable Energy, Subsidy, Infrastructure
brief_description: The programme promotes installation of grid-connected rooftop solar systems and provides Central Financial Assistance to residential consumers for adopting solar energy.
eligibility_criteria: Residential electricity consumers and Group Housing Societies with valid electricity connections are eligible. Applicants must have permission to use the rooftop where the solar system is proposed to be installed.
documents_required: Latest Electricity Bill, Aadhaar Card, Mobile Number, Email ID, Bank Account Details, Cancelled Cheque, Rooftop Ownership or Authorization Proof, and other documents required by DISCOM.
application_process: Apply through the National Rooftop Solar Portal, obtain technical feasibility approval from the DISCOM, install the solar system through an empanelled vendor, complete net-metering formalities, and submit bank details for subsidy disbursement.
benefits: Central Financial Assistance of 40% for rooftop solar systems up to 3 kW and 20% for additional capacity above 3 kW, subject to applicable MNRE guidelines.

implementing_agency: Department of Village and Ward Secretariats, Government of Andhra Pradesh
tags: Street Vendors, Working Capital, Artisans, Interest-Free Loan, Self Employment
brief_description: The scheme provides interest-free working capital assistance to street vendors and traditional artisans to reduce dependence on informal money lenders and strengthen livelihoods.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Andhra Pradesh, above 18 years of age, belong to an eligible low-income household, and be engaged in street vending, petty trade, or traditional artisan activities. The applicant must satisfy the income and landholding criteria prescribed by the Government.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Proof, Income Certificate, Bank Account Details, Mobile Number, Family Identification Details, and other documents prescribed by the Government.
application_process: Eligible beneficiaries are identified through Village and Ward Secretariats. Applications are processed through the designated government platform and verified before sanction of assistance.
benefits: Interest-free working capital loan of up to Rs.10,000 for eligible street vendors and traditional artisans.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa
tags: Art, Culture, Award, Lifetime Achievement, Recognition
brief_description: The award recognizes senior artists who have made outstanding lifetime contributions to the field of art and culture in Goa.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an individual artist aged 60 years or above and should have made significant contributions in his or her field of art. The artist should have contributed to the cultural field in Goa for at least 10 years.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Age Proof, Aadhaar Card, Biodata, Bank Account Details, Performance Photographs, Performance Certificates, Recommendation Letter, and Identity Proof.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Directorate of Art and Culture, Government of Goa within the notified application period.
benefits: Cash award of Rs.25,000 along with a shawl, shrifal, and certificate of appreciation. Up to 30 artists may be selected annually subject to availability of funds.

implementing_agency: Department of Panchayat Raj and Rural Development, Government of Telangana
tags: Rural Development, Gram Panchayat, Village Planning, Infrastructure, Community Participation
brief_description: Grama Jyothi is a flagship rural development programme aimed at strengthening Gram Panchayats and promoting comprehensive village development through participatory planning and convergence of government services.
eligibility_criteria: The scheme is implemented through Gram Panchayats and benefits all residents of participating rural villages in Telangana.
documents_required: Not Applicable.
application_process: Development plans are prepared and implemented through Gram Panchayats with the participation of villagers, functional committees, and line departments.
benefits: Improved delivery of services related to water and sanitation, nutrition, education, agriculture, infrastructure, poverty reduction, resource management, and rural development.

implementing_agency: Chhattisgarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Construction Workers, Tools, Carpenter, Electrician, Plumber, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for purchasing work tools required by registered construction workers engaged in different trades.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered construction worker in Chhattisgarh, at least 18 years of age, and eligible to receive assistance for only one trade under the scheme.
documents_required: Training Certificate, Registration Card, Bank Passbook Copy, Aadhaar Card, and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Obtain the application form from the concerned Labour Office, fill in the required details, attach supporting documents, and submit it for verification and approval.
benefits: Financial assistance for purchasing trade-specific work tools required for occupations such as mason, electrician, plumber, carpenter, painter, and other construction-related trades.

scheme_name: Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme for Middle Income Group (CLSS-MIG)
implementing_agency: National Housing Bank (NHB), Housing and Urban Development Corporation (HUDCO), and State Bank of India (SBI)
tags: Housing, Home Loan, Interest Subsidy, CLSS, PMAY
brief_description: The scheme provides interest subsidy on housing loans to Middle Income Group families for the purchase, construction, or repurchase of houses under PMAY (Urban).
eligibility_criteria: The beneficiary family should not own a pucca house anywhere in India and should not have availed benefits under any other central housing scheme. MIG-I households must have annual income between Rs.6 lakh and Rs.12 lakh, while MIG-II households must have annual income between Rs.12 lakh and Rs.18 lakh.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Residence Proof, Income Proof, Bank Account Details, Property Documents, and other documents required by the lending institution.
application_process: Apply through participating banks, housing finance companies, or other approved lending institutions. After loan approval and verification, the interest subsidy is credited directly to the loan account.
benefits: Interest subsidy of 4% on housing loans up to Rs.9 lakh for MIG-I beneficiaries and 3% on housing loans up to Rs.12 lakh for MIG-II beneficiaries, for a maximum tenure of 20 years.

implementing_agency: Department of Higher Education, Government of Himachal Pradesh
tags: Scholarship, Girl Students, Education, Financial Assistance, Valmiki Community
brief_description: The scheme provides scholarship assistance to girl students belonging to Valmiki families engaged in unclean occupations to support higher education.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Himachal Pradesh, belong to a Valmiki family engaged in an unclean occupation, be a girl student, and have secured admission to a post-matric course in a recognized institution.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Himachal Bonafide Certificate, Previous Examination Mark Sheets, Student Bank Account Statement, and Certificate proving the parent or guardian is engaged in an unclean occupation.
application_process: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application before the prescribed deadline.
benefits: Scholarship of Rs.18,000 per annum for eligible girl students pursuing higher education.

scheme_name: Free Coaching for Students with Disabilities
implementing_agency: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), Government of India
tags: Disability, Free Coaching, Competitive Examinations, Education, Scholarship
brief_description: The scheme provides free coaching support to students with disabilities preparing for competitive examinations and admissions to higher educational institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should have at least 40% disability, be preparing for competitive examinations or higher education entrance tests, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.6 lakh, and should not be receiving similar benefits under another central government scheme.
documents_required: Disability Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Previous Qualification Mark Sheets, Income Certificate, and other documents prescribed by the implementing organization.
application_process: Eligible students should apply through empanelled coaching institutions or voluntary organizations approved by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities.
benefits: Free coaching, study material, and financial assistance including monthly stipend of Rs.2,500 for local students and Rs.5,000 for outstation students.

implementing_agency: Women and Child Development Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
tags: Orphans, Financial Assistance, Education, Skill Development, After Care
category: Social Welfare & Empowerment, Education & Learning, Women & Child Development
brief_description: The scheme provides financial, educational, and rehabilitation support to orphan children, abandoned children, and care leavers transitioning from childcare institutions into independent life.
eligibility_criteria: For After Care: Children residing in childcare institutions for at least five years and above 18 years of age. Orphan and abandoned children may receive relaxation in residency requirements. Benefits are available up to 24 years of age. For Sponsorship: Children below 18 years of age whose parents have died and who are living with relatives or guardians.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Birth Certificate, Death Certificate of Parents, Educational Certificates, Bank Account Details, Residence Proof, and other documents prescribed by the Women and Child Development Department.
application_process: Eligible beneficiaries are identified through Child Care Institutions and District Child Protection Units. Applications are processed through the official State Child Protection Society portal and approved by the competent committee.
benefits: Rs.5,000 per month during internship, vocational training, or skill development programs. Financial assistance of Rs.5,000 to Rs.8,000 per month for higher education. Sponsorship assistance of Rs.4,000 per month for eligible orphan children living with relatives or guardians. Ayushman health coverage is also provided.

implementing_agency: Kerala Social Security Mission
tags: Healthcare, Children, Chronic Diseases, Medical Assistance, BPL
brief_description: The scheme provides free treatment and financial assistance to children suffering from severe chronic illnesses and congenital disorders whose families are unable to afford treatment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Kerala, below 18 years of age, belong to a financially disadvantaged family, and be suffering from eligible chronic diseases such as kidney disorders, cardiovascular diseases, cerebral palsy, hemophilia, thalassemia, sickle cell anemia, orthopedic deformities, neuro-developmental disabilities, congenital anomalies, or serious accident-related conditions requiring surgery.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or Identity Proof, Age Proof, Income Certificate, BPL Card (if applicable), Ration Card, Residence Certificate, Passport-size Photograph, and Medical Records.
application_process: Beneficiaries may approach the scheme counsellor at empanelled hospitals or contact the concerned District Social Justice Officer. No separate application is generally required, and eligibility is assessed by designated counsellors.
benefits: Free treatment in empanelled hospitals and initial financial assistance up to Rs.50,000. Additional assistance may be sanctioned based on medical necessity and recommendations of the concerned hospital authorities.

scheme_name: Mission for Integrated Development of Horticulture (MIDH)
implementing_agency: State and Union Territory Horticulture Departments
target_beneficiaries: Farmers, Farmer Producer Organizations, Registered Societies, Government Agencies, and Agribusiness Enterprises
tags: Horticulture, Farmers, Nurseries, Polyhouse, Greenhouse, Post Harvest Infrastructure
brief_description: MIDH is a centrally sponsored scheme that promotes holistic development of the horticulture sector through production enhancement, infrastructure creation, post-harvest management, and market development.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers, farmer groups, Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), registered societies, government agencies, and eligible agribusiness entities engaged in horticulture activities are eligible as per component-specific guidelines.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Land Ownership Documents, Bank Account Details, Photographs, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Project Proposal, and other documents required under specific MIDH components.
application_process: Interested beneficiaries should apply through the District or Block Horticulture Officer. Applications are evaluated by the State Horticulture Mission and approved as per MIDH guidelines.
benefits: Financial assistance for establishment of nurseries, tissue culture units, orchards, protected cultivation structures, micro-irrigation systems, beekeeping units, horticulture mechanization, post-harvest infrastructure, cold chain facilities, and market development activities.

implementing_agency: Department of Women and Child Development and Social Welfare, Government of West Bengal
tags: Girl Child, Scholarship, Education, Child Marriage Prevention, Women Empowerment
category: Education & Learning, Women & Child Development, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: Kanyashree Prakalpa is a conditional cash transfer scheme that encourages girls to remain in education and delays child marriage by providing financial assistance to eligible adolescent girls.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an unmarried girl residing in West Bengal, generally aged between 13 and 18 years, studying in a recognized educational institution, and belonging to a family with annual income not exceeding Rs.1.2 lakh. Certain income restrictions may be relaxed for girls with special needs or those residing in child care institutions.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Birth Certificate or Age Proof, Income Certificate, School or College Enrollment Certificate, Bank Account Details, Residence Proof, Passport-size Photograph, and Declaration of Unmarried Status.
application_process: Apply online through the Kanyashree Portal or through the educational institution where the applicant is enrolled. The application is verified by institutional and government authorities before approval.
benefits: Annual scholarship of Rs.1,000 for eligible girls aged 13–18 years and a one-time grant of Rs.25,000 upon attaining 18 years of age, subject to fulfillment of scheme conditions.

scheme_name: Tripura Scheme for Incentive to Girl Child
implementing_agency: Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura
tags: Girl Child, Financial Assistance, BPL Families, Women and Child Welfare
category: Women & Child Development, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly financial assistance to girl children born in BPL families to support their welfare and development.
eligibility_criteria: Up to two girl children in a BPL family who were born on or after 01 June 2009 are eligible. Benefits are available until the child attains 15 years of age.
documents_required: Birth Certificate of the Child, BPL Card, Aadhaar Card of Parent or Guardian, Residence Proof, Bank or Post Office Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and other documents prescribed by the Department.
application_process: Applications may be submitted through the concerned Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) along with supporting documents for verification and approval.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.300 per month per eligible girl child until she attains 15 years of age. A maximum of two girl children per family can receive benefits under the scheme.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Employment, Labour Department, Government of West Bengal
tags: Employment, Financial Assistance, Job Seekers, Skill Development, Placement
brief_description: Yuvashree Prakalpa provides financial assistance to unemployed youth registered with the Employment Bank to improve employability and support skill development.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of West Bengal, unemployed, registered with the Employment Bank, have passed at least Class 8, be between 18 and 45 years of age, and should not have availed financial assistance or loans under any State or Central Government self-employment scheme. Only one member of a family is eligible.
documents_required: Application Form, Unemployment Certificate from a Group-A Officer, Self Declaration, Age Proof, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Disability Certificate (if applicable), Educational Certificates, Ration Card or Voter ID Card, and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Register on the Employment Bank portal, complete the enrollment process, obtain Employment Bank registration, and submit the prescribed application form with supporting documents through the concerned Employment Exchange.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.1,500 per month, access to skill development programs, employment guidance, placement support, and opportunities for self-employment training.

implementing_agency: Backward Classes Welfare and Tribal Development Department, Government of West Bengal
tags: Scholarship, Scheduled Caste, Students, Education, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The Shikshashree Scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste day scholar students studying in Classes V to VIII in recognized schools of West Bengal.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category, be a day scholar studying in Classes V to VIII in a Government, Government-aided, or Government-recognized school in West Bengal, and have annual family income not exceeding Rs.2.5 lakh.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Age Proof, Family Income Certificate, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, and Educational Documents.
application_process: Apply online through the OASIS Scholarship Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Annual financial assistance of Rs.750 for Classes V, VI, and VII, and Rs.800 for Class VIII.

implementing_agency: Department of Social Welfare, Government of Jammu and Kashmir
target_beneficiaries: Girl Children from Economically Weaker Families
tags: Girl Child, Financial Assistance, Social Welfare, Women Empowerment
category: Women & Child Development, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: The scheme provides long-term financial security to girl children through government-funded deposits to promote the welfare and empowerment of girls.
eligibility_criteria: The girl child should be born on or after 01 April 2015, be a resident of Jammu and Kashmir, and belong to a family with annual income below Rs.75,000.
documents_required: Application Form, Aadhaar Card, Birth Certificate of the Child, Residence Proof, Income Certificate, Parent or Guardian Identity Proof, Bank Account Details, and CDPO Recommendation Letter.
application_process: Eligible applicants should apply through the concerned Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) with the required documents for verification and approval.
benefits: Government contribution of Rs.1,000 per month for 14 years under a recurring deposit account, followed by a cumulative term deposit phase, creating a substantial maturity amount for the beneficiary upon completion.

implementing_agency: Department of Information Technology, Government of Goa
tags: Quality Certification, IT Industry, Reimbursement, MSME, Certification
brief_description: The scheme reimburses expenses incurred by eligible Information Technology units in obtaining industry-standard quality certifications.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a new or existing Information Technology unit operating in Goa as a proprietorship, partnership firm, LLP, private limited company, or public limited company. The unit must have incurred expenditure on approved quality certifications.
documents_required: Aadhaar-linked Bank Account Details, Registration Certificate of the Unit, Quality Certification Documents, Invoices and Payment Proofs, PAN Card, GST Registration (if applicable), and other documents prescribed by the Department.
application_process: Apply online through the Goa Online Portal by registering, selecting the scheme under IT Services, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Reimbursement of expenses incurred for obtaining approved quality certifications, subject to a maximum limit of Rs.5,00,000 per unit.

scheme_name: Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme for Scheduled Castes and Others
implementing_agency: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
target_beneficiaries: Scheduled Caste Students and Children of Persons Engaged in Hazardous Occupations
tags: Scholarship, Scheduled Caste, Students, Education, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Caste students and children of persons engaged in specified unclean or hazardous occupations to support pre-matric education and reduce dropout rates.
eligibility_criteria: For Component 1, applicants should belong to the Scheduled Caste category and be studying in Classes IX or X, with annual family income not exceeding Rs.2.5 lakh. For Component 2, applicants should be studying in Classes I to X and be children of manual scavengers, tanners, flayers, waste pickers, or persons engaged in hazardous cleaning occupations.
documents_required: Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Academic Certificates, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Domicile Certificate, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) by registering, completing the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Annual scholarship assistance for day scholars and hostellers studying in eligible classes, with additional allowance for students with disabilities as per scheme norms.

implementing_agency: Department of Higher Education, Government of Chhattisgarh
tags: BPL, College Students, Graduate, Postgraduate, Scholarship, Education
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to students from Below Poverty Line (BPL) families pursuing undergraduate and postgraduate studies in Government colleges of Chhattisgarh.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be studying in a Government college under the Higher Education Department, Chhattisgarh, belong to a BPL family, should not be receiving benefits under another scholarship scheme, must pass annual examinations in the first attempt, and maintain satisfactory attendance.
documents_required: Educational Certificates, Aadhaar Card, Bank Account Details, Mobile Number, Email ID, BPL Card, Passport-size Photograph, and Disability Certificate (if applicable).
application_process: Apply through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) by registering, completing the scholarship application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application for institutional and state-level verification.
benefits: Rs.300 per month for undergraduate students and Rs.500 per month for postgraduate students for up to 10 months in an academic year. Scholarship amount is transferred directly to the beneficiary's bank account through DBT.

implementing_agency: Women and Child Development Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
tags: Girl Child, Education, Financial Assistance, Women Empowerment, Social Welfare
category: Women & Child Development, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: Ladli Laxmi Yojana promotes the welfare, education, and empowerment of girl children by providing long-term financial assistance and educational incentives.
eligibility_criteria: The girl child should be born on or after 01 January 2006, be a resident of Madhya Pradesh, be registered at the local Anganwadi Centre, and belong to a family that satisfies the prescribed conditions including family planning norms and non-payment of income tax.
documents_required: Girl Child's Samagra ID, Family Samagra ID, Photograph of the Girl Child with Parents, Family Planning Certificate (where applicable), and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Ladli Laxmi Portal by submitting the self-declaration, entering Samagra IDs, filling in beneficiary details, uploading documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Government deposits Rs.6,000 annually for five years in the beneficiary's name. Educational incentives are provided at different stages of schooling, along with assistance for higher education. Eligible beneficiaries receive a maturity benefit of up to Rs.1,00,000 upon fulfilling scheme conditions.

implementing_agency: Kerala State Council for Science, Technology and Environment (KSCSTE)
tags: Post Doctoral Fellowship, Women Scientists, Research, Science, Career Break
brief_description: The programme supports women scientists who have experienced career breaks by providing post-doctoral fellowship opportunities to re-enter scientific research.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a woman, permanent resident of Kerala, unemployed due to career break, possess a PhD in Science or Engineering, be below 40 years of age (relaxation applicable for reserved categories), and have at least one SCI-indexed publication or patent.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Photograph, Age Proof, Kerala Residence Proof, Postgraduate and PhD Certificates, Research Experience Certificates, Research Publications, Career Break Certificate, Biodata, Declaration Form, Consent from Scientist Mentor, and Endorsement from Institution Head.
application_process: Apply online through the KSCSTE portal. Applications undergo eligibility screening, peer review, and presentation before the Research Advisory Committee before final selection.
benefits: Post-doctoral fellowship of Rs.45,000 per month plus 10% HRA and an annual contingency grant of Rs.75,000 for a maximum period of two years.

scheme_name: Research Fellowship in Sciences for Meritorious Students (RFSMS)
implementing_agency: University Grants Commission (UGC)
tags: Research Fellowship, PhD, Science, Higher Education, UGC
brief_description: The RFSMS scheme provides fellowship support to meritorious students pursuing PhD research in science subjects in UGC-recognized institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants should be registered for a PhD in Science subjects in Universities with Potential for Excellence, Centres with Potential for Excellence, Centres of Advanced Studies, or Departments of Special Assistance identified by UGC.
documents_required: PhD Registration Certificate, Academic Records, Research Proposal, Identity Proof, and other documents prescribed by UGC.
application_process: Eligible candidates should apply through their respective universities or as per the procedure notified by UGC from time to time.
benefits: Fellowship assistance for pursuing doctoral research in science disciplines as per UGC norms.

scheme_name: Bus Traveling Facility for Visually Impaired (Bus Concession Scheme)
implementing_agency: Delhi Transport Corporation (DTC)
tags: Disability, Visual Impairment, Free Bus Travel, DTC, Transport Concession
brief_description: The scheme provides free travel facilities in DTC buses to visually impaired persons residing in Delhi and concession benefits for escorts in eligible cases.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Delhi with at least 40% visual disability and possess a disability identification card issued through the competent authority. Escort concession is available for persons with 100% blindness.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or Identity Proof, Disability Certificate, SDM-issued Card, Residence Proof, and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Obtain the application form from the concerned DTC depot, submit the completed form with supporting documents, and collect the bus pass after verification and approval.
benefits: Free travel in DTC buses for visually impaired persons and 50% concession for an escort or attendant accompanying a person with 100% blindness.

implementing_agency: Department of Agriculture and Cooperation, Government of Assam
tags: Agriculture, MSP, Farmers, Procurement, Oilseeds, Pulses
brief_description: The Price Support Scheme (PSS) ensures remunerative prices to farmers by procuring eligible agricultural produce at the Minimum Support Price (MSP) whenever market prices fall below MSP.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a farmer residing in Assam and sell eligible agricultural produce at designated procurement centres established by the nodal procurement agency.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Identity Proof, Land Records or Cultivation Proof, Bank Account Details, Farmer Registration Details, and other documents prescribed by the procurement agency.
application_process: Eligible farmers can sell notified crops at designated procurement centres established by the nodal agency during procurement operations announced under the Price Support Scheme.
benefits: Procurement of eligible agricultural produce at Minimum Support Price (MSP), ensuring assured income and protection from market price fluctuations.

implementing_agency: District Deputy Director (Scheduled Caste Welfare), Government of Gujarat
tags: Scheduled Caste, Students, Uniform Assistance, Education, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for uniforms to Scheduled Caste students studying in primary schools, encouraging school attendance and educational participation.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category and be studying in Classes 1 to 8. There is no income limit under the scheme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, School Bonafide Certificate, Student Identity Proof, Bank Account Details, and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Apply through the concerned District Scheduled Caste Welfare Office or through the prescribed state scholarship and welfare portal as notified by the Government of Gujarat.
benefits: Financial assistance for purchase of school uniforms for eligible Scheduled Caste students studying in Classes 1 to 8.

implementing_agency: Startup Promotion Cell (SPC), Government of Goa
tags: Startup, Research and Development, Innovation, Reimbursement, Entrepreneurship
brief_description: The scheme reimburses eligible research and development expenses incurred by startups in Goa to promote innovation, technology development, and commercialization.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a Goa-certified startup with a valid Startup Promotion Cell certificate. The startup should be engaged in research and development activities, possess a working prototype, and employ eligible PhD-qualified personnel for at least 12 months where salary reimbursement is claimed.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card of Director or CEO, Startup Registration Certificate, Employment Contracts, Salary Slips, Bank Statements, Research Proposal, Research Work Documents, Proof of R&D Expenditure, and supporting documents prescribed by the Startup Promotion Cell.
application_process: Apply online through the Goa Startup Portal or submit the prescribed application form and supporting documents to the Startup Promotion Cell within six months of incurring the eligible expenditure.
benefits: Reimbursement of 50% of eligible R&D expenses, including salaries of PhD-qualified employees, for up to two years, subject to a maximum of Rs.5,00,000 per annum. Salary reimbursement component is capped at Rs.2,00,000.

implementing_agency: Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI)
tags: Internship, Food Safety, Students, Research, Public Health
brief_description: The FSSAI Internship Scheme provides students with practical exposure to food regulation, food safety administration, nutrition management, laboratory operations, legal affairs, and related domains.
