eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be pursuing a full-time graduate, postgraduate, or professional degree in Food Technology, Food Science, Nutrition, Biotechnology, Life Sciences, Public Health, Agriculture, Management, Law, Journalism, Information Technology, or related disciplines from a recognized institution.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Resume, No Objection Certificate (NOC) from Institution, Recommendation Letter, Character Certificate, Educational Qualification Documents, and Student Identity Card.
application_process: Apply online through the FSSAI Internship Portal by completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application within the prescribed timeline.
benefits: Monthly stipend of Rs.10,000 for deserving interns based on attendance and performance. Interns also receive internship certificates upon successful completion.

scheme_name: National Centre for Communication Security (NCCS) Internship Scheme
implementing_agency: National Centre for Communication Security (NCCS), Department of Telecommunications
tags: Internship, Cyber Security, Telecommunications, Engineering, Students
brief_description: The NCCS Internship Scheme provides students with exposure to communication security, telecom infrastructure security, source code analysis, hardware security, malware analysis, and cybersecurity research.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian student pursuing or having completed a degree in Electronics, Communication Engineering, Telecommunications, Information Technology, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or related disciplines with at least 60% marks from a recognized institution.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Identity Proof, Age Proof, Educational Certificates and Mark Sheets, Student Identity Card or Enrollment Proof, Recommendation Letter from Institution, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with CV and supporting documents in response to NCCS internship notifications. Shortlisted candidates may be called for screening or interview.
benefits: Category-I interns receive Rs.7,500 per month and Category-II interns receive Rs.15,000 per month as token remuneration. Successful interns receive an internship completion certificate.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Allied Cultural Activities
implementing_agency: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
target_beneficiaries: NGOs, Trusts, Registered Societies, Not-for-Profit Organizations
tags: Culture, Audio Visual Equipment, Cultural Infrastructure, NGO, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for procurement of audio-visual equipment to enhance cultural performances, festivals, and public cultural events across India.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered not-for-profit organization, trust, society, or not-for-profit company engaged in allied cultural activities for at least 3 years. The organization must obtain a recommendation from the concerned District Authority or State Government.
documents_required: Project Proposal, Registration Certificate, Memorandum of Association or Trust Deed, List of Governing Body Members, Audited Financial Statements of Last 3 Years, Income Tax Documents, Proof of Matching Contribution, Board Resolution, NGO Darpan Unique ID, PAN Card, Bank Authorization Letter, Recommendation Letter from District Authority, and Undertakings as prescribed under the scheme.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with all required documents to the Ministry of Culture through the National Science Centre. Applications are evaluated by an Expert Committee before approval.
benefits: Financial assistance up to 90% of the approved project cost. Maximum assistance is Rs.1 crore for audio projects and Rs.1.5 crore for audio-video projects, including taxes and operation and maintenance costs.

scheme_name: Pension to Indigent Sportspersons of Odisha
implementing_agency: Department of Sports and Youth Services, Government of Odisha
tags: Sports, Pension, Financial Assistance, Sportspersons, Social Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly pension to former sportspersons of Odisha who are living in indigent conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Odisha, have represented Odisha in recognized sporting disciplines, be at least 50 years of age for males or 40 years for females (relaxation applicable in special cases), and have annual income not exceeding Rs.50,000.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Birth Certificate, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Passbook, Income Certificate, Sports Achievement Certificates, Disability Certificate (if applicable), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Sports Odisha Portal by registering, completing the profile, uploading required documents, and submitting the pension application.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.3,000 to eligible sportspersons. Surviving spouses of deceased pensioners may also apply subject to scheme conditions.

implementing_agency: Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT)
tags: Internship, Foreign Trade, Public Policy, Economics, Law
brief_description: The DGFT Summer Internship Scheme provides students and researchers an opportunity to gain exposure to India's foreign trade policy formulation and implementation process.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian national with an excellent academic record, pursuing or having completed studies in Public Policy, Economics, Finance, Management, Law, or related disciplines, with at least 60% marks in graduation from a recognized institution.
documents_required: Curriculum Vitae (CV), Internship Application Form, Permission Letter from Institution (if applicable), Academic Records, and Identity Proof.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form and CV through email to internship-dgft@gov.in within the application period notified by DGFT.
benefits: Monthly stipend of Rs.10,000, internship experience at DGFT Headquarters in New Delhi, logistical support, and certificate upon successful completion.

implementing_agency: Department of Women and Child Development and Social Welfare, Government of West Bengal
tags: Disability Pension, Social Welfare, Financial Assistance, Differently Abled
brief_description: The Manabik Scheme provides monthly financial assistance to persons with disabilities who are unable to earn a livelihood due to disability.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of West Bengal for at least 10 years, possess a disability of 50% or more, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.1,00,000, and be declared unfit for physical work by the competent medical authority.
documents_required: Disability Certificate, Aadhaar Card, EPIC Card or Digital Ration Card, Bank Passbook, and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Obtain and submit the application form through Duare Sarkar Camps, Block Development Office, Sub-Divisional Office, or Controller of Vagrancy Office along with required documents.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.1,000 directly credited to the beneficiary's bank account through DBT.

scheme_name: Scheme of Financial Assistance for the Preservation and Development of Cultural Heritage of the Himalayas
implementing_agency: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
target_beneficiaries: NGOs, Trusts, Registered Societies, Colleges and Universities
tags: Cultural Heritage, Himalayas, Research, Documentation, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme supports preservation, protection, documentation, promotion, and dissemination of the cultural heritage of the Himalayan region through research, publications, training, and cultural activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered society, trust, college, university, or voluntary organization functioning for at least 3 years and possessing the necessary resources and expertise to undertake heritage-related projects.
documents_required: Registration Certificate, Memorandum of Association or Trust Deed, Audited Accounts of Last 3 Years, Annual Reports, Project Proposal, Research Personnel Details, PAN Card, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form with supporting documents through the concerned State Government or directly to the Ministry of Culture where applicable. Organizations must also be registered on NGO Darpan.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.10 lakh per project. Normally up to 75% of project cost is supported, while the remaining contribution is to be borne by the applicant organization or State Government as per scheme norms.

implementing_agency: Religious Trusts and Endowments Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
tags: Pilgrimage, Senior Citizens, Religious Tourism, Tirth Darshan
brief_description: The scheme provides eligible senior citizens of Madhya Pradesh with a one-time opportunity to undertake pilgrimage to selected religious destinations across India free of cost.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh. Male applicants should be 60 years or above and female applicants 58 years or above. Persons with more than 60% disability are exempt from the age limit. Applicant should not be an income tax payer and should not have previously availed benefits under the scheme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or Voter ID Card, Residence Proof, Passport-size Photograph, Age Proof, Disability Certificate (if applicable), and Medical Fitness Certificate.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents at the nearest Tehsil or Sub-Tehsil office within the notified period. Selection is made by the district administration through prescribed procedures.
benefits: Free pilgrimage to selected religious destinations including travel, accommodation, food, drinking water, and related arrangements through IRCTC.

implementing_agency: Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Government of Odisha
tags: Education Assistance, Scholarship, Construction Workers, Students
brief_description: The scheme provides educational assistance to children of registered building and construction workers to support their studies from Class 6 onwards.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a child of a registered building and other construction worker who has completed at least one year of registration with the Board. Assistance is available for a maximum of two children per family. The worker must have active registration and up-to-date contributions.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card of Student, Photograph, Previous Marksheet, Aadhaar Card of Registered Parent, Construction Worker ID Card, Bank Passbook, Educational Institution ID Card or Admission Proof, Residence Certificate, Caste Certificate (if applicable), and Disability Certificate (if applicable).
application_process: Apply through the Odisha State Scholarship Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application online.
benefits: Educational assistance ranging from Rs.2,000 to Rs.40,000 per year depending on the course. Additional 20% assistance is provided for eligible girl students.

implementing_agency: Backward Communities Development Department, Government of Kerala
tags: Barber, OBC, Self Employment, Renovation Grant, Livelihood
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for modernization and renovation of traditional barbershops to improve working conditions and livelihood opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Kerala belonging to an Other Backward Community, engaged as a traditional barber, operating in a rural area, having at least 10 years of experience, annual family income not exceeding Rs.1,00,000, and age not exceeding 60 years.
documents_required: Photograph of Applicant, Photograph of Shop, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Experience Certificate, Ration Card, Identity Proof, Aadhaar Card, Shop Ownership or Rental Proof, Bank Account Details, Registration Certificate (if applicable), and Disability Certificate (if applicable).
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form through the concerned Grama Panchayat along with required documents. Eligible applications are scrutinized and forwarded to the Regional Deputy Director for approval.
benefits: Financial assistance equal to 50% of project cost subject to a maximum of Rs.25,000 for modernization and renovation of traditional barbershops.

implementing_agency: Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Entrepreneurship, MSME, Subsidy, Loan Assistance, NEEDS
brief_description: The scheme supports educated first-generation entrepreneurs by providing entrepreneurship training, capital subsidy, interest subvention, and assistance in obtaining institutional finance.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Tamil Nadu for at least 3 years, be a first-generation entrepreneur aged 21–35 years (up to 45 years for special categories), possess minimum educational qualification of 12th pass, Diploma, ITI or equivalent, and propose a project costing between Rs.10 lakh and Rs.5 crore.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or Voter ID Card, PAN Card, Passport-size Photograph, Educational Qualification Certificates, Age Proof, Community Certificate, Residence Proof, Bank Account Details, and Special Category Certificates (if applicable).
application_process: Apply online through the MSME Department portal. Eligible applicants undergo screening, Entrepreneurship Development Programme (EDP) training, and loan appraisal by financial institutions before sanction.
benefits: Capital subsidy of 25% of project cost up to Rs.75 lakh, 3% interest subvention on term loans, priority allotment in SIDCO industrial estates, and additional incentives for eligible special category entrepreneurs.

scheme_name: Chief Minister's College Students Rehabilitation Scheme (CMCSRS)
implementing_agency: Directorate of University and Higher Education, Government of Manipur
tags: Student Welfare, Rehabilitation, Education Support, Scholarship, Higher Education
brief_description: The scheme supports undergraduate students affected by social unrest in Manipur by providing educational assistance, fee waivers, rehabilitation grants, and digital learning support.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an undergraduate student studying in a Government or Government-Aided college located in Manipur and affected by the prevailing social unrest.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, College Identity Card, Bank Account Details, and Certificate issued by the Sub-Divisional Officer (SDO) or Sub-Divisional Collector (SDC).
application_process: Eligible students may apply through the designated online portal as notified by the Directorate of University and Higher Education.
benefits: Free admission, inter-college transfer facility, one-time rehabilitation grant of Rs.10,000, free smartphones, and other educational support measures for eligible students.

implementing_agency: Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), Department of Science and Technology, Government of India
tags: Research Grant, Innovation, High Risk Research, Science and Technology
brief_description: The scheme supports innovative, high-risk research proposals that have the potential to generate paradigm-shifting scientific discoveries or technological breakthroughs.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian citizen residing in India and hold a regular academic or research position in a recognized institution. Applications may be submitted individually or jointly by a team of investigators.
documents_required: Biodata, Certificate from Principal Investigator, Endorsement Letter from Head of Institution, and Equipment Quotations (if project is approved).
application_process: Apply online through the SERB Online Portal by registering, selecting the High Risk - High Reward Research scheme, completing the proposal form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application for peer review.
benefits: Research grant covering equipment, consumables, contingency, travel, and institutional overheads. No upper funding limit is prescribed.

scheme_name: Revised Education Scheme for Children of Artisans and Weavers
implementing_agency: Directorate of Handicrafts and Handloom, Government of Jammu and Kashmir
target_beneficiaries: Children of Registered Artisans and Weavers
tags: Scholarship, Artisan, Weaver, Education Assistance, Students
brief_description: The scheme provides scholarship assistance to children of registered artisans and weavers to support continuation of their education.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a child of a registered artisan or weaver possessing a valid registration certificate from the Handicrafts or Handloom Department. The applicant should not be receiving benefits under any other scholarship scheme and should satisfy the prescribed income criteria.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Parent's Artisan or Weaver Registration Certificate, Income Certificate, NOC from concerned authorities, Educational Certificates, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and Residence Proof.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the concerned Directorate of Handicrafts and Handloom office or through the designated portal, if notified.
benefits: Scholarship assistance of Rs.1,500 per year up to primary level, Rs.2,000 per year for Classes 6–10, Rs.3,000 per year for Classes 11–12, and reimbursement of approved fees for eligible technical and professional courses.

implementing_agency: Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR)
tags: Agriculture, Postgraduate, Scholarship, ICAR, Higher Education
brief_description: The scholarship supports meritorious postgraduate students pursuing agricultural education in ICAR-recognized universities outside their state of domicile.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should secure admission through the ICAR All India Entrance Examination and pursue a postgraduate programme in an ICAR-recognized Agricultural University located outside the student's state of domicile. The student should maintain good academic performance and conduct.
documents_required: ICAR Entrance Examination Scorecard, Admission Letter, Academic Certificates, Identity Proof, Domicile Certificate, Bank Account Details, and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Eligible students are considered through the ICAR admission process and scholarship guidelines applicable to All India quota admissions.
benefits: Scholarship of Rs.3,000 per month for postgraduate students pursuing eligible programmes in agricultural universities outside their home state.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to ST Students under Gagan Bhi Shiksha Yojana
implementing_agency: Department of Tribal Welfare, Government of Goa
tags: Scheduled Tribe, Scholarship, Education Assistance, Students
brief_description: The scheme provides additional maintenance assistance to Scheduled Tribe students receiving Post-Matric Scholarship support to help meet educational, food, and travel expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to a Scheduled Tribe community in Goa, be a regular full-time student in a recognized institution, and be eligible for or already receiving the Centrally Sponsored Post-Matric Scholarship. Annual family income should not exceed Rs.5,00,000.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Previous Examination Marksheet, Income Certificate, Scheduled Tribe Certificate, Hostel Receipt (if applicable), Bank Passbook Copy, and Aadhaar Card.
application_process: Apply online through the Goa Scholarship Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Additional maintenance allowance of Rs.7,500 per year for day scholars and Rs.15,000 per year for hostellers.

scheme_name: Biotechnology Career Advancement and Re-orientation (BioCARe) Programme for Women Scientists
implementing_agency: Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Government of India
tags: Biotechnology, Women Scientists, Research Grant, Fellowship, Career Development
brief_description: BioCARe supports women scientists in biotechnology and allied fields by providing research grants and fellowship support to help them establish independent research careers.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a woman scientist below 55 years of age with a PhD, MD, MDS, MVSc, MTech in Biotechnology, MPharma, or equivalent qualification. Applicants should not hold regular employment and should not have previously received independent extramural research funding as Principal Investigator.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Address Proof, Date of Birth Certificate, Educational Qualification Certificates, Research Publications Details, Mentor Details, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Disability Certificate (if applicable), and Declaration Form.
application_process: Apply online in response to calls announced by the Department of Biotechnology. Eligible applications undergo screening, expert review, proposal presentation, and final approval.
benefits: Research grant up to Rs.60 lakh for Category-I applicants and up to Rs.40 lakh for Category-II applicants, including fellowship support and research expenses for a period of three years.

implementing_agency: Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Gujarat
tags: Newborn Health, Child Health, Screening, Early Detection, Healthcare
brief_description: Atal Sneh Yojana aims to improve the health of newborn children through early screening and timely treatment of congenital disorders and birth defects.
eligibility_criteria: All newborn children in Gujarat are eligible for screening under the scheme up to two months after birth.
documents_required: Birth Record, Hospital Registration Details, Parent or Guardian Identity Proof, and other documents as required by the health facility.
application_process: Eligible newborns are screened at designated Government health facilities and hospitals as part of the newborn health screening programme.
benefits: Free screening, early diagnosis, and treatment support for congenital disorders, birth defects, and other health conditions detected in newborn children.

implementing_agency: Leather Industries Development Corporation (LIDCOM), Government of Maharashtra
tags: Training, Skill Development, Employment, Entrepreneurship, Scheduled Caste
category: Business & Entrepreneurship, Skills & Employment, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: The scheme provides vocational training and skill development opportunities to students from the Charmakar community to support self-employment, entrepreneurship, and job placement.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an Indian citizen, permanent resident of Maharashtra, aged between 18 and 50 years, belong to the Charmakar community, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.1,00,000, and possess basic knowledge related to the proposed trade or business activity.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Age Proof, Educational Certificates, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, and Training-related documents as required.
application_process: Apply through the concerned District LIDCOM Office by submitting the prescribed application form along with supporting documents.
benefits: Free vocational training, skill development support, and assistance for self-employment and entrepreneurship opportunities.

implementing_agency: Arunachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Medical Assistance, Construction Workers, Health Support, Accident, Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides medical assistance to registered construction workers hospitalized due to illness or accidents.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Arunachal Pradesh, engaged in building and construction work, registered with the Arunachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, and have an active membership at the time of application.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Worker Registration Card, Medical Certificates, Hospital Discharge Summary, Treatment Records, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and Residence Proof.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with medical records and supporting documents to the concerned office of the Arunachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
benefits: Financial assistance for hospitalization and treatment expenses incurred due to illness or accidents as per Board norms.

scheme_name: Grant of Subsidy for Setting up of Ice Plant, Cold Storage and Fish Processing Unit
implementing_agency: Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare, Government of Puducherry
target_beneficiaries: Fishermen, Fisherwomen Cooperative Societies and Entrepreneurs
tags: Fisheries, Subsidy, Cold Storage, Fish Processing, Infrastructure
brief_description: The scheme supports establishment of ice plants, cold storage facilities, and fish processing units through capital subsidy assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a fisherman, fisherwoman cooperative society, entrepreneur, or private sector entity aged between 18 and 50 years and should not have availed subsidy for the same purpose from any other source.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Proof, Passport-size Photograph, Project Report, Bank Account Details, Land Ownership or Lease Documents, Registration Certificate (if applicable), and other documents prescribed by the Department.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare through the concerned office.
benefits: 10% subsidy up to Rs.3,00,000 for establishment of Ice Plant-cum-Cold Storage facilities and 10% subsidy up to Rs.10,00,000 for Fish Processing Units.

scheme_name: Development Support for Coconut in Traditional Areas - Water Augmentation
implementing_agency: Coconut Development Board, Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India
tags: Coconut, Water Conservation, Irrigation, Subsidy, Agriculture
brief_description: The scheme promotes water conservation and irrigation infrastructure in coconut plantations by providing subsidy support for water harvesting structures and irrigation equipment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an individual coconut grower, joint owner, or eligible family member with coconut holdings up to 10 hectares. The applicant should satisfy scheme conditions relating to land ownership and previous subsidy benefits.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Land Ownership Documents, Bank Passbook, Self Declaration, Estate Sketch, Quotations for Equipment, Project Estimate, Caste Certificate (if applicable), and other documents prescribed by the Coconut Development Board.
application_process: Apply online through the Coconut Development Board subsidy portal, obtain a Technical Feasibility Report, complete the approved activity, and submit the subsidy claim with supporting documents.
benefits: Subsidy of 40% of unit cost up to Rs.2,50,000 per beneficiary. Eligible SC/ST growers may receive an additional 10% subsidy subject to scheme norms.

implementing_agency: Department of Panchayati Raj and Rural Development, Government of Andhra Pradesh
target_beneficiaries: Women from BC, SC, ST and Minority Communities
tags: Women Empowerment, Financial Assistance, Livelihood Support, Social Welfare
category: Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women & Child Development
brief_description: YSR Cheyutha provides financial assistance to eligible women from BC, SC, ST, and Minority communities to promote livelihood generation, income enhancement, and economic empowerment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Andhra Pradesh, female, aged between 45 and 60 years, belong to BC, SC, ST, or Minority communities, and should not be receiving benefits under YSR Pension Kanuka. Family income should be below Rs.10,000 per month in rural areas and Rs.12,000 per month in urban areas. Other government-prescribed socio-economic criteria should also be satisfied.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Proof, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Bank Account Details, Family Identification Documents, Passport-size Photograph, and other documents prescribed by the Government.
application_process: Eligible beneficiaries are identified and verified through Village and Ward Secretariats. Applications are processed through the designated Government welfare platform.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.75,000 distributed in four annual installments of Rs.18,750 each.

implementing_agency: Tribal Area Development Department, Government of Rajasthan
tags: NEET, JEE, Coaching, Tribal Students, Education
brief_description: The scheme provides free coaching to tribal students preparing for NEET and JEE examinations to improve access to medical and engineering education.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to a Scheduled Tribe community in Rajasthan and have passed Class 12 in the Science stream with First Division or equivalent academic performance as prescribed by the Department.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Scheduled Tribe Certificate, Class 10 and 12 Mark Sheets, Residence Certificate, Passport-size Photograph, and other documents prescribed by the Department.
application_process: Eligible students may apply through the Tribal Area Development Department or the designated portal as notified by the Government of Rajasthan.
benefits: Free coaching, study materials, mentoring support, doubt-clearing sessions, and mock tests for NEET and JEE preparation.

scheme_name: Integrated Social Security Scheme - Pension for Physically Challenged Persons
implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Jammu and Kashmir
tags: Disability Pension, PwD, Social Security, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly pension assistance to persons with disabilities who have limited means of livelihood and require social security support.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Jammu and Kashmir, aged between 18 and 79 years, possess at least 40% disability, and have annual family income below Rs.75,000 or belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Disability Certificate, Income Certificate, Residence Certificate, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and Medical Assessment Report.
application_process: Apply through the concerned Tehsil Social Welfare Office by submitting the prescribed application form along with supporting documents.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.1,000 directly transferred to the beneficiary's bank account through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

implementing_agency: Department of Commerce and Industries, Government of Sikkim
tags: Startup, Entrepreneurship, Employment, Youth, Subsidy
brief_description: The Skilled Youth Startup Scheme supports educated unemployed youth in establishing self-employment ventures through bank-linked loans and government-backed subsidy assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Sikkim, aged between 18 and 45 years, possess at least Class 5 educational qualification, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.8 lakh, and should not have previously availed subsidy under PMEGP or similar schemes. The applicant must not be a bank defaulter.
documents_required: Certificate of Identification (COI), Aadhaar Card, Educational Certificates, Income Certificate, Residence Proof, Bank Account Details, Project Report, Trade License, Passport-size Photograph, and category certificates (if applicable).
application_process: Apply through the District Industries Centre (DIC) by submitting the prescribed application form, project proposal, and supporting documents for scrutiny and bank linkage.
benefits: Back-ended subsidy of 50% of project cost for BPL beneficiaries and 35% for other eligible beneficiaries, subject to scheme norms and project approval.

implementing_agency: Arunachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
target_beneficiaries: Families of Deceased Registered Construction Workers
tags: Construction Workers, Death Benefit, Funeral Assistance, Labour Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides funeral and immediate financial assistance to the family or nominee of a deceased registered construction worker.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a family member or registered nominee of a deceased construction worker who was registered with the Arunachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board and had active membership at the time of death.
documents_required: Death Certificate, Worker Registration Card, Aadhaar Card of Nominee, Bank Account Details, Nominee Proof, Residence Certificate, Passport-size Photograph, and other supporting documents.
application_process: The applicant should approach the concerned district office of the Arunachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board and submit the required documents for verification and sanction.
benefits: Immediate funeral assistance of Rs.1,000 and other death-related welfare benefits as admissible under Board rules.

implementing_agency: Department of Agriculture and Cooperation in collaboration with the Telangana State Seed Development Corporation
tags: Seed Production, Fodder Seeds, Livestock, Agriculture, Subsidy
brief_description: The Feed the Seed Programme promotes fodder seed production among livestock farmers by providing quality grass and fodder seeds at subsidized rates and encouraging sustainable fodder cultivation.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a farmer engaged in livestock rearing and possess or lease agricultural land suitable for fodder cultivation. Preference is given to farmers involved in seed production activities.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Proof, Land Ownership or Lease Documents, Livestock Details, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and other documents prescribed by the Department.
application_process: Eligible farmers may apply through the local Agriculture Department or Telangana State Seed Development Corporation office with the required documents.
benefits: Supply of fodder and grass seeds such as Lucerne, Stylosanthes, Guinea Grass, and African Tall varieties at 75% subsidy.

implementing_agency: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India
target_beneficiaries: Farmers, Self Help Groups, Farmer Producer Organizations, Cooperative Societies and Entrepreneurs
tags: Agriculture Mechanization, Farm Machinery, SMAM, Custom Hiring Centres, Subsidy
brief_description: SMAM promotes farm mechanization by providing financial assistance for agricultural machinery, custom hiring centres, demonstrations, training, and testing of farm equipment.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible beneficiaries include farmers, Self Help Groups (SHGs), Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), Cooperative Societies, User Groups, and entrepreneurs. Priority is given to small, marginal, and women farmers as per scheme guidelines.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Land Records, Bank Account Details, Farmer Registration Documents, Passport-size Photograph, and other documents prescribed by the implementing agency.
application_process: Apply through the State Agriculture Department or the designated agricultural mechanization portal by submitting the prescribed application form and supporting documents.
benefits: Financial assistance for purchase of agricultural machinery and equipment, establishment of custom hiring centres, farm machinery banks, demonstrations, and training programmes.

scheme_name: Chief Minister Samagra Gramya Unnayan Yojana (CMSGUY)
implementing_agency: Government of Assam
tags: Rural Development, Agriculture, Livelihood, Farmers, Village Development
brief_description: CMSGUY is a comprehensive rural development programme aimed at doubling farmers' income through focused investments in agriculture, fisheries, livestock, rural infrastructure, processing, and market linkages.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant group should consist of 8 to 10 adult farmers from the same village, possess a joint bank account, jointly apply under the scheme, and not include more than one member from the same family.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Joint Bank Account Details, Land Records, Group Membership Details, Passport-size Photographs, and other documents prescribed by the Government of Assam.
application_process: Eligible farmer groups may apply through the concerned Agriculture Department office or implementing agency under the notified components of the scheme.
benefits: Financial assistance and support through various agriculture, fisheries, livestock, infrastructure, processing, and livelihood development interventions under the programme.

implementing_agency: Women and Child Welfare Department, Government of Sikkim
tags: BPL Families, Death Benefit, Financial Assistance, Social Security
brief_description: The scheme provides one-time financial assistance to Below Poverty Line families on the death of the primary breadwinner.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Sikkim belonging to a Below Poverty Line family. Assistance is available upon the death of the primary breadwinner. The beneficiary family should possess a valid bank account, preferably under Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Death Certificate of the Breadwinner, BPL Certificate, Residence Proof, Bank Account Details, Family Identification Documents, Passport-size Photograph, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the concerned Women and Child Welfare Department office for verification and approval.
benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs.30,000 to the eligible bereaved family.

implementing_agency: Jharkhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Women Workers, Construction Workers, Bicycle Assistance, Labour Welfare
category: Skills & Employment, Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women & Child Development
brief_description: The scheme provides bicycles to registered women construction workers to improve mobility and support their employment activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a domiciled woman worker of Jharkhand aged between 18 and 60 years, registered with the Jharkhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, engaged in construction work, and have completed at least 90 days of work.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Worker Registration Card, Residence Certificate, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, Work Certificate, and other documents prescribed by the Board.
application_process: Apply online through the Shramadhan Portal by completing the application form and uploading the required documents.
benefits: Provision of a bicycle to eligible registered women construction workers.

scheme_name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)
implementing_agency: Women and Child Welfare Department, Government of Sikkim
tags: Disability Pension, BPL Families, Social Security, Direct Benefit Transfer
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly pension assistance to persons with severe or multiple disabilities belonging to Below Poverty Line families.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Sikkim, belong to a Below Poverty Line family, be 18 years of age or above, have a disability of 80% or more, have no regular source of income, and should not be receiving salary, pension, or family pension from the Central or State Government.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Disability Certificate, BPL Certificate, Residence Proof, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the concerned Women and Child Welfare Department office for verification and approval.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.2,000 provided through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

implementing_agency: Forest Department, Government of Chhattisgarh
target_beneficiaries: Farmers, Forest Rights Holders, Gram Panchayats and Joint Forest Management Committees
tags: Tree Plantation, Agroforestry, Environment Conservation, Farmers, Plantation
brief_description: The scheme promotes commercial and community tree plantation to improve environmental conservation and enhance farmers' income through agroforestry activities.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible beneficiaries include farmers who have sold paddy to the Government, forest rights holders willing to undertake tree plantation, Gram Panchayats, and Joint Forest Management Committees interested in plantation activities on eligible land.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Land Ownership or Forest Rights Documents, Bank Account Details, Residence Proof, Passport-size Photograph, and other documents prescribed by the Forest Department.
application_process: Apply through the Forest Department portal or the concerned Forest Range Office by submitting the prescribed application form and supporting documents.
benefits: Financial assistance and incentives for tree plantation on private and community land, supporting agroforestry, environmental conservation, and income generation.

implementing_agency: Department of Handlooms and Textiles, Government of Andhra Pradesh
tags: Handloom Weavers, Financial Assistance, Loom Modernization, Livelihood Support
brief_description: The scheme provides annual financial assistance to handloom weaver families to modernize looms and improve competitiveness against powerloom products.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Andhra Pradesh, engaged in handloom weaving as the primary livelihood, registered with the Handloom Department or Handloom Association, belong to a Below Poverty Line family, and own a handloom.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Weaver Identity Card, Handloom Registration Certificate, BPL Certificate, Bank Account Details, Residence Proof, Passport-size Photograph, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Eligible beneficiaries are identified through the Department of Handlooms and Textiles database and assistance is transferred through the designated government platform.
benefits: Annual financial assistance of Rs.24,000 per eligible handloom weaver family.

scheme_name: IVRI Institute Scholarship for M.V.Sc. and Ph.D. Students
implementing_agency: Indian Veterinary Research Institute (IVRI), Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR)
tags: Scholarship, Veterinary Science, M.V.Sc., Ph.D., Higher Education
brief_description: The scholarship provides financial assistance to meritorious M.V.Sc. and Ph.D. students admitted through competitive examinations at the Indian Veterinary Research Institute.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be admitted to M.V.Sc. or Ph.D. programmes at IVRI through the prescribed competitive entrance examination and maintain a minimum CGPA of 7.0 during the course of study. Additional academic eligibility conditions apply as prescribed by IVRI.
documents_required: Admission Letter, Academic Certificates and Mark Sheets, Identity Proof, Entrance Examination Scorecard, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and other documents prescribed by IVRI.
application_process: Eligible students are considered for scholarship awards after admission. Selection is made on the recommendation of the Standing Committee on Scholarship, Financial Assistance, and Academic Progress based on merit and academic performance.
benefits: Scholarship assistance for eligible M.V.Sc. and Ph.D. students as per IVRI scholarship norms and availability of funds.

implementing_agency: Department of Women Development and Child Welfare, Government of Telangana
tags: Nutrition, Pregnant Women, Maternal Health, Anemia Reduction, Anganwadi
brief_description: The scheme provides nutrition kits to pregnant women to improve maternal nutrition, reduce anemia, and promote healthier pregnancy outcomes.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a pregnant woman residing in Telangana, identified through Anganwadi Centres or health institutions. Priority is given to economically weaker families as per government guidelines.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Pregnancy Registration Record, Residence Proof, Health Card or Medical Records, Bank Account Details (if applicable), and other documents prescribed by the Department.
application_process: Eligible beneficiaries are identified through Anganwadi Workers and health officials. Nutrition kits are distributed through designated Anganwadi Centres and health facilities.
benefits: Monthly nutrition kits containing nutritional mix powder, dates, iron syrup, ghee, and other nutritional supplements to improve maternal health and reduce anemia.

implementing_agency: Department of Women Development and Child Welfare, Government of Telangana
target_beneficiaries: Pregnant Women, Lactating Mothers and Children
tags: Nutrition, Anganwadi, Maternal Health, Child Health, Supplementary Nutrition
brief_description: Arogya Lakshmi is a nutrition support programme that provides one nutritious meal every day to pregnant women, lactating mothers, and children through Anganwadi Centres.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Telangana. Eligible beneficiaries include pregnant women, lactating mothers, and children enrolled through Anganwadi Centres as per programme guidelines.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Pregnancy Registration Record (for pregnant women), Child Health Records (for children), Residence Proof, and other documents prescribed by the Department.
application_process: Eligible beneficiaries are identified and enrolled through Anganwadi Centres. Nutrition services and supplementary food are provided directly through designated centres.
benefits: One nutritious meal daily through Anganwadi Centres, including rice, dal, vegetables, eggs, milk, iron and folic acid supplementation, and nutrition support for children below six years of age.

implementing_agency: Government of Assam
tags: DBT, Women, Financial Assistance, Household Welfare, Social Security
category: Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women & Child Development
brief_description: The Assam Orunodoi Scheme provides monthly financial assistance to economically vulnerable households through direct benefit transfer to eligible women beneficiaries.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a woman from an eligible household in Assam. Priority is given to widows, unmarried women above 35 years of age, persons with disabilities, and economically vulnerable families. Household landholding and income criteria prescribed by the Government of Assam must be satisfied.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Proof, Bank Account Details, Income Certificate, Land Records (if applicable), Passport-size Photograph, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Eligible beneficiaries are identified through Gram Panchayats, Village Council Development Committees (VCDCs), and Urban Local Bodies (ULBs). Approved beneficiaries receive assistance through Direct Benefit Transfer.
benefits: Monthly financial assistance of Rs.1,000 directly credited to the bank account of the nominated woman beneficiary.

scheme_name: Competitive Exam Coaching for Tribal Students
implementing_agency: Tribal Area Development Department, Government of Rajasthan
tags: Competitive Examinations, Coaching, Scheduled Tribe, Education, Students
brief_description: The scheme provides free coaching to Scheduled Tribe students preparing for competitive examinations through designated tribal research and training institutions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Scheduled Tribe category and be a resident of Rajasthan. Both male and female students are eligible. The benefit can be availed only once.
documents_required: Educational Qualification Certificates, Mark Sheets, Domicile Certificate, Scheduled Tribe Certificate, Income Certificate (if applicable), Passport-size Photograph, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Applicants may obtain the application form from the Maharana Pratap Tribal Research and Training Institute or the concerned Project Officer's office, complete the form, attach supporting documents, and submit it to the designated authority.
benefits: Free coaching for various competitive examinations through government-supported coaching institutions.

implementing_agency: Industries and Commerce Department, Government of Assam
target_beneficiaries: Youth Entrepreneurs and Small Business Owners
tags: Entrepreneurship, Self Employment, Youth Empowerment, Financial Assistance, MSME
brief_description: SVAYEM provides financial assistance to youth entrepreneurs for establishing or expanding income-generating activities in manufacturing, trading, service, transport, and tourism sectors.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Assam, above 18 years of age, possess at least Class 5 educational qualification, have the skills or experience required for the proposed activity, should not be a loan defaulter, and should not have received PMEGP benefits during the previous five years.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Educational Qualification Certificate, Bank Account Details, Project Report, Passport-size Photograph, Skill Training Certificate (if applicable), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the designated SVAYEM portal or District Industries and Commerce Centre (DICC) by submitting the prescribed application form and supporting documents.
benefits: Financial assistance for setting up or expanding eligible income-generating ventures in manufacturing, trading, service, transport, and tourism sectors.

implementing_agency: District Deputy Director (Scheduled Caste Welfare), Government of Gujarat
tags: Scholarship, Scheduled Caste, Girls Education, Financial Assistance, Higher Education
brief_description: The scheme provides scholarship assistance to Scheduled Caste girl students pursuing higher education who are not eligible under the Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme due to income or other restrictions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a Scheduled Caste girl student residing in Gujarat. For diploma courses, the applicant should have secured at least 50% marks in Class 10 or Class 12 examinations. For graduation courses, the applicant should have secured at least 50% marks in Class 12. Annual family income should exceed Rs.2.5 lakh and the applicant should satisfy other prescribed conditions.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Educational Mark Sheets, Bonafide Certificate, Bank Account Details, Passport-size Photograph, and Residence Proof.
application_process: Apply through the Gujarat Scheduled Caste Welfare portal by completing the prescribed application form and uploading the required documents.
benefits: Scholarship assistance for eligible Scheduled Caste girl students pursuing diploma, undergraduate, and other higher education courses.

scheme_name: Assam Abhinandan Education Loan Subsidy Scheme
implementing_agency: Finance Department, Government of Assam
tags: Education Loan, Higher Education, Student, Loan Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme provides subsidy on education loans to students pursuing higher education to reduce the financial burden of educational expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Assam. The education loan should have been availed from a Scheduled Commercial Bank or Regional Rural Bank operating in Assam and should be at least Rs.1,00,000.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Education Loan Sanction Letter, Bank Account Details, Educational Admission Proof, Passport-size Photograph, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Eligible students may apply through the designated Assam Government portal by submitting the prescribed application form and supporting documents.
benefits: One-time education loan subsidy of up to Rs.50,000 for eligible students pursuing higher education.

scheme_name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) for MSME Sector: Assistance to SMEs for Raising Capital through SME Exchange
implementing_agency: Department of Industries, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu Administration
tags: MSME, SME Exchange, Capital Raising, Entrepreneurship, Subsidy
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides one-time financial assistance to MSMEs for expenditure incurred in raising capital through SME Exchange platforms.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a new or existing manufacturing or service MSME unit located in Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu. The unit should have commenced commercial production between 20 May 2022 and 19 May 2027 and must apply within one year from the listing date on the SME Exchange.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, PAN Card, GST Registration Certificate, CIN or Business Registration Documents, Land Ownership or Lease Documents, Electricity Connection Documents, Factory License, Consent to Operate Certificate (if applicable), Bank Details, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Single Window Portal by registering, completing the application form under the Investment Promotion Scheme, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial assistance equal to 25% of expenditure incurred for raising funds through SME Exchange, subject to a maximum limit of Rs.5,00,000.

scheme_name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) for MSME Sector: Assistance for Patent Registration and Trademark Registration
implementing_agency: Department of Industries, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu Administration
tags: Patent Registration, Trademark Registration, MSME, Innovation, Subsidy
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for obtaining patent and trademark registrations to encourage innovation and intellectual property protection.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an individual, new manufacturing or service MSME unit, or existing MSME undertaking expansion or diversification in Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu. Application should be submitted within one year of patent or trademark publication.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, PAN Card, GST Registration Certificate, Patent or Trademark Registration Documents, Business Registration Documents, Land Ownership or Lease Documents, Factory License, Consent to Operate Certificate (if applicable), and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Single Window Portal by registering, selecting the applicable IPS component, uploading documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Reimbursement of up to 75% of patent or trademark registration expenditure, subject to a maximum assistance of Rs.25,00,000. Attorney fees are reimbursable within prescribed limits.

scheme_name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) for MSME Sector: Incentives for Doubling of Export Values
implementing_agency: Department of Industries, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu Administration
tags: Exports, MSME, Entrepreneurship, Incentive, Export Promotion
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides incentives to MSMEs that significantly increase export performance and achieve growth in export value.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a manufacturing or service MSME unit located in Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu. The unit should have commenced commercial production between 20 May 2022 and 19 May 2027 and must apply within six months from the end of the previous financial year.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, PAN Card, GST Registration Certificate, Export Documents, Bank Statements, Business Registration Documents, Factory License, Consent to Operate Certificate (if applicable), and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Single Window Portal by completing the prescribed application form and uploading the required documents.
benefits: Women entrepreneurs receive incentive equal to 2% of export value up to Rs.30,00,000, while other entrepreneurs receive 1% of export value up to Rs.20,00,000.

scheme_name: Scheme for Establishment of Hi-Tech and Mini Dairy Units
implementing_agency: Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying, Government of Haryana
tags: Dairy Farming, Animal Husbandry, Self Employment, Subsidy, Dairy Units
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to unemployed persons for establishing hi-tech and mini dairy units with milch animals, promoting self-employment and dairy entrepreneurship.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Haryana aged between 18 and 55 years, unemployed, possess adequate space for keeping milch animals, and satisfy land requirements for larger dairy units. Applicants who have previously availed similar benefits under departmental livestock schemes are generally not eligible.
documents_required: Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Bank Passbook or Cancelled Cheque, Photograph of Animal Shed or Space, Residence Proof, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by registering, selecting the scheme, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application for verification and bank linkage.
benefits: 25% subsidy for 4 and 10 milch animal dairy units and interest subvention support for 20 and 50 milch animal dairy units. Additional support includes veterinary services, training, and assistance in obtaining bank loans.

scheme_name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) for Textile Sector: Interest Subsidy for Textile Sector
implementing_agency: Department of Industries, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu Administration
tags: Textile Industry, Technical Textiles, Interest Subsidy, Entrepreneurship, Industrial Incentives
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides credit-linked interest subsidy to textile and technical textile enterprises to promote investment, employment generation, and industrial growth in the textile sector.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a new or existing manufacturing or service enterprise in the textile sector undertaking expansion or diversification in Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu. Commercial production should commence between 20 May 2022 and 19 May 2027. Application must be submitted within one year of eligibility.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, PAN Card, GST Registration Certificate, Business Registration Documents, Land Ownership or Lease Documents, Electricity Connection Documents, Factory License, Consent to Operate Certificate (if applicable), Bank Loan Documents, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Single Window Portal by registering, selecting the applicable scheme component, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Interest subsidy of 60% of the interest charged by the bank, subject to a maximum of Rs.60,00,000 per annum for up to 5 years or till loan repayment, whichever is earlier. Export-oriented carpet industries are eligible for an additional 10% subsidy up to Rs.70,00,000.

implementing_agency: Transport Department, Government of Bihar
target_beneficiaries: SC, ST, EBC, BC, Minority and General Category Entrepreneurs
tags: Transport, Employment, Entrepreneurship, Bus Purchase, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme promotes rural and block-level transport connectivity while encouraging entrepreneurship through financial assistance for the purchase of passenger buses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of the concerned block in Bihar, be at least 18 years of age, possess a valid driving licence, should not be employed in government service, and belong to an eligible category as prescribed by the Government.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Residence Certificate, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Educational Qualification Certificate, Driving Licence, Passport-size Photograph, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the Transport Department portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Subsidy of Rs.5,00,000 for purchase of one passenger bus under the scheme.

scheme_name: Small Business in Service/Trading Sector for Persons with Disabilities (Divyangjan Swavalamban Yojana)
implementing_agency: Haryana Backward Classes and Economically Weaker Sections Kalyan Nigam
tags: Disability, Self Employment, Business Loan, Divyangjan, Entrepreneurship
brief_description: The scheme provides concessional loans to persons with disabilities for establishing self-employment ventures in agriculture, business, transport, service, and professional sectors.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana, aged between 18 and 55 years, possess at least 40% disability, and hold a valid Disability Certificate. UDID registration is mandatory for loans of Rs.15 lakh and above.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Disability Certificate, Haryana Domicile Certificate, Age Proof, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Concessional loans up to Rs.50,00,000 for self-employment activities with interest rates ranging from 5% to 9% per annum depending on the category and loan amount.

scheme_name: Term Loan Scheme - Credit Line 1 for Minority Community
implementing_agency: Haryana Backward Classes and Economically Weaker Sections Kalyan Nigam
tags: Minority Welfare, Self Employment, Term Loan, Entrepreneurship, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides concessional term loans to eligible persons from minority communities for self-employment and income-generating activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana, aged between 18 and 55 years, belong to a notified minority community (Muslim, Sikh, Christian, Buddhist, Parsi, or Jain), and have annual family income not exceeding Rs.98,000 in rural areas or Rs.1,20,000 in urban areas.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Haryana Domicile Certificate, Minority Community Certificate, Income Certificate, PAN Card, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), Passport-size Photograph, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by completing the prescribed application form and uploading supporting documents.
benefits: Term loan assistance up to Rs.20,00,000 at concessional interest rates for eligible self-employment and business activities.

implementing_agency: Department of Water Resources, River Development and Ganga Rejuvenation, Ministry of Jal Shakti
target_beneficiaries: Students and Recent Graduates in Mass Communication and Related Fields
tags: Internship, Mass Communication, Journalism, Students, Honorarium
brief_description: The internship programme provides practical exposure to students and recent graduates in mass communication, journalism, public relations, and related fields through participation in information, education, and communication activities of the Ministry.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be pursuing or have recently completed studies in Mass Communication, Journalism, Public Relations, MBA (Marketing), or related disciplines. Academic performance requirements prescribed by the Ministry must be satisfied.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Curriculum Vitae, Aadhaar Card or other Identity Proof, PAN Card, Bank Passbook, No Objection Certificate from Institution, and a 500-word statement of purpose.
application_process: Download the prescribed application form, complete it with required documents, and submit the application through email to the designated ministry email address within the prescribed timeline.
benefits: Honorarium of Rs.10,000 per month, internship completion certificate, workspace, internet facilities, and practical exposure to government communication activities.

scheme_name: Sanction of Motorized Three Wheeler to Orthopedically Challenged Persons
implementing_agency: Andhra Pradesh Differently Abled and Senior Citizens Assistance Corporation
tags: Disability, Mobility Aid, Three Wheeler, Self Employment, Students
brief_description: The scheme provides motorized three-wheelers to eligible persons with severe orthopedic disabilities to improve mobility, independence, education, and livelihood opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Andhra Pradesh, aged between 18 and 40 years, belong to a BPL family, possess at least 80% locomotor or orthopedic disability, and be either a student pursuing higher education or a self-employed person with at least 3 years of experience. The applicant should possess a valid driving licence.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Disability Certificate, Driving Licence, Educational Qualification Certificate, Income Certificate, Residence Proof, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, and Self-Employment Proof (if applicable).
application_process: Apply online through the Andhra Pradesh Differently Abled and Senior Citizens Assistance Corporation portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Free distribution of motorized three-wheelers to eligible beneficiaries.

implementing_agency: Haryana Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation
target_beneficiaries: Women Safai Karamcharis and Their Dependent Daughters
tags: Women Entrepreneurship, Self Employment, Loan, NSKFDC, Safai Karamchari
brief_description: The scheme provides concessional loans to women Safai Karamcharis and their dependent daughters for self-employment and income-generating activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana, female, aged between 18 and 55 years, and either a Safai Karamchari or dependent daughter of a Safai Karamchari. No income limit is prescribed.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, Safai Karamchari Certificate or Dependent Certificate issued by the competent authority, Passport-size Photograph, Bank Account Details, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Haryana Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation portal or the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by completing the application form and uploading required documents.
benefits: Loan up to Rs.1,00,000 for self-employment activities, 10% margin money support, and subsidy up to 50% of project cost subject to a maximum of Rs.10,000 for eligible BPL beneficiaries.

scheme_name: Investment Promotion Scheme (IPS) for Thrust Sectors: Food Processing Industries
implementing_agency: Department of Industries, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu Administration
tags: Food Processing, Interest Subsidy, Entrepreneurship, Industrial Incentives, MSME
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides credit-linked interest subsidy to food processing industries for establishment, expansion, and modernization of units in the Union Territory.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a new or existing manufacturing or service enterprise in the food processing sector undertaking expansion or diversification in Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu. Commercial production should commence between 20 May 2022 and 19 May 2027.
documents_required: Udyam Registration Certificate, PAN Card, GST Registration Certificate, Business Registration Documents, Land Ownership or Lease Documents, Electricity Connection Documents, Factory License, Consent to Operate Certificate (if applicable), Loan Sanction Documents, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Single Window Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Interest subsidy of 60% of interest charged by the bank, subject to a maximum of Rs.50,00,000 per annum for up to 5 years or till loan repayment, whichever is earlier. An additional 10% subsidy is available for units established in dedicated food processing industrial parks.

implementing_agency: Haryana Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation
tags: Self Employment, Term Loan, NSKFDC, Safai Karamchari, Entrepreneurship
brief_description: The scheme provides concessional term loans to Safai Karamcharis and their dependents for self-employment and income-generating activities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana, aged between 18 and 55 years, and be a Safai Karamchari or dependent of a Safai Karamchari. No income limit is prescribed.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, Qualification or Experience Certificates, Project Report (for loans above Rs.1,00,000), Safai Karamchari Certificate or Dependent Certificate, Driving Licence (for vehicle loans), Passport-size Photograph, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the Haryana Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation portal or the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by completing the application form and uploading required documents.
benefits: Term loans up to Rs.15,00,000 for eligible self-employment activities, 10% margin money support, and subsidy up to 50% of project cost subject to a maximum of Rs.10,000 for eligible BPL beneficiaries.

scheme_name: Saraswati Vidya Yojana: Reimbursement of Fees for Diploma, Graduation and Post-Graduation Professional Courses
implementing_agency: Directorate of Education, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu Administration
target_beneficiaries: Girl Students Pursuing Professional Higher Education
tags: Girls Education, Fee Reimbursement, Scholarship, Professional Courses, Higher Education
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides fee reimbursement assistance to girl students pursuing diploma, graduation, and postgraduate professional courses to promote higher education and reduce gender disparity.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a girl student whose parents, husband, or guardian are domiciled residents of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu. The student should have studied continuously for at least 5 years in the Union Territory, including the qualifying examination. Benefits are limited to the first two daughters of a family.
documents_required: Fee Receipts, Domicile Certificate, Income Certificate, Fee Structure Approval Order of Institution, Academic Result or Mark Sheet, Bonafide Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Bank Passbook, Declaration regarding non-availment of similar benefits, and Parent or Guardian Declaration.
application_process: Download and complete the prescribed application form, attach required documents, and submit it to the concerned District Education Office through registered post, speed post, or by hand before the prescribed deadline.
benefits: 100% reimbursement of tuition and academic fees for students from families with annual income below Rs.1,50,000 and 50% reimbursement for others. Maximum reimbursement is Rs.2,50,000 per year for diploma and undergraduate professional courses and Rs.3,50,000 per year for postgraduate professional courses. Additional hostel assistance up to Rs.30,000 per year is available.

scheme_name: Marriage Incentive Award for Marriages Between Differently Abled and Non-Disabled Persons
implementing_agency: Department for Welfare of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens, Government of Andhra Pradesh
tags: Marriage Incentive, Disability, Financial Assistance, Social Inclusion
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to encourage marriages between differently abled persons and non-disabled persons and promote social inclusion.
eligibility_criteria: One spouse should be a differently abled person. The couple should not have availed benefits under this scheme earlier. The bridegroom must be at least 21 years old and the bride at least 18 years old at the time of marriage.
documents_required: Disability Certificate, Marriage Registration Certificate, Joint Photographs of the Couple, Aadhaar Card, Ration Card or Residence Certificate, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the Assistant Director, Welfare of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens of the concerned district for verification and approval.
benefits: Marriage incentive assistance of Rs.1,00,000.

implementing_agency: National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation
tags: Education Loan, Scheduled Caste, Higher Education, Study Abroad, Professional Courses
brief_description: The scheme provides educational loans to Scheduled Caste students pursuing full-time professional and technical courses in recognized institutions in India and abroad.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category, be pursuing a full-time professional or technical course in a recognized institution, and have annual family income not exceeding Rs.3,00,000.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Income Certificate, Caste Certificate, Residence Proof, Educational Qualification Certificates, Admission Letter, Fee Structure Details, Identity Card of Institution, Passport and Visa (for overseas studies), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the State Channelizing Agency by submitting the prescribed application form and supporting documents. Applications are scrutinized, recommended, and forwarded to NSFDC for sanction and loan disbursement.
benefits: Education loan up to Rs.30,00,000 for studies in India and up to Rs.40,00,000 for studies abroad. Women beneficiaries receive an interest rebate of 0.5%.

scheme_name: Additional Pension to 100% Disabled Ex-Servicemen Having Children
implementing_agency: Sainik and Ardh Sainik Welfare Department, Government of Haryana
tags: Ex-Servicemen, Disability Pension, Children, Welfare, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides additional pension assistance to ex-servicemen who have 100% disability attributable to military service and have dependent children.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana, an ex-serviceman with 100% disability attributable to service, and have dependent children below 20 years of age who are unmarried and unemployed.
documents_required: Ex-Servicemen Identity Card, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), Discharge Book, Domicile Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Disability Certificate, PPO, Bank Passbook, Affidavit, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by completing the application form and uploading required documents.
benefits: Additional monthly pension of Rs.100 per child for Other Ranks, Rs.150 per child for JCOs, and Rs.200 per child for Officers, subject to prescribed maximum limits.

scheme_name: Partial Disability Benefit for Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Sikkim Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Construction Workers, Partial Disability, Labour Welfare, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered construction workers who suffer partial disability due to accidents occurring during the course of employment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered construction worker of the Sikkim Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board and should have suffered partial disability due to an employment-related accident.
documents_required: Identity Card, Medical Certificate, Employer Certificate or Letter from Establishment, Age Proof, Residence Proof, Registration Documents, and Passport-size Photograph.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the District Labour Office or authorized Welfare Board office for verification and approval.
benefits: Financial assistance ranging from Rs.10,000 to Rs.1,00,000 depending on the extent of disability.

implementing_agency: Sainik and Ardh Sainik Welfare Department, Government of Haryana
tags: Blindness, Ex-Servicemen, Financial Assistance, Disability Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly financial assistance to permanently blind ex-servicemen who are not receiving any pension or similar financial assistance from the Central or State Government.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a bona fide resident of Haryana, a 100% permanently blind ex-serviceman, and should not be receiving any pension or financial assistance from the Central Government, State Government, or any government-controlled organization.
documents_required: Ex-Servicemen Identity Card, Aadhaar Card, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), Domicile Certificate, Blindness Certificate, Discharge Book, Bank Passbook, Non-Employment Certificate, Affidavit, NOC from Social Welfare Department, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application for verification through the Zila Sainik Board.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.3,000 per month with annual enhancement as notified by the Government of Haryana.

scheme_name: Additional Pension to Widow of Ex-Serviceman Having No Child
implementing_agency: Sainik and Ardh Sainik Welfare Department, Government of Haryana
tags: Widow Pension, Ex-Servicemen, Financial Assistance, Social Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides additional pension assistance to widows of ex-servicemen who died while in service (other than battle casualties) and who have no children.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the widow of an ex-serviceman who died while in service but not in battle. The applicant should be a resident of Haryana and should not have any children. The benefit is available until death or remarriage.
documents_required: Ex-Servicemen/Widow Identity Card, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), Discharge Book, Domicile Certificate, Aadhaar Card, PPO, Bank Passbook, Affidavit, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by completing the application form and uploading the required documents.
benefits: Additional pension of Rs.100 per month over and above the regular pension.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Orphan Children of Ex-Servicemen
implementing_agency: Sainik and Ardh Sainik Welfare Department, Government of Haryana
tags: Orphan Children, Ex-Servicemen, Financial Assistance, Welfare
