brief_description: The scheme provides monthly financial assistance to orphan children of ex-servicemen who are residents of Haryana and are not receiving similar assistance from the State Government.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an orphan child of an ex-serviceman, a resident of Haryana, below 21 years of age, unmarried, unemployed, and not receiving similar financial assistance from any department of the Haryana Government.
documents_required: Ex-Servicemen Identity Card, Aadhaar Card, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), Birth Certificate, Death Certificates of Parents, Domicile Certificate, Bank Passbook, Non-Employment Certificate, Guardianship Certificate (for minors), Affidavit, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by completing the application form and uploading the required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.3,000 per month with annual enhancement as notified by the Government of Haryana.

implementing_agency: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation
tags: Marriage Assistance, Brahmin Welfare, Vedic Culture, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides one-time financial assistance to Brahmin brides marrying eligible Vedic priests engaged in traditional Vedic occupations to promote Vedic culture.
eligibility_criteria: Bride should be at least 18 years old and belong to the Brahmin community. Bridegroom should be at least 21 years old, reside in Andhra Pradesh, belong to the Brahmin community, and earn his livelihood through Veda Parayanam, Archakatvam, or Pourohityam.
documents_required: Aadhaar Cards of Bride and Bridegroom, Caste Certificates, Birth Certificates, Marriage Invitation Cards, Marriage Registration Certificate, Joint Marriage Photograph, Bank Passbook of Bride, Affidavit regarding Vedic occupation, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation portal by completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application before or within the prescribed period after marriage.
benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs.75,000.

implementing_agency: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation
tags: Funeral Assistance, Brahmin Welfare, Financial Assistance, Social Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to economically weaker Brahmin families to meet funeral expenses of a deceased family member.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a close relative and legal heir of the deceased Brahmin, belong to the Brahmin community, reside in Andhra Pradesh, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.75,000, and should not have received assistance for the same purpose under any other government scheme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Cards of Applicant and Deceased, Death Certificate, Income Certificate, Caste Certificate, Residence Certificate, Birth Certificate, Bank Passbook, Self-Declaration as Legal Heir, Passport-size Photograph, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation portal by completing the application form and uploading required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.10,000 towards funeral expenses.

scheme_name: Grant for the Purchase of Tools and Protective Gear for Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
tags: Construction Workers, Tools, Protective Gear, Labour Welfare, Financial Assistance
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered construction workers for purchasing tools and protective safety equipment required for their work.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu and should have completed at least six months of membership.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Membership Details, Subscription Details, Invoice for Purchase of Tools or Protective Gear, Utilization Certificate, Cash Memo, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the Member Secretary of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.10,000 for purchase of tools and protective gear. The benefit can be availed once every three years.

implementing_agency: Department of Agriculture, Animal Husbandry and Cooperative, Government of Jharkhand
tags: Agriculture, Crop Loan, Farmer, Kisan Credit Card, Loan Waiver
brief_description: The scheme provides relief to small and marginal farmers by waiving eligible outstanding short-term crop loans availed through Kisan Credit Cards.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Jharkhand, above 18 years of age, a small or marginal farmer, holder of a valid Kisan Credit Card, Aadhaar Card and ration card, and should have an eligible short-term crop loan from a recognized bank in Jharkhand.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Age Proof, Ration Card, Kisan Credit Card, Bank Passbook or Account Details, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Jharkhand Krishi Rin Mafi Yojana portal by registering with Aadhaar details, verifying loan information, completing the application, and paying the prescribed application fee.
benefits: Waiver of eligible outstanding short-term crop loans up to Rs.50,000 as per scheme guidelines.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Marriage for Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
target_beneficiaries: Registered Construction Workers and Their Children
tags: Construction Workers, Marriage Assistance, Labour Welfare, Financial Assistance
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for the marriage of registered construction workers and their children.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board with at least six months of membership or be one of the first two children of a registered worker.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Marriage Certificate, Bank Account Details, Membership Proof, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the Member Secretary of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
benefits: Rs.51,000 for marriage of a registered female worker, Rs.35,000 for marriage of a registered male worker, Rs.51,000 for marriage of a daughter of a registered worker, and Rs.35,000 for marriage of a son of a registered worker.

implementing_agency: Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Himachal Pradesh
tags: Animal Husbandry, Goat Farming, Financial Assistance, Rural Development
brief_description: The scheme promotes goat farming among economically weaker sections by providing goat units on subsidy to improve income generation and rural livelihoods.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh belonging to General, SC, ST, BPL, Women, or Landless categories. Training in goat husbandry is mandatory. Preference is given to unemployed persons, women, low-income families, and farmers with goat sheds.
documents_required: Bank Passbook, Income Certificate, Unemployment Certificate, BPL Certificate, Goat Husbandry Training Certificate, Caste Certificate (if applicable), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Department of Animal Husbandry portal by completing the application form and uploading the required documents.
benefits: 60% subsidy on approved goat units consisting of 3, 5, or 11 goats of approved breeds such as Beetal, Sirohi, Jamunapari, and White Himalayan.

scheme_name: Bharati Scheme for Education (Class 6 to Class 10)
implementing_agency: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation
target_beneficiaries: Brahmin Students Studying in Classes 6 to 10
tags: Education Assistance, Brahmin Students, Scholarship, School Education
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible Brahmin students studying in Classes 6 to 10 to encourage continuation of school education.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Brahmin community, be studying in Andhra Pradesh, have family income not exceeding Rs.3,00,000 per annum, and be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey. Preference is given to orphans, differently abled students, and children of single parents.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card of Student and Parents, Income Certificate, Caste Certificate, Residence Certificate, Birth Certificate, Study Certificate, Bank Passbook, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation portal by completing the registration process and uploading the required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.7,000 per academic year.

scheme_name: Bharati Scheme for Education - Chartered Accountancy Final
implementing_agency: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation
tags: Higher Education, Chartered Accountancy, Brahmin Students, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible Brahmin students pursuing the Chartered Accountancy Final course.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Brahmin community, reside in Andhra Pradesh, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.3,00,000, have cleared both groups of CA Intermediate, be registered for the CA Final examination, and be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card of Student and Parents, Income Certificate, Caste Certificate, Residence Certificate, Birth Certificate, CA Intermediate Mark Sheets, Proof of CA Final Registration and Fee Payment, Bank Passbook, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation portal by completing the registration process and uploading the required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.20,000.

scheme_name: Bharati Scheme for Master's Degree Overseas
implementing_agency: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation
target_beneficiaries: Brahmin Students Pursuing Master's Degree Abroad
tags: Overseas Education, Master's Degree, Financial Assistance, Students
brief_description: The scheme provides one-time financial assistance to eligible Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh who have secured admission to a master's degree course abroad.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Brahmin community, be a resident of Andhra Pradesh, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.6,00,000, and have secured admission to a master's degree course abroad of at least 12 months duration. Applicants who have already availed the benefit under the scheme are not eligible.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Degree Certificate, Senior Secondary Certificate, Bank Passbook, Passport-size Photograph, Admission Letter, Fee Payment Receipts, Passport, Visa, Air Ticket, Form I-20 or equivalent document, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation portal by completing the registration process, uploading the required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Rs.5,00,000 for overseas courses of 11 to 18 months duration and Rs.10,00,000 for master's degree programmes of more than 18 months duration.

scheme_name: Bharati Scheme for Education - Intermediate or Equivalent Courses
implementing_agency: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation
target_beneficiaries: Brahmin Students Pursuing Intermediate or Equivalent Courses
tags: Education Assistance, Intermediate, ITI, Polytechnic, Students
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible Brahmin students pursuing Intermediate or equivalent courses such as ITI, Polytechnic, D.Ed, CEC, and D.Pharmacy.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Brahmin community, be studying in Andhra Pradesh, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.3,00,000, be enrolled in a recognized institution, have passed all subjects of the previous academic year, and be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card of Student and Parents, Income Certificate, Caste Certificate, Residence Certificate, Birth Certificate, Study Certificate, Previous Academic Mark Sheets, Bank Passbook, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation portal by completing the registration process and uploading the required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.10,000 per academic year.

scheme_name: Swami Vivekananda Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme (Class 11 and Class 12)
implementing_agency: School Education Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
target_beneficiaries: BPL Students Studying in Government Schools
tags: Scholarship, Post-Matric, BPL Students, Education Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides scholarships to meritorious students from Below Poverty Line families studying in Classes 11 and 12 in government schools.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh, studying in Class 11 or Class 12 in a government school, belong to a BPL family with annual family income below Rs.1,00,000, and have passed the previous examination in first division.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Madhya Pradesh Domicile Certificate, BPL Certificate or Ration Card, Previous Class Mark Sheet, Income Certificate, Samagra ID, Bank Passbook, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Eligible students should contact the principal of their government school. Student details are entered on the Shiksha Portal by the institution based on submitted documents.
benefits: Scholarship of Rs.550 per year for girl students and Rs.500 per year for boy students.

scheme_name: Marriage Grant to Orphan Daughters of Ex-Servicemen
implementing_agency: Sainik and Ardh Sainik Welfare Department, Government of Haryana
tags: Marriage Assistance, Ex-Servicemen, Orphan Daughter, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for the marriage of dependent orphan daughters of ex-servicemen residing in Haryana.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a dependent orphan daughter of an ex-serviceman, a resident of Haryana, at least 18 years old, and assistance is limited to a maximum of two daughters per family.
documents_required: Ex-Servicemen Identity Card, Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP), Discharge Book, Haryana Domicile Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Bank Passbook, PPO, Age Proof, Wedding Card, Death Certificates of Parents, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.51,000 for the marriage of each eligible orphan daughter.

scheme_name: Ex-Gratia for Death of Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
target_beneficiaries: Nominees and Dependents of Deceased Registered Construction Workers
tags: Construction Workers, Death Benefit, Ex-Gratia, Labour Welfare
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
brief_description: The scheme provides ex-gratia financial assistance to nominees or dependents of registered construction workers in case of death.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the nominee or dependent of a deceased registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu.
documents_required: Death Certificate, Identity Proof of Applicant, Dependency or Legal Heir Certificate, Membership Details of the Deceased Worker, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Member Secretary of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
benefits: Rs.4,00,000 in case of death during the course of employment and Rs.2,00,000 in case of death due to other reasons.

implementing_agency: Labour and ESI Department, Government of Odisha
tags: Housing Assistance, Construction Workers, Direct Benefit Transfer, Rural Housing
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for construction of pucca houses for registered construction workers in rural areas who do not own a pucca house.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Odisha, registered with the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, aged between 18 and 60 years, engaged in construction work for at least 90 days during the preceding 12 months, and should not own a pucca house.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Ration Card, Residence Certificate, Income Certificate, Age Proof, Bank Account Details.
application_process: Eligible registered construction workers are identified through the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board and selected through verification by Block and District authorities before house sanction.
benefits: Housing assistance of Rs.1,20,000 in Non-IAP districts and Rs.1,30,000 in IAP districts. Additional incentives are available for early completion and convergence benefits under MGNREGS, SBM(G), electrification, drinking water, and other welfare schemes.

scheme_name: Integrated Social Security Scheme - Pension for Women in Distress
implementing_agency: Directorate of Social and Tribal Welfare, Administration of Union Territory of Ladakh
target_beneficiaries: Destitute Widows, Divorced and Deserted Women
tags: Women Pension, Widow Pension, Divorced Women, BPL, Social Security
category: Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women & Child Development
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly pension assistance to destitute widows, divorced women, and deserted women belonging to Below Poverty Line families.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Ladakh, belong to the BPL category, be a destitute widow, divorced or deserted woman aged between 40 and 79 years, and have no source of income.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Age Proof, Income Certificate, BPL Certificate, Residence Proof, Bank Account Details.
application_process: Apply online through the Ladakh e-Seva Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.1,000 per beneficiary.

scheme_name: Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Meritorious Students Incentive Scheme (Class 12 Passed)
implementing_agency: School Education Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
tags: Cash Award, Merit Scholarship, Class 12, Students
brief_description: The scheme provides a cash incentive to meritorious students who secure the highest marks in the Class 12 examination among government schools in their district.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh, have passed Class 12 from a Government Higher Secondary School, secured the highest marks in the district, and belong to a family with annual income below Rs.1,00,000.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Domicile Certificate, Ration Card, Class 12 Mark Sheet, Income Certificate, Samagra ID, Bank Passbook.
application_process: Eligible students should contact the principal of their government school. Student details are entered through the Shiksha Portal by the institution.
benefits: Cash award of Rs.5,000.

scheme_name: Tool Purchase Grant for Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Government of West Bengal
tags: Construction Workers, Tool Purchase, Labour Welfare, Reimbursement
brief_description: The scheme provides one-time reimbursement assistance to registered construction workers for purchasing professional tools required for their occupation.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal, have completed at least six months of membership, and have purchased tools related to their profession.
documents_required: Identity Card of Worker, Original Bills or Purchase Vouchers for Tools, and other supporting documents as required.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Assistant Labour Commissioner or Beneficiary Registering Officer.
benefits: One-time reimbursement of Rs.2,000.

scheme_name: Chanakya Swayam Upadhi Scheme for Micro Entrepreneurship - Transport Operator
implementing_agency: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation
tags: Self Employment, Transport Operator, Entrepreneurship, Brahmin Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides subsidy support to unemployed Brahmin youth for purchasing four-wheelers and establishing self-employment as transport operators.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should belong to the Brahmin community, reside in Andhra Pradesh, be aged between 21 and 50 years, have passed Class 10, possess a valid driving licence and badge, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.1,00,000, and should not own a three-wheeler or four-wheeler.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Residence Certificate, Income Certificate, Birth Certificate, Educational Qualification Certificate, Driving Licence, Driver Badge or Identity Proof, Passport-size Photograph, and Ration Card.
application_process: Apply online through the Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation portal by completing registration, uploading documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Back-end subsidy of 25% of vehicle cost subject to a maximum of Rs.2,00,000 along with loan assistance through the AP Brahmin Cooperative Credit Society.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Education of Self or Children of Beneficiaries
implementing_agency: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Government of West Bengal
target_beneficiaries: Registered Construction Workers and Their Children
tags: Education Assistance, Scholarship, Construction Workers, Higher Education
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered construction workers and their children pursuing Higher Secondary education and above.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal for at least six months or be a dependent child of such a member. The applicant should be studying in Higher Secondary or higher education courses.
documents_required: Identity Card of Worker, Educational Certificates, Dependent Certificate (if applicable), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Assistant Labour Commissioner or Beneficiary Registering Officer.
benefits: Financial assistance ranging from Rs.4,000 to Rs.10,000 depending on the level of education, from Higher Secondary to professional and postgraduate courses.

scheme_name: State Government Backward Class Scholarship (Class 6 to 10)
implementing_agency: School Education Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
tags: Scholarship, Backward Class, Minority Students, School Education
brief_description: The scheme provides scholarships to students belonging to Backward Classes and Minority communities studying in Classes 6 to 10.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh, belong to a Backward Class or Minority community, study in Classes 6 to 10, and have parents or guardians who are not income taxpayers and do not own more than 10 acres of land.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Domicile Certificate, Bank Passbook, Income Certificate, Samagra ID, Parent Declaration, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Eligible students should contact their school principal. Student details are entered through the Shiksha Portal by the educational institution.
benefits: Classes 6-8: Rs.200 per year for boys and Rs.300 per year for girls. Classes 9-10: Rs.300 per year for boys and Rs.400 per year for girls.

scheme_name: Medical Expenses for Treatment of Major Ailments Including Surgery
implementing_agency: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Government of West Bengal
target_beneficiaries: Registered Construction Workers and Their Dependents
tags: Medical Assistance, Surgery, Construction Workers, Health Support
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for treatment of major ailments and surgeries for registered construction workers and their dependents.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal or a dependent suffering from major illnesses such as cancer, heart disease, kidney disease, tuberculosis, eye disease, leprosy, or neurological disorders.
documents_required: Identity Card of Worker, Medical Certificate, Medical Bills, Dependent Certificate (if applicable), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with medical documents to the Assistant Labour Commissioner or Beneficiary Registering Officer.
benefits: Medical assistance up to Rs.20,000 per annum. Higher assistance may be sanctioned for major surgeries and critical illnesses.

scheme_name: Khule Jalsroton Mein Pen Aadharit Matsya Palan Yojana
implementing_agency: Animal and Fisheries Resources Department, Government of Bihar
target_beneficiaries: Fishermen Groups and Joint Liability Groups
tags: Fish Farming, Fisheries, Aquaculture, Subsidy, Employment
brief_description: The scheme promotes pen-based fish farming in large public water bodies to increase fish production, strengthen aquaculture, and generate employment opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered fishermen group, cooperative society, self-help group, or joint liability group engaged in fisheries activities and possessing valid lease rights or permission for the concerned water body.
documents_required: Photograph, Training Certificate, Water Body Lease Receipt, Group Registration Documents, Aadhaar Card or Identity Proof, Consent Letter, Bank Passbook or Cancelled Cheque, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Bihar Fisheries Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: 75% subsidy on the approved unit cost of Rs.10,50,000 per pen unit.

implementing_agency: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Government of West Bengal
target_beneficiaries: Nominees of Deceased Registered Construction Workers
tags: Funeral Assistance, Construction Workers, Labour Welfare, Death Benefit
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance towards funeral expenses of deceased registered construction workers.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the nominee, dependent, or legal heir of a deceased registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal.
documents_required: Identity Card of Worker, Death Certificate, Nominee or Legal Heir Certificate, Dependent Certificate (if applicable), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Assistant Labour Commissioner or Beneficiary Registering Officer.
benefits: Funeral assistance of Rs.3,000 payable to the nominee or legal heir of the deceased worker.

implementing_agency: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Government of West Bengal
target_beneficiaries: Nominees of Deceased Registered Construction Workers
tags: Death Benefit, Construction Workers, Accidental Death, Labour Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides death benefit assistance to the nominees of registered construction workers in case of natural or accidental death.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the nominee, dependent, or legal heir of a deceased registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal.
documents_required: Death Certificate, Nominee or Legal Heir Certificate, Identity Proof, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Assistant Labour Commissioner or Beneficiary Registering Officer.
benefits: Rs.50,000 in case of natural death and Rs.1,50,000 in case of accidental death.

scheme_name: Refund of Subscription for Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Government of West Bengal
tags: Subscription Refund, Construction Workers, Labour Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides refund of subscription amount deposited by registered construction workers along with interest under specified conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal who has attained 60 years of age, wishes to discontinue membership after three years, or be the nominee/legal heir of a deceased member.
documents_required: Identity Card of Worker, Nominee or Legal Heir Certificate, Death Certificate (if applicable), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Assistant Labour Commissioner or Beneficiary Registering Officer.
benefits: Refund of the entire subscription amount deposited by the beneficiary along with 8% interest.

implementing_agency: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Government of West Bengal
tags: Pension, Family Pension, Construction Workers, Labour Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides monthly pension to eligible registered construction workers after attaining 60 years of age and family pension to nominees after the beneficiary's death.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal, have completed at least 5 years of membership, and have attained 60 years of age.
documents_required: Identity Card of Worker, Age Proof, Bank Account Details, Nominee or Heirship Certificate (for family pension), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form to the Assistant Labour Commissioner or Beneficiary Registering Officer.
benefits: Monthly pension ranging from Rs.750 to Rs.1,120. Family pension is payable at 50% of the pension amount received by the beneficiary before death.

implementing_agency: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Government of West Bengal
target_beneficiaries: Female Construction Workers and Wives of Registered Construction Workers
tags: Miscarriage Assistance, Women Workers, Labour Welfare, Health Support
category: Health & Wellness, Women & Child Development, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to female construction workers and wives of registered construction workers in case of miscarriage.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered female member of the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of West Bengal or the wife of a registered male member. The benefit can be availed a maximum of two times during the beneficiary's lifetime.
documents_required: Identity Card of Worker, Medical Certificate confirming miscarriage, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with medical documents to the Assistant Labour Commissioner or Beneficiary Registering Officer.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.6,000 per miscarriage, limited to two claims during the beneficiary's lifetime.

implementing_agency: Social Justice and Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
tags: Hostel Assistance, Disabled Students, Higher Education, Rental Support
brief_description: The scheme provides hostel and rental assistance to students with disabilities pursuing higher secondary and higher education where hostel facilities are not available.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh, have at least 40% disability, be studying in Class 11 or above in a recognized institution, and have parental annual income not exceeding Rs.96,000.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Birth Certificate, Domicile Certificate, Previous Academic Mark Sheet, Income Certificate, Disability Certificate, Institution Registration Proof, Samagra ID, Bank Account Details, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the District Office of Social Justice and Disabled Welfare for verification and approval.
benefits: Rental assistance of Rs.750 per month for hostel accommodation and Rs.1,000 per month towards electricity and water charges.

scheme_name: Hostel Facility for Students Availing Education in Delhi
implementing_agency: Scheduled Caste Welfare Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
target_beneficiaries: Scheduled Caste Students Studying in Delhi
tags: Hostel Facility, Higher Education, Scheduled Caste, Scholarship, Delhi
brief_description: The scheme provides hostel accommodation and related financial assistance to Scheduled Caste students from Madhya Pradesh pursuing higher education in recognized institutions located in Delhi.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh, belong to the Scheduled Caste category, and be enrolled as a regular student in an undergraduate, postgraduate, professional, or higher course at a recognized institution in Delhi.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Madhya Pradesh Domicile Certificate, Caste Certificate, Previous Academic Mark Sheet, Degree Certificate (if applicable), Samagra ID, Bank Passbook, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form through the concerned educational institution to the Residential Commissioner, Madhya Pradesh Bhawan, New Delhi.
benefits: Monthly hostel rent assistance of Rs.1,000, scholarship of Rs.500 per month, water and electricity assistance of Rs.100 per month, and a one-time admission grant of Rs.2,000.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Education of Children of Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Education Assistance, Scholarship, Construction Workers, Students
brief_description: The scheme provides educational assistance to children of registered construction workers pursuing studies from Class VI onwards.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a child of a registered member of the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. The worker should have completed at least one year of registration and the child should be studying in Class VI or above.
documents_required: Registration Identity Card of Worker, Proof of Annual Contribution Deposit, Mark Sheet of Student, Aadhaar Card, Bank Account Details, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board portal by logging into the registered worker account and selecting the education assistance benefit.
benefits: Educational assistance ranging from Rs.2,000 to Rs.40,000 per year depending on the class or course pursued. Additional cash award of Rs.10,000 for students securing 90% or above in Class 10 examinations.

scheme_name: Pathari Kshetra Talab Nirman Aadharit Matsya Palan Yojana
implementing_agency: Animal and Fisheries Resources Department, Government of Bihar
target_beneficiaries: Fish Farmers and Landowners in Plateau Areas
tags: Fish Farming, Pond Construction, Fisheries, Subsidy, Employment
brief_description: The scheme promotes fisheries development through construction of fish ponds and related infrastructure in plateau-dominated districts of Bihar.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should own or lease eligible land in identified plateau-dominated districts and construct a pond with a minimum water area of 0.5 acre and maximum of 1 acre as per scheme guidelines.
documents_required: Photograph of Proposed Land, Aadhaar Card, Bank Account Details, Land Ownership Certificate or Lease Agreement, Land Map, Caste Certificate, Revenue Receipt, Affidavit for Dispute-Free Land, Consent Form, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Bihar Fisheries Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading documents, and submitting the application to the District Fisheries Office.
benefits: Subsidy of up to 80% of the approved unit cost of Rs.16.70 lakh per acre. Subsidy is released in installments after pond construction and infrastructure verification.

scheme_name: Dr. Bhim Rao Ambedkar Meritorious Student Award Scheme
implementing_agency: Scheduled Caste Welfare Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
tags: Merit Award, Scheduled Caste Students, Education, Academic Excellence
brief_description: The scheme provides cash awards to meritorious Scheduled Caste students securing top positions in Class 10 and Class 12 board examinations.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh, belong to the Scheduled Caste category, and secure top ranks in Class 10 or Class 12 examinations conducted by MP Board, CBSE, or ICSE.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Domicile Certificate, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Mark Sheet, Samagra ID, Bank Passbook, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Eligible students are identified through examination results and processed by the Scheduled Caste Welfare Department through the concerned educational institutions.
benefits: Awards range from Rs.5,000 to Rs.51,000 based on rank and examination level.

implementing_agency: Animal and Fisheries Resources Department, Government of Bihar
tags: Fish Farming, Aquaculture, Farmers, Employment, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme promotes integrated fish-based aquaculture development on underutilized Chaur lands to increase fish production and create employment opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be an individual, family, group beneficiary, entrepreneur, private landowner, or lessee possessing eligible Chaur land in notified districts.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Bank Account Details, Land Ownership Certificate or Lease Agreement, Revenue Receipt, Land Records, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Bihar Fisheries Portal by registering, completing the application form, uploading required documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: 50% subsidy for General category beneficiaries, 70% subsidy for SC, ST, and Extremely Backward Class beneficiaries, and 40% subsidy for entrepreneur-based projects.

implementing_agency: Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
target_beneficiaries: Nominees of Deceased Registered Construction Workers
tags: Death Benefit, Construction Workers, Labour Welfare, Accidental Death, Natural Death
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to nominees of registered construction workers in case of natural or accidental death.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the nominee of a deceased registered member of the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
documents_required: Registration ID Card of Deceased Worker, Proof of Annual Contribution Deposit, Death Certificate, Identity Proof of Applicant, Bank Account Details, Form XIV Report (for accidental death), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Odisha Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board portal by logging into the registered worker account and selecting the death benefit scheme.
benefits: Rs.1,00,000 in case of natural death and Rs.2,00,000 in case of accidental death.

implementing_agency: Fisheries and Animal Resources Development Department, Government of Odisha
tags: Fish Farming, Aquaculture, Pond Excavation, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme promotes freshwater aquaculture by supporting excavation of new ponds and creation of water bodies for fish farming.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a permanent resident of Odisha, possess eligible land free from encumbrances, have a bank account in Odisha, and construct a pond between 0.2 and 2 hectares with a minimum depth of 6 feet.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Land Ownership Documents, Bank Passbook Copy, and Caste Certificate (if applicable).
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Assistant Fisheries Officer or District Fisheries Officer.
benefits: Subsidy of 40% for General category beneficiaries and 50% for SC, ST, and Women beneficiaries on approved pond excavation and aquaculture input costs.

scheme_name: Disability Benefit for Construction Workers Injured at Worksite
implementing_agency: Tripura Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Disability Benefit, Workplace Accident, Construction Workers, Labour Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered construction workers who suffer temporary or permanent disability due to workplace accidents.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered construction worker who sustained temporary or permanent disability due to an accident at the worksite.
documents_required: Disability Certificate, Medical Board Recommendation, Medical Reports, Accident Intimation Proof, BOCW Registration Card, Bank Passbook, Ration Card, Age Proof, Self-Undertaking, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) with biometric verification and submission of all required documents.
benefits: For temporary disability, 50% of the notified minimum wage for unskilled construction workers for up to 3 months. For permanent disability exceeding 90%, Rs.3,000 per month until the beneficiary attains 60 years of age.

scheme_name: Rehabilitation of Cured Leprosy Persons - Assistance to Parents
implementing_agency: Social Security and Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities Department, Government of Odisha
target_beneficiaries: Parents and Guardians of Cured Leprosy Children
tags: Leprosy Rehabilitation, Parents Assistance, Child Welfare, Financial Support
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to parents or guardians of cured leprosy children to support their upbringing and rehabilitation.
eligibility_criteria: Parents or guardians should be permanent residents of Odisha and the child should be a cured leprosy person below 18 years of age.
documents_required: Domicile Certificate, Birth Certificate or Age Proof of Child, Proof of Cured Leprosy Status, Guardianship Certificate (if applicable), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Block Office or Urban Local Body Administration.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.1,000 per month per eligible child until the age of 18 years.

scheme_name: Rehabilitation of Cured Leprosy Persons - Marriage Incentive and Support
implementing_agency: Social Security and Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities Department, Government of Odisha
tags: Marriage Assistance, Leprosy Rehabilitation, Women Welfare, Financial Support
category: Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women & Child Development
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance for the marriage of daughters of cured leprosy persons facing social and economic challenges.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a daughter of a cured leprosy person who is a permanent resident of Odisha and has attained the legal age for marriage.
documents_required: Domicile Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Birth Certificate or Age Proof, Proof of Cured Leprosy Status of Parent, Marriage-related documents, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Block Office or Urban Local Body Administration.
benefits: One-time financial assistance of up to Rs.50,000 for marriage expenses.

scheme_name: Funeral Assistance for Registered Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Tripura Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
target_beneficiaries: Nominees, Spouses, and Dependents of Deceased Registered Construction Workers
tags: Funeral Assistance, Construction Workers, Labour Welfare, Death Benefit
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance towards funeral expenses in case of the death of a registered construction worker.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the nominee, spouse, or dependent of a deceased registered construction worker. The deceased worker should not have any outstanding dues payable to the Board.
documents_required: BOCW Registration Card, Death Certificate, Bank Account Details of Nominee, Ration Card, Photo Identity Proof, No Objection Certificate from other nominees (if applicable), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) by submitting the prescribed application form and required documents within 30 days of the worker's death.
benefits: One-time funeral assistance of Rs.10,000.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance in Case of Death of Registered Construction Worker
implementing_agency: Tripura Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
target_beneficiaries: Nominees, Spouses, and Dependents of Deceased Registered Construction Workers
tags: Death Benefit, Construction Workers, Labour Welfare, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to the nominee, spouse, or dependent of a deceased registered construction worker.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the nominee, spouse, or dependent of a deceased registered construction worker. The application must be submitted within one year of death and the worker should not have any outstanding dues payable to the Board.
documents_required: BOCW Registration Card, Death Certificate, Bank Account Details of Nominee, Ration Card, Photo Identity Proof, FIR (for accidental death), No Objection Certificate from other nominees (if applicable), and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) by submitting the prescribed application form and supporting documents.
benefits: Rs.2,00,000 in case of natural death and Rs.4,00,000 in case of accidental death during the course of employment.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Religious Trusts and Endowments, Government of Madhya Pradesh
tags: Pilgrimage, Sindhu Darshan, Ladakh, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible pilgrims from Madhya Pradesh undertaking the Sindhu Darshan pilgrimage in Ladakh.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh, selected by the Government of Madhya Pradesh for the Sindhu Darshan pilgrimage, should have completed the pilgrimage, and should not be an income taxpayer.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Domicile Certificate, Pilgrimage Completion Certificate, Expenditure Details, Selection Certificate, Bank Account Details, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form with expenditure records and pilgrimage certificates to the concerned District Collector within 60 days of completing the pilgrimage.
benefits: Reimbursement of 50% of actual pilgrimage expenses, subject to a maximum of Rs.10,000 per pilgrim.

implementing_agency: Madhya Pradesh Labour Welfare Board
tags: Widow Assistance, Labour Welfare, Deceased Worker, Financial Assistance
category: Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women & Child Development
brief_description: The scheme provides recurring financial assistance to widows of deceased workers employed in establishments covered under the Madhya Pradesh Labour Welfare Fund Act.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the widow of a deceased worker who was employed in a covered establishment for at least one year and registered with the Madhya Pradesh Labour Welfare Board. The widow should not have remarried and should not be receiving similar assistance from another government department.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Domicile Certificate, Death Certificate of Worker, Bank Account Details, Non-Remarriage Affidavit, Employer Certification, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through a Lok Seva Kendra or submit the prescribed application form at the regional office or labour welfare centre of the Madhya Pradesh Labour Welfare Board.
benefits: Rs.6,000 every six months, totaling Rs.12,000 per year.

implementing_agency: Telangana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Disability Relief, Workplace Accident, Construction Workers, Financial Assistance
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered construction workers who suffer permanent disability due to workplace accidents.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered construction worker under the Telangana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board and should have suffered total or partial permanent disability due to a workplace accident.
documents_required: Passport-size Photograph, Aadhaar Card, BOCW Registration Card, Renewal Proof, Disability Certificate, FIR Copy (if applicable), Bank Passbook Copy, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Download the prescribed application form from the Telangana BOCW Board website, complete the form, attach supporting documents, and submit it to the concerned Assistant Labour Officer.
benefits: Rs.5,00,000 for total permanent disability and up to Rs.4,00,000 for partial permanent disability depending on the extent of disability.

scheme_name: Chief Minister Higher Education Scholarship Scheme
implementing_agency: Higher Education Department, Government of Rajasthan
target_beneficiaries: Students from Low-Income Families Pursuing Higher Education
tags: Higher Education, Scholarship, Students, PwD, Financial Assistance
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to meritorious students from economically weaker families pursuing higher education in Rajasthan.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Rajasthan, studying as a regular student in a recognized higher or technical institution in Rajasthan, have scored at least 60% in Class 12 Rajasthan Board examinations, belong to the top one lakh merit list, have annual family income not exceeding Rs.2.5 lakh, and should not be receiving any other scholarship. PwD students must have at least 40% disability.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Jan Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Domicile Certificate, Income Certificate, Educational Certificates, Disability Certificate (if applicable), Bank Account Details, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Rajasthan Higher Education Scholarship Portal using Jan Aadhaar registration and SSO ID. Complete profile creation, upload required documents, and submit the application online.
benefits: Rs.500 per month for up to 10 months (Rs.5,000 annually). Students with 40% or more disability receive Rs.1,000 per month for up to 10 months (Rs.10,000 annually). Benefits can continue for up to 5 years subject to continuation of studies.

scheme_name: Tripura Tribal Folk Arts and Cultural Financial Assistance Scheme, 2022
implementing_agency: Tribal Welfare Department, Government of Tripura
target_beneficiaries: Scheduled Tribe Individuals, NGOs, and Government Organizations
tags: Tribal Culture, Folk Arts, Festivals, NGOs, Cultural Preservation
brief_description: Provides financial assistance for promoting and preserving tribal folk arts, festivals, cultural programs, exhibitions, and traditional practices.
eligibility_criteria: Scheduled Tribe individuals, government organizations, and NGOs involved in preserving and promoting tribal culture, festivals, folk songs, dances, exhibitions, melas, and community cultural programs.
documents_required: Application Form, Organization Registration Documents (for NGOs), Identity Proof, Event Proposal, Budget Details, and other documents as prescribed by the Tribal Welfare Department.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form along with supporting documents to the Director of Tribal Welfare or the concerned District, Sub-Divisional, or Block authority.
benefits: Government events may receive assistance up to 15% above previous year's expenditure. NGOs may receive up to 10% of previous year's expenditure. New applicants may receive up to Rs.30,000 for organizing eligible cultural events.

implementing_agency: Telangana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
target_beneficiaries: Nominees, Dependents, and Legal Heirs of Deceased Construction Workers
tags: Natural Death, Construction Workers, Labour Welfare, Financial Assistance
brief_description: Provides financial relief to the family of a registered construction worker in case of natural death.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be the nominee, dependent, or legal heir of a registered construction worker whose death occurred due to natural causes.
documents_required: Photograph, BOCW Registration Card, Aadhaar/Ration Card, Renewal Proof, Death Certificate, Bank Passbook Copy, Advance Stamped Receipt, and supporting documents.
application_process: Download the application form from the Telangana BOCW portal and submit it with required documents to the concerned Labour Department officer.
benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs.1,00,000 to the nominee, dependent, or legal heir.

implementing_agency: Punjab Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
target_beneficiaries: Registered Construction Workers and Their Families
tags: Health Care, Construction Workers, Urban Health, Labour Welfare
brief_description: Provides primary healthcare services to registered construction workers and their families through Urban Health Kiosks in slum areas.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered construction worker under the Punjab BOCW Welfare Board or a family member of a registered worker.
documents_required: Worker Registration ID and any supporting documents required by the Board.
application_process: Approach the nearest Sewa Kendra, Labour Inspector, or Registering Officer to avail healthcare services under the scheme.
benefits: Access to primary healthcare facilities through Urban Health Kiosks operated by Punjab Health Society.

implementing_agency: Department of Finance, Government of Arunachal Pradesh
tags: Crop Loan, Kisan Credit Card, Zero Interest Loan, Agriculture
category: Agriculture, Rural Development, Banking & Financial Services
brief_description: Provides zero-interest crop loans to farmers through interest subvention on crop loans and Kisan Credit Card limits up to Rs.3 lakh.
eligibility_criteria: Farmers cultivating short-term crops in Arunachal Pradesh who avail crop loans or Kisan Credit Cards through eligible banks and maintain timely repayment records.
documents_required: Land Holding Certificate, Cultivation Certificate from Circle Officer, Aadhaar Card, Aadhaar-linked Bank Account Details, and bank-required loan documents.
application_process: Obtain land holding certification from local authorities, apply for crop loan or Kisan Credit Card through participating banks, and complete loan formalities with required documents.
benefits: 4% interest subvention on crop loans up to Rs.3 lakh, plus an additional 3% incentive for timely repayment, effectively enabling zero-interest crop loans for eligible farmers.

implementing_agency: Education Department, Government of Arunachal Pradesh
tags: Girl Education, Fixed Deposit, Financial Assistance, Secondary Education
brief_description: Provides a fixed deposit incentive to eligible girl students studying in Class VI to encourage continuation of education up to Class X.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be an unmarried girl studying in a school in Arunachal Pradesh, have passed Class V, possess an SBI savings account, and fulfill the withdrawal conditions of completing Class X and attaining 18 years of age.
documents_required: Class V Pass Certificate, Passport-size Photograph, Birth Certificate, SBI Bank Account Details, and Class X Pass Certificate for maturity withdrawal.
application_process: Apply through the school principal by submitting the prescribed application form and required documents. The school forwards eligible applications to the Directorate of Elementary Education.
benefits: A one-time fixed deposit of Rs.10,000 in the name of the eligible girl student. The maturity amount becomes available after completion of Class X and attaining 18 years of age.

implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Chhattisgarh
target_beneficiaries: Senior Citizens and Persons with Disabilities
tags: Pilgrimage, Senior Citizens, PwD, Travel Assistance
brief_description: Provides government-supported pilgrimage opportunities to eligible senior citizens and persons with disabilities to visit designated pilgrimage sites outside the state.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Chhattisgarh, either a senior citizen aged 60 years or above or a person with disability aged 18 years or above, not be an income taxpayer, and should not have previously availed the scheme.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Passport-size Photograph, Domicile Certificate, Age Proof, Residence Proof, Income Proof, BPL Card (if applicable), and supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form to the Gram Panchayat, District Panchayat, or Deputy Director of Panchayat and Social Welfare. Beneficiaries are selected through a lottery process.
benefits: Free pilgrimage opportunity to designated pilgrimage destinations outside Chhattisgarh, available once in a lifetime.

implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Chhattisgarh
tags: Marriage Assistance, Disability, Financial Assistance, Social Rehabilitation
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to promote social rehabilitation through marriage of persons with disabilities.
eligibility_criteria: At least one spouse must be a resident of Chhattisgarh and have a disability of 40% or more. The marriage must be legally valid, the groom must be between 21 and 45 years of age, the bride between 18 and 45 years, and neither spouse should be an income taxpayer.
documents_required: Marriage Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Domicile Certificate, Age Proof, Disability Certificate, Marriage Certificate, Affidavits, and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form to the Joint or Deputy Director of the Social Welfare Department within six months of marriage.
benefits: Rs.50,000 if either spouse has a disability and Rs.1,00,000 if both spouses have disabilities.

scheme_name: Reimbursement of Expenditure for Occupational Diseases
implementing_agency: Punjab Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
target_beneficiaries: Registered Construction Workers and Their Dependents
tags: Occupational Diseases, Medical Reimbursement, Construction Workers, Health Assistance
brief_description: Provides reimbursement of treatment expenses incurred for specified occupational and critical diseases.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered beneficiary of the Punjab Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board or a dependent family member. Treatment expenses must be verified by the Civil Surgeon and the applicant should have exhausted the health card benefit under the Bhagat Puran Singh Sehat Bima Yojana.
documents_required: Medical Certificate, Treatment Bills, Proof of Expenditure verified by Civil Surgeon, Registration Details, and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the nearest Sewa Kendra, Labour Inspector, or Registering Officer along with supporting medical documents.
benefits: Reimbursement of actual treatment expenses up to Rs.1,00,000, whichever is lower.

implementing_agency: Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
target_beneficiaries: Registered Construction Workers and Their Dependents
tags: Medical Assistance, Hospitalization, Construction Workers, Health Benefit
brief_description: Provides hospitalization assistance to registered construction workers and their dependents receiving treatment in recognized hospitals.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered construction worker under the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board or a dependent family member. Hospitalization must be for at least 48 continuous hours in a recognized hospital.
documents_required: Discharge Summary, Treatment Bills, Form 22A, Self Declaration, Employment Certificate/90 Days Work Certificate, Aadhaar Card, and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Karnataka BOCW portal by logging in, selecting the scheme, uploading documents, and submitting the application within six months from the date of hospitalization.
benefits: Rs.300 per day of hospitalization subject to actual expenditure, with a maximum assistance of Rs.20,000.

implementing_agency: Punjab Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
target_beneficiaries: Registered Construction Workers and Their Family Members
tags: Denture, Spectacles, Hearing Aid, Health Assistance, Construction Workers
brief_description: Provides financial assistance for dentures, spectacles, and hearing aids to registered construction workers and their eligible family members.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered construction worker under the Punjab Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board or an eligible family member. The worker should be regularly contributing to the Board.
documents_required: Government Medical Officer's Prescription, Attested Bills, Registration Details, and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the nearest Sewa Kendra, Labour Inspector, or Registering Officer along with the required medical documents.
benefits: spectacles - 800, denture - Rs.5,000, hearing_aid - Rs.6,000

implementing_agency: Punjab Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Maternity Benefit, Childbirth Assistance, Women Workers
brief_description: Provides financial assistance for childbirth-related expenses to registered construction workers for up to two living children.
eligibility_criteria: The worker must be registered with the Board for at least six months before childbirth and must have regularly deposited contributions. Benefits are available for a maximum of two living children.
documents_required: Child Photograph with Parents, Aadhaar Card of Child, Birth Certificate, and Death Certificate if applicable.
application_process: Apply through the nearest Sewa Kendra, Labour Inspector, or Registering Officer.
benefits: Female construction worker - Rs.21,000 per child (maximum 2 children) and Male construction worker - Rs.5,000 per child (maximum 2 children)

implementing_agency: Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Mother and Child Welfare, Nutrition, Preschool Education
brief_description: Provides financial support for nutrition and preschool education of children born to registered women construction workers.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered woman construction worker. The child must be below 3 years of age. Benefits are available for the first two children only.
documents_required: Board Identity Card, Affidavit, Bank Account Proof, Child Photograph, Birth Certificate, Discharge Summary, and Employment Certificate.
application_process: Apply online through the Karnataka BOCW portal within six months of delivery. Assistance can be claimed annually up to three years.
benefits: Rs.6,000 per year (Rs.500 per month) for up to 3 years for each eligible child.

implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Bihar
tags: Death Benefit, Family Assistance, BPL
brief_description: Provides one-time financial assistance to BPL families after the death of the primary earning member.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Bihar and a dependent family member of the deceased breadwinner. The deceased must have been between 18 and 60 years of age and the family must belong to the BPL category.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Death Certificate, Age Proof of Deceased, BPL Card, FIR (if applicable), Bank Details, and supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the application at the RTPS counter at the block office along with supporting documents.
benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs.20,000.

implementing_agency: Punjab Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
target_beneficiaries: Spouses of Deceased Registered Construction Workers
tags: Widow Pension, Family Pension, Construction Workers
brief_description: Provides monthly family pension to the surviving spouse of a deceased registered construction worker.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be the spouse of a deceased registered construction worker who had completed the required membership period with the Board.
documents_required: Death Certificate, FIR/Post-Mortem Report (if applicable), Bank Account Details, and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the nearest Sewa Kendra, Labour Inspector, or Registering Officer.
benefits: Rs.1,000 per month as family pension until remarriage or eligibility for another pension.

implementing_agency: Department of Social Welfare, Government of Uttarakhand
tags: Old Age Pension, Senior Citizens, Financial Assistance, BPL
brief_description: Provides monthly pension to senior citizens aged 60 years and above who belong to economically weaker sections of Uttarakhand.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be 60 years of age or older and belong to a BPL family or have a monthly income not exceeding Rs.4,000.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, BPL Ration Card, Income Certificate, Domicile Certificate, Birth Certificate, Residence Proof, Bank Account Details, and Caste Certificate (if applicable).
application_process: Apply online through the Uttarakhand Social Welfare Department portal by registering, filling the application form, uploading documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs.1,500.

scheme_name: Alpsankhyak Muslim Parityaqta/Talaqshuda Mahila Sahayata Yojana
implementing_agency: Directorate of Minority Welfare, Government of Bihar
tags: Minority Welfare, Muslim Women, Divorced Women, Financial Assistance
category: Women & Child Development, Social Welfare & Empowerment
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to divorced or abandoned Muslim women to strengthen their economic condition and promote self-reliance.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a resident of Bihar, belong to the Muslim minority community, be aged between 18 and 50 years, have annual family income below Rs.4,00,000, and be divorced, abandoned, or have a husband suffering from severe mental disability. Widows are not eligible.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Photograph, Age Proof, Community Certificate, Divorce/Abandonment Certificate, Income Certificate, Residence Proof, Medical Certificate (if applicable), and Bank Account Details.
application_process: Collect or download the application form, complete it with required documents, and submit it to the District Minority Welfare Office.
benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs.25,000.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Traditional Artisans (Panchavruthi Scheme)
implementing_agency: Karnataka Vishwakarma Communities Development Corporation Limited
tags: Artisans, Vishwakarma Community, Loan, Subsidy, Skill Development
brief_description: Provides financial support to traditional Vishwakarma artisans for purchasing modern tools and upgrading their trade skills.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must belong to the Vishwakarma community, be a permanent resident of Karnataka, aged between 18 and 55 years, engaged in a traditional Vishwakarma occupation, and should not have availed KVCDC loans previously.
documents_required: Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, Income Certificate, Bank Passbook, and Caste Certificate.
application_process: Obtain the application form from KVCDC or download it online, fill in details, attach documents, and submit it to the District Manager.
benefits: Loan amount - Up to Rs.80,000, Subsidy amount - Up to Rs.20,000, Total assistance - Up to Rs.1,00,000, Interest rate - 4% per annum

implementing_agency: Agriculture and Farmers Welfare Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Agriculture, Pulses, NPV, Pest Control, Subsidy
brief_description: Provides subsidy support for the distribution of Nuclear Polyhedrosis Virus (NPV) used in biological pest control for pulse crops.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a farmer cultivating pulse crops and possess proof of land ownership or cultivation.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Land Ownership Proof, Crop Cultivation Proof, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Bank Account Details, and supporting documents.
application_process: Collect the application form from the Agriculture Office, complete it, attach documents, and submit it to the appropriate agriculture authority.
benefits: 50% subsidy on input cost, limited to Rs.250 per hectare.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme for Self-Help Groups of Minorities
implementing_agency: Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation
tags: Minority Welfare, SHGs, Women Empowerment, Self Employment, Subsidy
brief_description: Provides subsidy support to minority women self-help groups engaged in self-employment activities.
eligibility_criteria: SHG members must belong to minority communities, be Karnataka residents, aged between 18 and 55 years, have family income below Rs.6,00,000, and the SHG must be registered and operational for at least six months.
documents_required: SHG Registration Certificate, Aadhaar Cards of Members, Income Certificates, Bank Account Details, Group Resolution, Photographs, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation portal by completing registration, verifying Aadhaar, uploading documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: 50% of the unit cost subject to a maximum subsidy of Rs.2,00,000.

implementing_agency: Department of Handlooms and Textiles, Government of Karnataka
tags: Handloom, Weaver, Skill Development, Subsidy, Textile
brief_description: A scheme to preserve and promote handloom weaving in Karnataka by supporting traditional and new weavers with financial assistance for handlooms and related equipment.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a handloom weaver with a Weaver Identity Card. Traditional weavers should be actively engaged in weaving. New entrants must have completed government-recognized handloom training. Membership in a Handloom Weaver Cooperative Society, Association, Organization, or Karnataka Handloom Development Corporation is required.
documents_required: Weaver Identity Card, Handloom Training Certificate (for new weavers), Loan Sanction Letter or Proof of Own Contribution, and any other required documents.
application_process: Apply through the district office of the Department of Handlooms and Textiles by submitting the prescribed application form along with supporting documents.
benefits: subsidy - 50% of the cost of handloom equipment / machinery, financing - Remaining 50% through own contribution or bank/financial institution loan

implementing_agency: Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation (KMDC)
tags: Education Loan, Minority Students, Professional Courses
category: Education & Learning, Banking, Financial Services and Insurance
brief_description: Provides educational loans to minority students pursuing professional and higher education courses.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must belong to a notified religious minority community in Karnataka, be a permanent resident of Karnataka, and have an annual family income not exceeding Rs.8,00,000.
documents_required: Photographs, Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Admission Letter, Marks Cards, Bonafide Certificate, Fee Structure, Bank Details, Self-Declaration Forms, and Indemnity Bond.
application_process: Apply online through the KMDC portal using Aadhaar-based authentication and upload the required documents.
benefits: Loan amount - Rs.50,000 to Rs.5,00,000 per year , Service charge - 2%, Repayment - Starts 6 months after completion of the course

implementing_agency: Agriculture and Farmers Welfare Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Agriculture, Soil Health Card, Soil Testing
brief_description: Provides Soil Health Cards to farmers based on laboratory analysis of soil samples to promote balanced nutrient management.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a farmer residing in Tamil Nadu who has submitted soil samples for testing and paid the prescribed testing fee.
documents_required: Identity Proof, Land Ownership Proof, Soil Sample Submission Receipt, Payment Receipt, and other supporting documents if required.
application_process: Submit soil samples through the Agriculture Department and apply at the nearest Agricultural Office.
benefits: Soil Health Card containing nutrient status and fertilizer recommendations.

implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Bihar
target_beneficiaries: Orphaned, Destitute, HIV/AIDS Affected and Leprosy Affected Children
tags: Children, Orphan, HIV/AIDS, Leprosy, Financial Assistance
category: Social Welfare & Empowerment, Women & Child Development
brief_description: Provides monthly financial assistance to vulnerable children including orphans, destitute children, and children affected by HIV/AIDS or leprosy.
eligibility_criteria: Children aged 0–18 years who are orphaned, destitute, affected by HIV/AIDS or leprosy, or whose parents suffer from these conditions. Income criteria apply for orphan and destitute categories.
documents_required: Birth Certificate, Death Certificate of parents (if applicable), Income Certificate, BPL Certificate, HIV/AIDS or Leprosy Medical Records, Bank Account Details, and Child Welfare Committee documents where applicable.
application_process: Application forms can be obtained from Anganwadi Centres, ICDS offices, or District Child Protection Units and submitted with supporting documents.
benefits: financial_assistance - Rs.1,000 per month

implementing_agency: Social Welfare Department, Government of Bihar
tags: Inter-caste Marriage, Marriage Incentive, Financial Assistance
brief_description: Promotes inter-caste marriages by providing financial assistance to eligible couples.
eligibility_criteria: Bride and groom must be residents of Bihar. The marriage must be an inter-caste marriage. Bride must be at least 18 years old and groom at least 21 years old at the time of marriage.
documents_required: Aadhaar Cards, Marriage Certificate, Photographs, Caste Certificates, Income Certificate, Age Proof, Residence Proof, Bank Account Details, and Identity Proofs.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form with supporting documents to the Assistant Director, District Social Security Office.
benefits: Grant amount - Rs.1,00,000 (one-time assistance)

implementing_agency: Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Bicycle, Construction Worker, Labour, Financial Assistance, Welfare
brief_description: The scheme provides financial assistance to registered construction workers for purchasing a bicycle.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a registered construction worker under the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board with at least one year of continuous active membership.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Photograph of Bicycle with Board Name Plate, GST Purchase Receipt of Bicycle, Residence Proof, Ration Card, Bank Account Details, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Self Declaration.
application_process: Register with the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board and apply online through the Board portal by submitting the required documents and purchase receipt.
benefits: Financial assistance of up to Rs.3,500 for purchasing a bicycle.

scheme_name: Distribution of Certified Seeds of Maize
implementing_agency: Agriculture and Farmers Welfare Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Agriculture, Farmer, Maize, Seed Distribution, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme provides subsidy support to maize-growing farmers for purchasing certified maize seeds.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a resident farmer of Tamil Nadu cultivating maize in the notified districts. Preference is given to small and marginal farmers, SC/ST farmers, and women farmers.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Land Ownership or Tenancy Proof, Recent Photograph, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Bank Account Details, Supporting Documents as required.
application_process: Obtain the application form from the nearest Agriculture Department office, submit the completed form along with required documents to the concerned agricultural officer.
benefits: Subsidy of 50% of the cost of certified maize seeds, subject to a maximum of Rs.1,200 per quintal.

scheme_name: Distribution of Gypsum for Oilseed Crops
implementing_agency: Agriculture and Farmers Welfare Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Agriculture, Farmer, Gypsum, Oilseeds, Subsidy
brief_description: The scheme provides subsidy assistance on gypsum and transportation costs to farmers producing foundation and certified class seeds of oilseed crops.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant should be a farmer producing and supplying foundation or certified class oilseed seeds to the Agriculture Department.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Land Ownership or Lease Documents, Seed Production and Supply Certificate, Bank Account Details, Supporting Documents as required.
application_process: Submit the application through the nearest Agriculture Department office along with the prescribed documents.
benefits: Subsidy of 50% of the cost of gypsum and transportation charges, subject to a maximum of Rs.750 per hectare.

implementing_agency: Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Accident Assistance, Construction Worker, Labour, Disability, Death Benefit
brief_description: The scheme provides accident compensation and financial assistance to registered construction workers and their families in case of death or disability.
