eligibility_criteria: Locomotor disability of more than 75% and not covered under any other rehabilitation assistance scheme.
documents_required: Disability certificate, Aadhaar card, residence proof, bank account details, and passport-size photograph.
application_process: Apply through the District Differently Abled Welfare Office.
benefits: Monthly maintenance allowance of Rs.2,000 directly credited to the beneficiary's bank account.

scheme_name: PRASHAD Scheme (Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual Augmentation Drive)
implementing_agency: Ministry of Tourism, Government of India
target_beneficiaries: State Governments, Local Communities, Tourism Stakeholders
tags: Tourism, Pilgrimage, Heritage, Infrastructure, Religious Tourism
brief_description: A central sector scheme for integrated development of pilgrimage destinations and enhancement of religious tourism infrastructure.
eligibility_criteria: No direct beneficiary eligibility. Implemented through states and government agencies.
documents_required: Not applicable for citizens.
application_process: No direct application process for citizens.
benefits: Development of pilgrimage infrastructure, improved tourism facilities, employment generation, cultural preservation, and economic growth in pilgrimage destinations.

scheme_name: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme
implementing_agency: Government of Tamil Nadu
target_beneficiaries: Differently Abled Persons and Their Families
tags: Health Insurance, Disability, Family Welfare, Healthcare
brief_description: Provides comprehensive health insurance coverage to differently abled persons and their family members in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility_criteria: At least one family member must be a differently abled person and a resident of Tamil Nadu.
documents_required: Disability certificate, Aadhaar card, residence proof, family details, and supporting documents as prescribed.
application_process: Apply through designated insurance enrollment centers or District Differently Abled Welfare Offices.
benefits: Comprehensive health insurance coverage for differently abled persons and their family members.

implementing_agency: Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (UKBOCW)
tags: Construction Worker, Solar Lantern, Solar Light, Labour, Welfare
brief_description: Provides solar lanterns or solar lights to registered construction workers as a one-time welfare benefit.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered construction worker in Uttarakhand with active membership for at least three months. Benefit can be availed only once in a lifetime and should not have been received under any other scheme.
documents_required: Labour card, Aadhaar card, self-declaration, mobile number, and other prescribed documents.
application_process: Apply through the nearest Shram Suvidha Kendra after registration with the Welfare Board.
benefits: One-time provision of a solar light or solar lantern (LED/CFL) to eligible construction workers.

implementing_agency: Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (UKBOCW)
tags: Construction Worker, Toolkit, Labour Welfare, Financial Assistance
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to registered construction workers for purchasing essential work toolkits.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered construction worker in Uttarakhand with active membership for at least three months. Benefit is available only once in a lifetime.
documents_required: Labour card, Aadhaar card, bank account details, self-declaration certificate, and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the nearest Shram Suvidha Kendra with the required documents.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.10,000 for purchase of construction toolkits and safety equipment.

scheme_name: Interest Subsidy Scheme for Establishment of 1-20 Milch Animal Farm (SC Category)
implementing_agency: Directorate of Animal Husbandry, Government of Gujarat
tags: Animal Husbandry, Dairy Farming, Milch Animals, Loan Subsidy, Scheduled Caste
brief_description: Provides interest subsidy on bank loans taken by Scheduled Caste farmers for establishing dairy farms with 1 to 20 milch animals.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a permanent resident of Gujarat, belong to the Scheduled Caste category, and have obtained a bank loan for establishing a dairy unit.
documents_required: Aadhaar card, caste certificate, land proof, bank loan documents, bank account details, consent form, and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply online through the I-Khedut portal and submit supporting documents to the designated office.
benefits: Interest subsidy up to 12% on eligible dairy farm loans for a period of five years through DBT.

implementing_agency: Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (UKBOCW)
tags: Maternity Benefit, Construction Worker, Labour Welfare, Women
brief_description: Provides maternity assistance to registered women construction workers for up to two childbirths.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered woman construction worker with active membership and apply within two months of delivery. Benefit is limited to the first two children.
documents_required: Labour card, Aadhaar card, ANC card, maternity certificate from medical officer, self-declaration, and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the nearest Shram Suvidha Kendra with required maternity-related documents.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.10,000 during maternity for eligible registered women construction workers.

scheme_name: Matrimonial Incentives for Persons with Disabilities
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Special Assistance, Government of Maharashtra
tags: Marriage Assistance, Disability, Social Welfare, Financial Incentive
brief_description: Provides financial incentives to encourage marriages between a person with disability and a non-disabled person.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a permanent resident of Maharashtra, have at least 40% disability, and be married to a non-disabled person.
documents_required: Disability certificate, marriage certificate, residence proof, Aadhaar card, bank details, photographs, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form to the District Social Welfare Office.
benefits: Financial assistance up to Rs.50,000, including savings certificate, cash assistance, and household utility support.

implementing_agency: Ministry of Panchayati Raj
tags: Property Rights, Rural Development, Drone Survey, Land Records, Village
brief_description: SVAMITVA Yojana provides legal property ownership records to rural household owners through drone-based mapping of inhabited village areas.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must own property in a rural inhabited (abadi) area covered under the scheme.
documents_required: Documents are collected and verified by local authorities during the survey and ownership verification process.
application_process: Properties are surveyed through drone mapping, ownership is verified through Gram Sabha and objection processes, and property cards are issued to eligible owners.
benefits: Provides legal property cards, reduces land disputes, enables access to loans using property as an asset, and improves rural planning through GIS-based records.

implementing_agency: Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Medical Assistance, Hospitalization, Construction Worker, Labour Welfare
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to registered construction workers hospitalized for five or more days due to illness or accidents.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered construction worker under the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board and hospitalized for at least five days.
documents_required: Hospital discharge certificate and medicine bills.
application_process: Apply online through the DBOCW Welfare Board portal after registration as a construction worker.
benefits: Rs.2,000 for the first five days of hospitalization and Rs.200 per day thereafter, subject to a maximum of Rs.10,000.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Miscarriage for Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Miscarriage Assistance, Women Workers, Labour Welfare, Health Support
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to registered women construction workers or wives of registered workers in case of miscarriage.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered woman construction worker or wife of a registered worker and have suffered a miscarriage.
documents_required: Medical miscarriage certificate or pregnancy treatment records.
application_process: Apply through the DBOCW Welfare Board portal after worker registration.
benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs.3,000.

scheme_name: Uniform Allowance to the Wards of Ex-Servicemen/Widows
implementing_agency: Department of Sainik Welfare, Puducherry
tags: Education Support, Uniform Allowance, Ex-Servicemen, Students
brief_description: Provides reimbursement towards school or college uniform expenses for wards of ex-servicemen and widows.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a ward of an ex-serviceman or widow studying in a recognized educational institution.
documents_required: ESM identity card, discharge certificate, study certificate, fee receipts, bank passbook, and supporting documents.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form to the Department of Sainik Welfare, Puducherry.
benefits: Up to Rs.2,000 per year for school students and Rs.2,500 per year for higher education students, subject to actual expenses.

scheme_name: Grant for Purchase of Work Related Tools
implementing_agency: Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Tool Grant, Construction Worker, Labour Welfare, Financial Assistance
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to registered construction workers for purchasing work-related tools.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be registered with the Welfare Board for at least three years, contribute regularly, and be below 55 years of age.
documents_required: Invoice or purchase bill for work-related tools.
application_process: Apply online through the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board portal.
benefits: Grant of Rs.5,000 once every five years for purchase of work-related tools.

implementing_agency: Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Funeral Assistance, Death Benefit, Labour Welfare, Construction Worker
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to the nominee or dependent of a registered construction worker in the event of the worker's death.
eligibility_criteria: The deceased worker must have been registered with the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. The applicant should be the nominee or dependent of the deceased worker.
documents_required: Proof of death and funeral-related documents as prescribed by the Board.
application_process: Apply through the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board portal after worker registration and submission of required documents.
benefits: Funeral assistance of Rs.10,000 to the nominee or dependent of the deceased worker.

scheme_name: Yatra Pass in DTC Non-AC Buses for Construction Workers
implementing_agency: Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Travel Assistance, Bus Pass, DTC, Labour Welfare, Construction Worker
brief_description: Provides financial support for purchasing DTC non-AC bus passes for registered construction workers.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a registered construction worker under the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
documents_required: Labour card and any other documents prescribed by the department.
application_process: Workers can apply through the DTC bus pass portal after registration with the Welfare Board.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.2,400 every three months towards DTC non-AC bus pass expenses.

scheme_name: Scholarship for National Child Award and Merit Award Recipients
implementing_agency: Directorate of School Education, Puducherry
tags: Scholarship, Merit Award, Student, Education, Talent Recognition
brief_description: Provides scholarships to children who have received National Child Awards or other recognized national and international merit awards.
eligibility_criteria: Students who are recipients of National Child Awards or other recognized national or international merit awards and are studying in Puducherry.
documents_required: Application form, award certificates, educational records, and other documents prescribed by the Directorate.
application_process: Submit the prescribed application form through the Head of Institution, who forwards it to the Directorate of School Education.
benefits: Scholarship of Rs.6,000 per year, disbursed through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

implementing_agency: Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Disability Pension, Labour Welfare, Construction Worker, Social Security
brief_description: Provides monthly pension to registered construction workers who suffer permanent disability due to workplace accidents or specified illnesses.
eligibility_criteria: Registered worker with at least three years of regular membership, 70%–100% permanent disability, and not receiving disability pension from another government source.
documents_required: Identity proof, disability certificate, residence proof, bank details, undertaking, work slip, and NOC from the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment.
application_process: Apply through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal after registration under the Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
benefits: Monthly disability pension of Rs.3,000.

implementing_agency: Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Marriage Assistance, Kanyadan, Labour Welfare, Daughter Marriage
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to registered construction workers for the marriage of their daughters.
eligibility_criteria: Worker must be registered with HBOCB, have at least one year of regular membership, completed 90 days of work in the preceding year, and be up to date with contributions.
documents_required: Worker ID, marriage registration certificate, self-declaration, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal after HBOCB registration and verification.
benefits: Rs.51,000 as Kanyadan assistance. Eligible beneficiaries may also receive an additional Rs.50,000 marriage assistance, making the total support up to Rs.1,01,000.

implementing_agency: Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Housing, Home Loan, Construction Worker, Labour Welfare
brief_description: Provides interest-free housing loan assistance to registered construction workers for house purchase or construction.
eligibility_criteria: Registered with HBOCB, minimum 5 years regular membership, maximum age 52 years, and benefit available only once in a lifetime.
documents_required: Worker ID, residence proof, land/property documents, approved building plan, bank details, ownership documents, NOC, and other supporting records.
application_process: Apply through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal after HBOCB registration and verification.
benefits: Interest-free housing loan up to Rs.2,00,000. Additional interest compensation support available for eligible PMAY beneficiaries.

scheme_name: Award of Scholarships to Outstanding NCC Cadets of Puducherry
implementing_agency: Directorate of School Education, Puducherry
tags: Scholarship, NCC, Cadet, Education
brief_description: Scholarship scheme recognizing outstanding NCC cadets from Puducherry.
eligibility_criteria: Indian citizen, Puducherry resident, minimum 75% parade attendance, required camp participation, and good disciplinary record.
documents_required: Nativity certificate, Aadhaar, NCC certificate, attendance records, marksheets, photograph, and character certificate.
application_process: Submit application through the NCC Company/Troop Commander, who forwards eligible applications for approval.
benefits: Rs.500 per month for Senior Division cadets and Rs.250 per month for Junior Division cadets.

implementing_agency: Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Women, Sewing Machine, Labour Welfare, Financial Assistance
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to registered women construction workers for purchasing a sewing machine.
eligibility_criteria: Woman construction worker registered with HBOCB, minimum one year regular membership, and active membership status.
documents_required: Worker ID, undertaking, purchase details, bank account information, and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal after HBOCB registration.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.4,500 for purchase of a sewing machine. Benefit available once in a lifetime.

nodal_ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
tags: Internship, Technology, Digital India, Skill Development
brief_description: Internship opportunity for students to gain practical experience under MeitY mentors in Digital India initiatives.
eligibility_criteria: Indian students pursuing eligible technical, legal, or management programs with at least 60% marks and currently in their final year.
documents_required: Academic records, identity proof, institute recommendation, and documents specified in the internship notification.
application_process: Register on the National Government Services Portal and apply online for the MeitY Digital India Internship Scheme.
benefits: Monthly stipend of Rs.10,000 and internship completion certificate.

implementing_agency: Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Funeral Assistance, Death Benefit, Labour Welfare
brief_description: Provides funeral assistance to the nominee or legal heir of a deceased registered construction worker.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be the nominee or legal heir of a deceased worker registered with HBOCB.
documents_required: Death certificate, nominee/legal heir proof, worker ID, identity proof, photograph, and undertaking.
application_process: Apply through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal with required documents.
benefits: Funeral assistance of Rs.15,000.

implementing_agency: Department for the Welfare of Differently Abled Persons, Government of Tamil Nadu
tags: Disability, Multimedia Training, Skill Development, Hearing Impaired, Locomotor Disability
brief_description: Provides one-month multimedia training to hearing-impaired and locomotor-disabled persons.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be hearing impaired or locomotor disabled and should have passed 8th standard.
documents_required: Disability Identity Card and 8th Pass Certificate.
application_process: Apply through the District Differently Abled Welfare Officer by submitting the prescribed application form and required documents.
benefits: One-month multimedia training programme conducted through designated training institutions.

implementing_agency: Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Travel Assistance, Labour Welfare, Home Town Visit, Construction Worker
brief_description: Provides reimbursement of travel expenses for registered construction workers and their families to visit their hometown once every year.
eligibility_criteria: Registered construction worker with minimum two years regular membership and active status under HBOCB.
documents_required: Worker ID card, original travel tickets, undertaking, and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal after HBOCB registration and verification.
benefits: Travel reimbursement for up to five family members based on Haryana Roadways fare or actual second-class railway fare.

scheme_name: West Bengal Migrant Workers Welfare Scheme: Death Assistance
implementing_agency: West Bengal Migrant Workers Welfare Board
tags: Migrant Worker, Death Benefit, Accident Benefit, Financial Assistance
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to nominees of registered migrant workers in case of natural or accidental death.
eligibility_criteria: Nominee of a registered migrant worker who died due to natural or accidental causes.
documents_required: Photo, Aadhaar card, bank account details, employment certificate, and other supporting documents.
application_process: Register and apply through the Karmasathi (Parijay Shramik) mobile application.
benefits: Rs.50,000 for natural death and Rs.2,00,000 for accidental death.

implementing_agency: Chhattisgarh Unorganized Workers State Social Security Board
tags: E-Rickshaw, Financial Assistance, Employment, Unorganized Workers
brief_description: Provides financial support to eligible unorganized workers for purchasing e-rickshaws.
eligibility_criteria: Registered auto/cycle rickshaw driver aged 18–50 years, registered with the Board for at least 90 days, holding valid commercial driving licence and RTO registration.
documents_required: Aadhaar card, residence certificate, ration card, bank details, and e-rickshaw purchase receipt.
application_process: Apply online through the Chhattisgarh Labour Department portal after worker registration.
benefits: Government grant of Rs.30,000, beneficiary contribution of Rs.10,000, and remaining amount through bank loan.

scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Mahila Nirman Shramik Samaan Yojana (HBOCB)
implementing_agency: Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
tags: Women Welfare, Labour Welfare, Construction Worker, Financial Assistance
brief_description: Provides annual financial assistance to registered women construction workers for purchasing essential household and personal-use items.
eligibility_criteria: Registered female construction worker with minimum one year regular membership under HBOCB.
documents_required: Worker ID card, undertaking with purchase details, and supporting documents.
application_process: Apply through the Antyodaya SARAL Portal after HBOCB registration and membership renewal.
benefits: Annual financial assistance of Rs.5,100 for purchase of sarees, suits, slippers, raincoats, umbrellas, sanitary napkins, kitchen utensils, and similar essentials.

scheme_name: Banglashree for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises: Subsidy for Water Conservation/Environment Compliance (WCS)
implementing_agency: Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises and Textiles, Government of West Bengal
tags: Entrepreneurship, Environment Compliance, Incentives, MSME, Reimbursement, Subsidy, Water Conservation, WCS
brief_description: Provides reimbursement of expenditure incurred towards Effluent Water Treatment Plants and pollution control devices for eligible MSMEs in West Bengal.
detailed_description: The Subsidy for Water Conservation/Environment Compliance (WCS) is a sub-scheme under Banglashree for MSMEs launched by the Department of MSME and Textiles, Government of West Bengal. The scheme aims to encourage sustainable industrial practices by reimbursing expenses incurred on Effluent Water Treatment Plants for wastewater recycling and other pollution control devices.
eligibility_criteria: The applicant must be a Micro, Small or Medium Enterprise in the manufacturing sector. The enterprise should have commenced production on or after 1 April 2019 and should operate in the private, cooperative, joint, state-owned, or industrial self-help group sector. The Date of Commencement of Commercial Production (DOP) must be between 1 April 2020 and 31 March 2025.
application_process: Register on the Shilpasathi Portal. Create a user account, generate a CAF (Common Application Form), complete the MSME Incentive application, upload required documents, verify details, and submit the application online. The first application must be submitted within 12 months from the Date of Commencement of Production or notification date of the scheme, whichever is later.
benefits: Eligible Micro and Small Enterprises receive reimbursement of 50% of expenditure incurred towards Effluent Water Treatment Plants and pollution control devices, subject to a maximum limit of Rs.2,00,000.

scheme_name: Disability (Due to Accident or Other Reason) Scheme - Haryana Labour Welfare Board
implementing_agency: Haryana Labour Welfare Board
tags: Accident, Disability, Financial Assistance, Labour, PwD, Worker
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to registered workers in Haryana who become disabled due to an accident or other reasons, based on the percentage of disability.
eligibility_criteria: The applicant must be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana and registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board. The worker's monthly salary should not exceed Rs.25,000. Workers who become disabled due to an accident or other reasons, inside or outside the workplace, are eligible.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, passport-size photograph, Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID), bank account details, residence proof, ration card, caste certificate (if applicable), age proof, disability certificate, worker ID card or office ID proof, employer certificate, previous month's salary slip, attested disability certificate, medical certificate, self-undertaking/affidavit, ESI card, and any other documents as required.
application_process: Register on the Haryana Labour Welfare Board portal using Family ID or Aadhaar. After registration, log in to the Welfare Board Beneficiary Portal, select the scheme, fill in the application form, upload required documents, and submit. Alternatively, apply through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by registering, logging in, selecting the scheme, completing verification through Family ID and OTP, uploading documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.1,00,000 for minor disability (up to 50% disability) and Rs.1,50,000 for major disability (above 50% disability).

scheme_name: Banglashree for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises: Subsidy for Patent Registration (PRS)
implementing_agency: Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises and Textiles, Government of West Bengal
tags: Entrepreneurship, Incentives, MSME, Patent Registration, PRS, Reimbursement, Subsidy
brief_description: Provides reimbursement of expenses incurred for obtaining patent registration by eligible MSMEs in West Bengal.
eligibility_criteria: The applicant must be a Micro, Small or Medium Enterprise in the manufacturing sector. The enterprise should have commenced production on or after 1 April 2019 and operate in the private, cooperative, joint, state-owned, or industrial self-help group sector. The Date of Commencement of Commercial Production (DOP) must be between 1 April 2020 and 31 March 2025.
documents_required: Memorandum and Articles of Association/Partnership documents, project report, loan sanction and disbursement letters, audited balance sheets, plant and machinery details, land/building documents, mutation and conversion certificates, Consent to Operate certificate from WBPCB, SGST documents, trade licence, electricity bill, Udyam/Udyog Aadhaar registration, SC/ST certificate (if applicable), statutory licences, self-declaration, proof of fees paid for patent registration, and Patent Registration Certificate.
application_process: Register on the Shilpasathi Portal. Create a user account, generate a CAF (Common Application Form), complete the MSME Incentive application, upload required documents, verify details, and submit the application online. The first application must be submitted within 12 months from the Date of Commencement of Production or notification date of the scheme, whichever is later.
benefits: Eligible Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises receive reimbursement of 50% of expenditure incurred for obtaining Patent Registration, subject to a maximum limit of Rs.5,00,000.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance for Encouraging Worker's Children's Participation in Cultural Activities - Haryana Labour Welfare Board
implementing_agency: Haryana Labour Welfare Board
tags: Artist, Cultural Competitions, Dance, Financial Assistance, Music, Participation in Cultural Activities, Worker
brief_description: Provides financial assistance to children of registered workers in Haryana for participating in and winning cultural competitions at district, division, state, national, and international levels.
eligibility_criteria: The applicant must be employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana and registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board. The worker's monthly salary should not exceed Rs.25,000. Benefits are available for children of registered workers who participate in or secure positions in cultural competitions. There is no minimum service requirement and no limit on the number of eligible children. Applications must be submitted within one year from the date of issuance of the participation or achievement certificate.
documents_required: Worker ID card issued by the organization, employer certificate, previous month's salary slip, cultural participation/achievement certificate attested by the District Cultural Officer, self-undertaking, ration card, ESI card, proof that the child is dependent on the worker, Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID), bank account details, Aadhaar card, passport-size photograph, residence proof, caste certificate (if applicable), age proof, disability certificate (if applicable), and any other required documents.
application_process: Register on the Haryana Labour Welfare Board portal using Family ID or Aadhaar. After registration, log in to the Welfare Board Beneficiary Portal, select the scheme, complete the application form, upload the required documents, and submit. Alternatively, apply through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal by registering, logging in, selecting the scheme, verifying through Family ID and OTP, uploading documents, and submitting the application.
benefits: Financial assistance is provided for participation and achievements in cultural competitions such as dance and music. Assistance ranges from Rs.2,000 to Rs.21,000 depending on the competition level (district, division, state, national, or international), whether participation is individual or group-based, and whether the child secures a prize position.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance to Widow/Dependent in Case of Death of a Worker - Haryana Labour Welfare Board
implementing_agency: Haryana Labour Welfare Board
tags: Death, Dependent, Financial Assistance, Widow, Worker
brief_description: Provides financial assistance of Rs.2,00,000 to the widow or dependent of a registered worker employed in industrial or commercial establishments in Haryana in case of the worker's death.
eligibility_criteria: The applicant must be the widow or dependent of a deceased worker. The deceased worker should have been employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana and registered with the Haryana Labour Welfare Board. There is no salary limit or minimum service period requirement. The application must be submitted within two years from the date of death.
documents_required: Worker ID proof issued by the organization, Smart Card/Office ID, employer certificate, previous month's salary slip, death certificate, affidavit declaring dependency on the deceased worker and non-availment of similar benefits, self-undertaking, ration card, ESI card, Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID), bank account details, and any other documents required by the department.
application_process: Visit the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal and register as a new user if not already registered. Verify mobile number and email through OTP. Log in to the portal, select the relevant scheme, enter the Family ID (Parivar Pehchan Patra), verify through OTP, fill in the application form, upload the required documents, and submit the application.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.2,00,000 is provided to the widow or dependent of the deceased worker.

implementing_agency: Directorate of Dairy Development, Government of Bihar
tags: Animal, Disease, Farmers, Insurance
category: Agriculture, Rural & Environment, Banking, Financial Services and Insurance
brief_description: Pashu Bima Yojana provides insurance coverage for milch cattle to protect farmers against financial losses caused by cattle death due to diseases or other reasons and to strengthen the rural economy.
eligibility_criteria: The applicant should be a farmer owning milch cattle, a member of a Milk Producer Cooperative Society, and the cattle should be healthy at the time of insurance.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Milk Producer Society Membership Receipt, and Passport-size Photograph of the proposer.
application_process: Register on the Bihar Dairy MIS portal by providing personal details, Aadhaar number, district, mobile number, and OTP verification. After registration, log in using the registered mobile number and password received via SMS. Complete the application form, enter proposer details, upload the Milk Producer Society membership receipt and photograph, and submit the application online.
benefits: The maximum insured value per milch cattle is Rs.60,000. The annual insurance premium is Rs.2,100 (3.5% of the insured value). The State Government provides a subsidy of Rs.1,575 (75% of the premium), while the farmer pays Rs.525 (25%). Insurance coverage is provided for one year, and ear tags are fitted to insured cattle by the insurance company.

scheme_name: West Bengal Handloom and Khadi Weavers Financial Benefit Scheme 2024: Support for One Time Settlement (OTS) of NPA Accounts of PWCS
target_beneficiaries: Registered Societies, Business Entity, Individual, Joint Liability Groups (JLGs), NGO, Self Help Groups (SHGs)
tags: Handloom, Khadi Weavers, NPA, OTS, PWCS
brief_description: This sub-scheme under the West Bengal Handloom and Khadi Weavers Financial Benefit Scheme 2024 provides financial support for One Time Settlement (OTS) of NPA accounts of Primary Weavers Cooperative Societies (PWCS), helping them become debt-free.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible beneficiaries include Handloom Weavers (Entrepreneur Weavers, Master Weavers, Individual Weavers, Allied Workers associated with Handloom Weaving, Cooperative Weavers, and Weavers associated with Handloom Clusters) and Khadi Weavers.
documents_required: Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Proof of being a Handloom or Khadi Weaver, and any other documents as required.
application_process: Eligible PWCS, Khadi Societies, or Individual Weavers may apply either online or offline in the prescribed format notified by the Department of MSME and Textiles, Government of West Bengal, and submit the application to the Director of Textiles.
benefits: Provides 100% of the overdue principal amount and 25% of the overdue interest on NPA accounts of viable PWCS as on 31 March 2024. The remaining 75% of overdue interest and any penal interest will be written off by the West Bengal State Cooperative Bank Ltd. or the concerned District Central Cooperative Bank Ltd.

scheme_name: West Bengal Handloom and Khadi Weavers Financial Benefit Scheme 2024: Assistance to Individual Handloom Weaver
implementing_agency: Department of MSME and Textiles, Government of West Bengal
tags: Financial Benefit, Handloom, Handloom Weaver
brief_description: This sub-scheme under the West Bengal Handloom and Khadi Weavers Financial Benefit Scheme 2024 provides one-time financial assistance to individual handloom weavers who are not members of any Primary Weavers Cooperative Society.
eligibility_criteria: Individual handloom weavers who are not covered under any Primary Weavers Cooperative Society (PWCS) are eligible for assistance under the scheme.
documents_required: Photograph, Aadhaar Card, Proof of being a Handloom or Khadi Weaver, and any other documents as required.
application_process: Eligible individual weavers may apply either online or offline in the prescribed format notified by the Department of MSME and Textiles, Government of West Bengal, and submit the application to the Director of Textiles.
benefits: One-time financial assistance of Rs.5,000 per weaver for repair or renovation of the work shed and/or repair or modification of looms and accessories. An additional Rs.10,000 is provided as raw material assistance for the production of handloom products, subject to procurement of yarn through approved channels.

scheme_name: Scheme of Death Benefit for Weavers & Artisans of West Bengal
implementing_agency: Department of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises and Textiles, Government of West Bengal
tags: Artisans, Death, Social Welfare, Weavers
brief_description: This scheme provides financial assistance to the family of a deceased weaver or artisan in West Bengal.
eligibility_criteria: The deceased artisan or weaver should have been between 18 and 60 years of age at the time of death. The death should have occurred on or after 7 March 2024. The claimant should not have availed benefits under any other State Government death benefit scheme.
documents_required: Death Certificate of the deceased artisan/weaver, Age Proof, Identity Proof of claimant, Proof of being a Weaver/Artisan, Bank Account Details of claimant, Certificate from the local BDO regarding eligibility of claim, and any other documents as required.
application_process: The eligible claimant should collect and submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the local BDO or SDO office of the concerned Block or Municipal Corporation area. The application must be submitted within three months from the date of death of the artisan or weaver. The claimant must also obtain and submit an eligibility certificate from the local BDO.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.2,00,000 is provided to the family of each deceased weaver or artisan.

implementing_agency: Haryana Labour Welfare Board
tags: Death, Financial Assistance, Labour, Worker
brief_description: Under this scheme, financial assistance of Rs.5,00,000 is provided to the widow or dependents of workers employed in industrial and commercial establishments in Haryana in the event of the worker's death due to workplace-related incidents.
eligibility_criteria: The applicant should be the widow or dependent of a deceased worker employed in an industrial or commercial establishment in Haryana. There is no minimum service period or salary requirement. Applications must be submitted within two years from the date of death. The scheme covers deaths caused by workplace incidents, accidents within the establishment, natural disasters, heart attacks, snake bites, fire, building collapse, or death occurring while being taken to the hospital for treatment. Dependents of both registered and unregistered workers are eligible in specified cases.
documents_required: Worker ID Proof/Smart Card/Office ID Card, Employee Certificate, Employer Certificate, Previous Month Salary Slip, Death Certificate, Disability Certificate (if applicable), Medical Certificate, FIR/DDR/Post-Mortem Report, ESI Card, Affidavit declaring dependency and non-availment of similar benefits, Self-Undertaking, Ration Card, Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID), Bank Account Details, and any other documents as required.
application_process: Apply through the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal. Register as a new user if not already registered by providing basic details and completing OTP verification. Log in to the portal, search for the scheme, complete Family ID verification, fill in the application form, upload the required documents, preview the details, and submit the application online.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs.5,00,000 is provided to the widow or dependents of a worker who dies due to eligible workplace-related incidents.
